text.KJV: And gladness is taken away, and joy out of the plentiful field; and in the vineyards there shall be no singing, neither shall there be shouting: the treaders shall tread out no wine in their presses; I have made their vintage shouting to cease.
text.NABRE: From the orchards are taken away joy and gladness, In the vineyards there is no singing, no shout of joy; In the wine presses no one treads grapes, the vintage shout is stilled.
text.DRB: And gladness and joy shall be taken away from Carmelus, and it shall not rejoice nor make jubilation in vineyards. He shall not tread wine in the press that was wont to tread: the voice of the treaders I have taken away.

text.NRSV: Therefore my heart throbs like a harp for Moab, and my very self for Kir-heres.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, mine inward parts—for Moab, like a lyre, shall make a plaintive sound,—Yea what is within me, for Kir-heres.
text.KJV: Wherefore my bowels shall sound like an harp for Moab, and mine inward parts for Kir–haresh.
text.NABRE: Therefore for Moab my heart moans like a lyre, my inmost being for Kir-hareseth.
text.DRB: For this my belly shall sound as an harp to Moab, & my bowels to the wall of baked brick.

text.NRSV: When Moab presents himself, when he wearies himself upon the high place, when he comes to his sanctuary to pray, he will not prevail.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be—When it is seen that Moab hath laboured in vain on the high place, He shall enter into his holy place to pray, and shall not prevail,
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, when it is seen that Moab is weary on the high place, that he shall come to his sanctuary to pray; but he shall not prevail.
text.NABRE: When Moab wears himself out on the high places, and enters his sanctuary to pray, it shall avail him nothing.
text.DRB: And it shall be: when it shall appear that Moab hath laboured for his excelses, he shall go into his sanctuaries to pray, & shall not prevail.

text.NRSV: This was the word that the Lord spoke concerning Moab in the past.
text.Rotherham: This, is the word which Yahweh spake concerning Moab in, time past;
text.KJV: This is the word that the Lord hath spoken concerning Moab since that time.
text.NABRE: That is the word the Lord spoke against Moab in times past.
text.DRB: This is the word, that our Lord spake to Moab from that time:

text.NRSV: But now the Lord says, In three years, like the years of a hired worker, the glory of Moab will be brought into contempt, in spite of all its great multitude; and those who survive will be very few and feeble.
text.Rotherham: But now, hath Yahweh spoken saying, In three years,—as the years of a hireling, shall the glory of Moab be diminished, with all the great multitude,—even a very small remnant, of no account.
text.KJV: But now the Lord hath spoken, saying, Within three years, as the years of an hireling, and the glory of Moab shall be contemned, with all that great multitude; and the remnant shall be very small and feeble.
text.NABRE: But now the Lord speaks: In three years, like the years of a hired laborer, the glory of Moab shall be empty despite all its great multitude; and the remnant shall be very small and weak.
text.DRB: and now our Lord hath spoken saying: In three years, as the years of an hired servant, the glory of Moab shall be taken away upon all the multitude of people, and it shall be left little & small, not much.

text.NRSV: An oracle concerning Damascus. See, Damascus will cease to be a city, and will become a heap of ruins.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Damascus,—Lo! Damascus, is to be removed from being a city, And shall become a heap of ruins:
text.KJV: The burden of Damascus. Behold, Damascus is taken away from being a city, and it shall be a ruinous heap.
text.NABRE: Chapter 17 Damascus Oracle on Damascus: See, Damascus shall cease to be a city and become a pile of ruins;
text.DRB: The burden of Damascus. Behold Damascus shall cease to be a city, and shall be as an heap of stones in ruin.

text.NRSV: Her towns will be deserted forever; they will be places for flocks, which will lie down, and no one will make them afraid.
text.Rotherham: Forsaken, are the desolate cities,—For flocks, shall they serve, Which shall lie down and have none to make them afraid.
text.KJV: The cities of Aroer are forsaken: they shall be for flocks, which shall lie down, and none shall make them afraid.
text.NABRE: Her cities shall be forever abandoned, for flocks to lie in undisturbed.
text.DRB: The forsaken cities of Aroer shall be for flocks, and they shall rest there, and there shall be none to terrify them.

text.NRSV: The fortress will disappear from Ephraim, and the kingdom from Damascus; and the remnant of Aram will be like the glory of the children of Israel, says the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the fortress cease from Ephraim, And the kingdom from Damascus and the remnant of Syria,—Like the glory of the sons of Israel, shall they be,—Declareth Yahweh of hosts.
text.KJV: The fortress also shall cease from Ephraim, and the kingdom from Damascus, and the remnant of Syria: they shall be as the glory of the children of Israel, saith the Lord of hosts.
text.NABRE: The fortress shall vanish from Ephraim and dominion from Damascus; The remnant of Aram shall become like the glory of the Israelites— oracle of the Lord of hosts.
text.DRB: And aid shall cease from Ephraim, and the kingdom from Damascus: and the remnant of Syria shall be as the glory of the children of Israel: saith the Lord of hosts.

text.NRSV: On that day the glory of Jacob will be brought low, and the fat of his flesh will grow lean.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day, That the glory of Jacob, shall be diminished, And, the fatness of his flesh, shall be wasted;
text.KJV: And in that day it shall come to pass, that the glory of Jacob shall be made thin, and the fatness of his flesh shall wax lean.
text.NABRE: On that day The glory of Jacob shall fade, and his full body shall grow thin.
text.DRB: And it shall be in that day: the glory of Jacob shall be diminished, and the fatness of his flesh shall become lean.

text.NRSV: And it shall be as when the reaper gathers standing grain and his arm harvests the ears, and as when one gleans the ears of grain in the valley of Rephaim.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass—That, as the harvestman gathereth standing corn, And with his arm—the ears, he reapeth, Yea it shall come to pass—That, so, shall he be who gleaneth ears, in the vale of Rephaim;
text.KJV: And it shall be as when the harvestman gathereth the corn, and reapeth the ears with his arm; and it shall be as he that gathereth ears in the valley of Rephaim.
text.NABRE: Like the reaper’s mere armful of stalks, when he gathers the standing grain; Or as when one gleans the ears in the Valley of Rephaim.
text.DRB: And it shall be as one gathering in harvest that which remaineth, and his arm shall gather the ears of corn: and it shall be as he that seeketh ears in the vale of Raphaim.

text.NRSV: Gleanings will be left in it, as when an olive tree is beaten— two or three berries in the top of the highest bough, four or five on the branches of a fruit tree, says the Lord God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Yet shall there be left therein, a gleaning. As in the beating of an olive-tree,—Two-three berries in the head of the tree-top,—Four—five, among her fruitful boughs, Declareth Yahweh God of Israel.
text.KJV: Yet gleaning grapes shall be left in it, as the shaking of an olive tree, two or three berries in the top of the uppermost bough, four or five in the outmost fruitful branches thereof, saith the Lord God of Israel.
text.NABRE: Only gleanings shall be left in it, as when an olive tree has been beaten— Two or three olives at the very top, four or five on its most fruitful branches— oracle of the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.DRB: And the fruit thereof shall be left, as it were a cluster of grapes in it, and as the shaking of the olive tree, of two or three olives in the top of a bough, or four or five in the tops thereof, saith our Lord the God of Israel.

text.NRSV: On that day people will regard their Maker, and their eyes will look to the Holy One of Israel;
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall the son of earth look to him that made him,—And his eyes unto the Holy One of Israel, be turned;
text.KJV: At that day shall a man look to his Maker, and his eyes shall have respect to the Holy One of Israel.
text.NABRE: On that day people shall turn to their maker, their eyes shall look to the Holy One of Israel.
text.DRB: In that day man shall incline himself to his maker, and his eyes shall look to the holy one of Israel.

text.NRSV: they will not regard the altars, the work of their hands, and they will not look to what their own fingers have made, either the sacred poles or the incense altars.
text.Rotherham: And he shall not look unto the altars the work of his own hands,—Nor to what his own fingers have made, shall his eye be turned, Whether Sacred Stems or Sun-pillars.
text.KJV: And he shall not look to the altars, the work of his hands, neither shall respect that which his fingers have made, either the groves, or the images.
text.NABRE: They shall not turn to the altars, the work of their hands, nor shall they look to what their fingers have made: the asherahs or the incense stands.
text.DRB: And he shall not incline to the altars, which his hands made: and the things that his fingers wrought he shall not regard, as groves and temples.

text.NRSV: On that day their strong cities will be like the deserted places of the Hivites and the Amorites, which they deserted because of the children of Israel, and there will be desolation.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall his fortified cities become Like a neglected bough and a topmost branch, Which they neglected because of the sons of Israel,—So shall there be desolation.
text.KJV: In that day shall his strong cities be as a forsaken bough, and an uppermost branch, which they left because of the children of Israel: and there shall be desolation.
text.NABRE: On that day his strong cities shall be like those abandoned by the Hivites and Amorites When faced with the Israelites; and there shall be desolation.
text.DRB: In that day the cities of his strength shall be left, as the ploughs and the corn that were left before the face of the children of Israel, and thou shalt be desolate.

text.NRSV: For you have forgotten the God of your salvation, and have not remembered the Rock of your refuge; therefore, though you plant pleasant plants and set out slips of an alien god,
text.Rotherham: Because thou didst forget the God of thy salvation, And thy Rock of refuge, thou didst not remember, For this cause, shalt thou plant very pleasant plants, And, the slip of a stranger, shalt thou set:
text.KJV: Because thou hast forgotten the God of thy salvation, and hast not been mindful of the rock of thy strength, therefore shalt thou plant pleasant plants, and shalt set it with strange slips:
text.NABRE: Truly, you have forgotten the God who saves you, the Rock, your refuge, you have not remembered. Therefore, though you plant plants for the Pleasant One, and set out cuttings for a foreign one,
text.DRB: Because thou hast forgotten God thy saviour, and hast not remembered thy strong helper: therefore shalt thou plant a faithful plant, & shalt sow a strange seed.

text.NRSV: though you make them grow on the day that you plant them, and make them blossom in the morning that you sow; yet the harvest will flee away in a day of grief and incurable pain.
text.Rotherham: In the day when thou plantest, fence thou in, And in the morning, cause thou, thy slip, to blossom,—A harvest will have waved in the day of destiny, and mortal pain.
text.KJV: In the day shalt thou make thy plant to grow, and in the morning shalt thou make thy seed to flourish: but the harvest shall be a heap in the day of grief and of desperate sorrow.
text.NABRE: Though you make them grow the day you plant them and make them blossom the morning you set them out, The harvest shall disappear on a day of sickness and incurable pain.
text.DRB: In the day of thy planting shall be the wild grape, & in the morning thy seed shall flourish: the harvest is taken away in the day of inheritance, and he shall be vehemently sorry.

text.NRSV: Ah, the thunder of many peoples, they thunder like the thundering of the sea! Ah, the roar of nations, they roar like the roaring of mighty waters!
text.Rotherham: Alas! the booming of many peoples, Like the booming of the seas, shall they boom,—And the rushing of nations! Like the rushing of mighty waters, shall they rush.
text.KJV: Woe to the multitude of many people, which make a noise like the noise of the seas; and to the rushing of nations, that make a rushing like the rushing of mighty waters!
text.NABRE: Ah! the roaring of many peoples— a roar like the roar of the seas! The thundering of nations— thunder like the thundering of mighty waters!
text.DRB: Woe to the multitude of many peoples, being as the multitude of the sounding sea: & the tumult of multitudes, as the sound of many waters.

text.NRSV: The nations roar like the roaring of many waters, but he will rebuke them, and they will flee far away, chased like chaff on the mountains before the wind and whirling dust before the storm.
text.Rotherham: Though nations like the rushing of many waters, shall rush, Yet shall one rebuke him, And he shall flee far away,—And be chased As the chaff of the mountains before a wind, And as whirling stubble before a storm!
text.KJV: The nations shall rush like the rushing of many waters: but God shall rebuke them, and they shall flee far off, and shall be chased as the chaff of the mountains before the wind, and like a rolling thing before the whirlwind.
text.NABRE: But God shall rebuke them, and they shall flee far away, Driven like chaff on the mountains before a wind, like tumbleweed before a storm.
text.DRB: Peoples shall sound as the sound of waters overflowing, and he shall rebuke him, and shall fly far off: and he shall be violently taken away as the dust of the mountains at the face of the wind, and as a whirlwind before a tempest.

text.NRSV: At evening time, lo, terror! Before morning, they are no more. This is the fate of those who despoil us, and the lot of those who plunder us.
text.Rotherham: At eventide, lo! terror, Before morning, he is not! This, is the portion of them who plunder us, And the lot of them who make of us a prey.
text.KJV: And behold at eveningtide trouble; and before the morning he is not. This is the portion of them that spoil us, and the lot of them that rob us.
text.NABRE: At evening, there is terror, but before morning, they are gone! Such is the portion of those who despoil us, the lot of those who plunder us.
text.DRB: In the eventide, and behold trouble: in the morning, and he shall not be. This is the portion of them, that have wasted us, & the lot of them that spoiled us.

text.NRSV: Ah, land of whirring wings beyond the rivers of Ethiopia,
text.Rotherham: Ho! thou land of the buzzing of wings,—Which is beyond the rivers of Ethiopia:
text.KJV: Woe to the land shadowing with wings, which is beyond the rivers of Ethiopia:
text.NABRE: Chapter 18 Ethiopia Ah! Land of buzzing insects, beyond the rivers of Ethiopia,
text.DRB: Woe to the land the cymbal of wings, which is beyond the rivers of Æthiopia,

text.NRSV: sending ambassadors by the Nile in vessels of papyrus on the waters! Go, you swift messengers, to a nation tall and smooth, to a people feared near and far, a nation mighty and conquering, whose land the rivers divide.
text.Rotherham: That sendeth by the sea, ambassadors Even in vessels of paper-reed, upon the face of the waters,—Go ye swift messengers Unto a nation drawn out and polished, Unto a people terrible from their beginning and onwards,—A nation most mighty and subduing, Whose land rivers have cut through.
text.KJV: That sendeth ambassadors by the sea, even in vessels of bulrushes upon the waters, saying, Go, ye swift messengers, to a nation scattered and peeled, to a people terrible from their beginning hitherto; a nation meted out and trodden down, whose land the rivers have spoiled!
text.NABRE: Sending ambassadors by sea, in papyrus boats on the waters! Go, swift messengers, to a nation tall and bronzed, To a people dreaded near and far, a nation strong and conquering, whose land is washed by rivers.
text.DRB: which sendeth legates into the sea, and in the vessels of bulrushes upon the waters. Go ye swift angels to a nation shaken a sunder, and torn in pieces: to a terrible people, after which there is none other: to a nation expecting and trodden under foot, whose land the floods have spoiled:

text.NRSV: All you inhabitants of the world, you who live on the earth, when a signal is raised on the mountains, look! When a trumpet is blown, listen!
text.Rotherham: All ye inhabitants of the world And ye, that dwell in the earth,—When there is lifted up an ensign on the mountains, Ye shall look, And when there is blown a horn, Ye shall hearken.
text.KJV: All ye inhabitants of the world, and dwellers on the earth, see ye, when he lifteth up an ensign on the mountains; and when he bloweth a trumpet, hear ye.
text.NABRE: All you who inhabit the world, who dwell on earth, When the signal is raised on the mountain, look! When the trumpet blows, listen!
text.DRB: All ye inhabitants of the world, which abide in the earth, where the sign shall be lifted up in the mountains, you shall see, and shall hear the sound of the trumpet:

text.NRSV: For thus the Lord said to me: I will quietly look from my dwelling like clear heat in sunshine, like a cloud of dew in the heat of harvest.
text.Rotherham: For, thus, said Yahweh unto me,—I must be quiet I must look on in my fixed place of abode,—Like a bright heat on the light, Like a cloud of dew in the heat of harvest.
text.KJV: For so the Lord said unto me, I will take my rest, and I will consider in my dwelling place like a clear heat upon herbs, and like a cloud of dew in the heat of harvest.
text.NABRE: For thus says the Lord to me: I will be quiet, looking on from where I dwell, Like the shimmering heat in sunshine, like a cloud of dew at harvest time.
text.DRB: because thus saith our Lord to me: I will rest, and consider in my place, as the noon light is clear, and as a cloud of dew in the day of harvest.

text.NRSV: For before the harvest, when the blossom is over and the flower becomes a ripening grape, he will cut off the shoots with pruning hooks, and the spreading branches he will hew away.
text.Rotherham: For before harvest, when the bud is perfect, And, the blossom, becometh a ripening grape, Then will one cut down the twigs with pruning-hooks, And the tendrils, will he remove [and] cast down:
text.KJV: For afore the harvest, when the bud is perfect, and the sour grape is ripening in the flower, he shall both cut off the sprigs with pruning hooks, and take away and cut down the branches.
text.NABRE: Before the vintage, when the flowering has ended, and the blooms are succeeded by ripening grapes, Then comes the cutting of branches with pruning hooks, and the discarding of the lopped-off shoots.
text.DRB: For before harvest it hath wholly flourished, and unripe perfection shall spring forth, and the boughs thereof shall be cut off with hooks: and the things that are left, shall be cut off, and shaken out.

text.NRSV: They shall all be left to the birds of prey of the mountains and to the wild animals of the earth. And the birds of prey will summer on them, and all the wild animals of the earth will winter on them.
text.Rotherham: They shall be left together. To the ravenous birds, of the mountains, And to the beast of the earth,—Then shall the ravenous bird, summer upon them, And, every beast of the earth, upon them, shall winter.
text.KJV: They shall be left together unto the fowls of the mountains, and to the beasts of the earth: and the fowls shall summer upon them, and all the beasts of the earth shall winter upon them.
text.NABRE: They shall all be left to the mountain vultures and to the beasts of the earth; The vultures shall summer on them, all the beasts of the earth shall winter on them.
text.DRB: And they shall be left together to the birds of the mountains, and beasts of the earth: and the fowls shall be upon it the whole summer, and all the beasts of the earth shall winter upon it.

text.NRSV: At that time gifts will be brought to the Lord of hosts from a people tall and smooth, from a people feared near and far, a nation mighty and conquering, whose land the rivers divide, to Mount Zion, the place of the name of the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: In that time, shall there be borne along. As a present unto Yahweh of hosts A people drawn out and polished, Even from a people terrible from their beginning and onwards,—nation most mighty and subduing Whose land rivers, have cut through, Unto the place of the Name of Yahweh of hosts. Mount Zion.
text.KJV: In that time shall the present be brought unto the Lord of hosts of a people scattered and peeled, and from a people terrible from their beginning hitherto; a nation meted out and trodden under foot, whose land the rivers have spoiled, to the place of the name of the Lord of hosts, the mount Zion.
text.NABRE: Then will gifts be brought to the Lord of hosts—to the place of the name of the Lord of hosts, Mount Zion—from a people tall and bronzed, from a people dreaded near and far, a nation strong and conquering, whose land is washed by rivers.
text.DRB: In that time shall a gift be brought to the Lord of hosts, of a people plucked asunder and rent in pieces: of a terrible people, after which there hath been none other, of a nation expecting and trodden under foot, whose land the floods have spoiled, to the place of the name of the Lord of hosts, mount Sion.

text.NRSV: An oracle concerning Egypt. See, the Lord is riding on a swift cloud and comes to Egypt; the idols of Egypt will tremble at his presence, and the heart of the Egyptians will melt within them.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Egypt: Lo! Yahweh, riding upon a swift cloud, and he will enter Egypt, And the idols of Egypt shall shake at his presence, And, the heart of Egypt, shall melt within him;
text.KJV: The burden of Egypt. Behold, the Lord rideth upon a swift cloud, and shall come into Egypt: and the idols of Egypt shall be moved at his presence, and the heart of Egypt shall melt in the midst of it.
text.NABRE: Chapter 19 Egypt Oracle on Egypt: See, the Lord is riding on a swift cloud on his way to Egypt; The idols of Egypt tremble before him, the hearts of the Egyptians melt within them.
text.DRB: The burden of Ægypt. Behold our Lord will ascend upon a swift cloud, and will enter into Ægypt, and the idols of Ægypt shall be moved at his presence, & the heart of Ægypt shall melt in the midst thereof.

text.NRSV: I will stir up Egyptians against Egyptians, and they will fight, one against the other, neighbor against neighbor, city against city, kingdom against kingdom;
text.Rotherham: And I will stir up Egyptians against Egyptians, And they shall fight—Every one against his brother and Every one against his neighbour,—City against city, and Kingdom against kingdom.
text.KJV: And I will set the Egyptians against the Egyptians: and they shall fight every one against his brother, and every one against his neighbour; city against city, and kingdom against kingdom.
text.NABRE: I will stir up Egypt against Egypt: brother will war against brother, Neighbor against neighbor, city against city, kingdom against kingdom.
text.DRB: And I will make the Ægyptians to run together against the Ægyptians: and a man shall fight against his brother, & every man against his friend, city against city, kingdom against kingdom.

text.NRSV: the spirit of the Egyptians within them will be emptied out, and I will confound their plans; and they will consult the idols and the spirits of the dead and the ghosts and the familiar spirits;
text.Rotherham: And the spirit of Egypt, shall vanish, within them, Yea the sagacity thereof, will I swallow up,—And they will seek Unto the idols and Unto them that mutter, and Unto them that have familiar spirits, and Unto the wizards;
text.KJV: And the spirit of Egypt shall fail in the midst thereof; and I will destroy the counsel thereof: and they shall seek to the idols, and to the charmers, and to them that have familiar spirits, and to the wizards.
text.NABRE: The courage of the Egyptians shall ebb away within them, and I will bring their counsel to nought; They shall consult idols and charmers, ghosts and clairvoyants.
text.DRB: And the spirit of Ægypt shall be broken in the bowels thereof, & I will overthrow their counsel headlong: and they shall ask their idols, & their diviners, and Pythons, and Soothsayers.

text.NRSV: I will deliver the Egyptians into the hand of a hard master; a fierce king will rule over them, says the Lord, the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: And I will deliver the Egyptians into the hand of a cruel lord,—And a fierce king shall rule over them, Declareth the Lord, Yahweh of hosts.
text.KJV: And the Egyptians will I give over into the hand of a cruel lord; and a fierce king shall rule over them, saith the Lord, the Lord of hosts.
text.NABRE: I will deliver Egypt into the power of a cruel master, A harsh king who shall rule over them— oracle of the Lord, the Lord of hosts.
text.DRB: And I will deliver Ægypt into the hand of cruel masters, and a strong king shall rule over them, saith our Lord the God of hosts.

text.NRSV: The waters of the Nile will be dried up, and the river will be parched and dry;
text.Rotherham: And the waters shall be dried up from the great stream,—And the River, shall waste and be dry;
text.KJV: And the waters shall fail from the sea, and the river shall be wasted and dried up.
text.NABRE: The waters shall be drained from the sea, the river shall parch and dry up;
text.DRB: And the water of the sea shall be dried up, & the river shall be made desolate, and dry.

text.NRSV: its canals will become foul, and the branches of Egypt’s Nile will diminish and dry up, reeds and rushes will rot away.
text.Rotherham: And rivers, shall stink, The canals of Egypt be shallow and waste, Reed and rush, be withered;
text.KJV: And they shall turn the rivers far away; and the brooks of defence shall be emptied and dried up: the reeds and flags shall wither.
text.NABRE: Its streams shall become foul, and the canals of Egypt shall dwindle and parch. Reeds and rushes shall wither away,
text.DRB: And the rivers shall fail: the rivers of the rampiers shall be diminished, & dried up. The reed and bulrush shall wither:

text.NRSV: There will be bare places by the Nile, on the brink of the Nile; and all that is sown by the Nile will dry up, be blown away, and be no more.
text.Rotherham: The meadows by the Nile, by the mouth of the Nile. And all that is sown in the Nile, Shall be dry, driven away, and not be!
text.KJV: The paper reeds by the brooks, by the mouth of the brooks, and every thing sown by the brooks, shall wither, be driven away, and be no more.
text.NABRE: and bulrushes on the bank of the Nile; All the sown land along the Nile shall dry up and blow away, and be no more.
text.DRB: the channel of the river shall be spoiled of his fountain, & all sown corn field that is watered shall be dried up, it shall wither, and shall not be.

text.NRSV: The fishers will mourn and lament, all who cast hooks in the Nile; and those who spread nets on the water will languish.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the fishers, lament, And all shall mourn who cast in the Nile a hook,—And they who spread nets on the face of the waters shall languish;
text.KJV: The fishers also shall mourn, and all they that cast angle into the brooks shall lament, and they that spread nets upon the waters shall languish.
text.NABRE: The fishermen shall mourn and lament, all who cast hook in the Nile; Those who spread their nets in the water shall pine away.
text.DRB: And the fishers shall lament, & all that cast angle into the river shall mourn, and they that spread net upon the face of the water shall pine away.

text.NRSV: The workers in flax will be in despair, and the carders and those at the loom will grow pale.
text.Rotherham: Then shall turn pale The workers in combed flax,—and The weavers of white linen;
text.KJV: Moreover they that work in fine flax, and they that weave networks, shall be confounded.
text.NABRE: The linen-workers shall be disappointed, the combers and weavers shall turn pale;
text.DRB: They shall be confounded that wrought twisting flax, combing and weaving fine things.

text.NRSV: Its pillars will be crushed, and all who work for hire will be grieved.
text.Rotherham: Then shall her pillars be crushed,—All who make wages, be bowed down in soul.
text.KJV: And they shall be broken in the purposes thereof, all that make sluices and ponds for fish.
text.NABRE: The spinners shall be crushed, all the hired laborers shall be despondent.
text.DRB: And the watered places thereof shall be dry, all they that made pools to take fishes.

text.NRSV: The princes of Zoan are utter fools; the wise counselors of Pharaoh give stupid advice. How can you say to Pharaoh, “I am one of the sages, a descendant of ancient kings”?
text.Rotherham: Surely, foolish, are the princes of Zoan, the wisest counsellors of Pharaoh, in counsel are brutish,—How can ye say unto Pharaoh, Son of the wise, am I Son of the kings of olden time?
text.KJV: Surely the princes of Zoan are fools, the counsel of the wise counsellors of Pharaoh is become brutish: how say ye unto Pharaoh, I am the son of the wise, the son of ancient kings?
text.NABRE: Utter fools are the princes of Zoan! the wisest of Pharaoh’s advisers give stupid counsel. How can you say to Pharaoh, “I am a descendant of wise men, of ancient kings”?
text.DRB: The Princes of Tanis are fools, the wise counsellers of Pharao have given unwise counsel: how say ye unto Pharao: I am the son of the wise, the son of the ancient kings?

text.NRSV: Where now are your sages? Let them tell you and make known what the Lord of hosts has planned against Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Where then are thy wise men? Pray let them tell thee! And let them know what Yahweh of hosts hath purposed on Egypt!
text.KJV: Where are they? where are thy wise men? and let them tell thee now, and let them know what the Lord of hosts hath purposed upon Egypt.
text.NABRE: Where then are your wise men? Let them tell you and make known What the Lord of hosts has planned against Egypt.
text.DRB: Where are now thy wise men? Let them tell thee, & shew what the Lord of hosts hath thought concerning Ægypt.

text.NRSV: The princes of Zoan have become fools, and the princes of Memphis are deluded; those who are the cornerstones of her tribes have led Egypt astray.
text.Rotherham: Doting are the princes of Zoan, Deceived are the princes of Noph: They who are the corner-stone of her tribes, have led Egypt astray.
text.KJV: The princes of Zoan are become fools, the princes of Noph are deceived; they have also seduced Egypt, even they that are the stay of the tribes thereof.
text.NABRE: The princes of Zoan have become fools, the princes of Memphis have been deceived. The chiefs of its tribes have led Egypt astray.
text.DRB: The Princes of Tanis are become fools, the Princes of Memphis are withered away, they have deceived Ægypt, the corner of the peoples thereof.

text.NRSV: The Lord has poured into her a spirit of confusion; and they have made Egypt stagger in all its doings as a drunkard staggers around in vomit.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, hath infused in her midst, a spirit of perverseness,—And they have led Egypt astray into all his own doings, As a drunken man staggereth into his own vomit;
text.KJV: The Lord hath mingled a perverse spirit in the midst thereof: and they have caused Egypt to err in every work thereof, as a drunken man staggereth in his vomit.
text.NABRE: The Lord has prepared among them a spirit of dizziness, And they have made Egypt stagger in whatever she does, as a drunkard staggers in his vomit.
text.DRB: Our Lord hath mingled in the midst thereof the spirit of gladness: and they have made Ægypt to err in every work thereof, as he erreth that is drunk and vomiteth.

text.NRSV: Neither head nor tail, palm branch or reed, will be able to do anything for Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And Egypt shall have nothing which can be done, Which head or tail, palm-top or rush, can do!
text.KJV: Neither shall there be any work for Egypt, which the head or tail, branch or rush, may do.
text.NABRE: Egypt shall accomplish nothing— neither head nor tail, palm branch nor reed, shall accomplish anything.
text.DRB: And Ægypt shall have no work, to make the head and the tail, the perverter and restrainer.

text.NRSV: On that day the Egyptians will be like women, and tremble with fear before the hand that the Lord of hosts shakes over them.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall Egypt be like unto women,—And shall start and tremble because of the brandishing of the hand of Yahweh of hosts, which he is about to brandish over it.
text.KJV: In that day shall Egypt be like unto women: and it shall be afraid and fear because of the shaking of the hand of the Lord of hosts, which he shaketh over it.
text.NABRE: On that day the Egyptians shall be like women, trembling with fear, because of the Lord of hosts shaking his fist at them.
text.DRB: In that day Ægypt shall be as women, & they shall be astonished, & shall be afraid at the face of the moving of the hand of the Lord of hosts, which he shall move over it.

text.NRSV: And the land of Judah will become a terror to the Egyptians; everyone to whom it is mentioned will fear because of the plan that the Lord of hosts is planning against them.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the soil of Judah become, to Egypt, a terror; Every one to whom it is mentioned, will tremble,—Because of the purpose of Yahweh of hosts, which he is purposing against it.
text.KJV: And the land of Judah shall be a terror unto Egypt, every one that maketh mention thereof shall be afraid in himself, because of the counsel of the Lord of hosts, which he hath determined against it.
text.NABRE: And the land of Judah shall be a terror to the Egyptians. Every time they think of Judah, they shall stand in dread because of the plan the Lord of hosts has in mind for them.
text.DRB: And the land of Juda shall be a fear to Ægypt: every one that shall remember it, shall quake at the face of the counsel of the Lord of hosts, which he hath thought concerning it.

text.NRSV: On that day there will be five cities in the land of Egypt that speak the language of Canaan and swear allegiance to the Lord of hosts. One of these will be called the City of the Sun.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall there be five cities in the land of Egypt Speaking the language of Canaan, And swearing unto Yahweh of hosts,—The city of destruction, shall be the name of one!
text.KJV: In that day shall five cities in the land of Egypt speak the language of Canaan, and swear to the Lord of hosts; one shall be called, The city of destruction.
text.NABRE: On that day there shall be five cities in the land of Egypt that speak the language of Canaan and swear by the Lord of hosts; one shall be called “City of the Sun.”
text.DRB: In that day there shall be five cities in the land of Ægypt, speaking the tongue of Chanaan, and swearing by the Lord of hosts: one shall be called the city of the sun.

text.NRSV: On that day there will be an altar to the Lord in the center of the land of Egypt, and a pillar to the Lord at its border.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall there be An altar unto Yahweh, in the midst of the land of Egypt,—And a pillar near the boundary thereof unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: In that day shall there be an altar to the Lord in the midst of the land of Egypt, and a pillar at the border thereof to the Lord.
text.NABRE: On that day there shall be an altar to the Lord at the center of Egypt, and a sacred pillar to the Lord near its boundary.
text.DRB: In that day there shall be an altar of our Lord in the midst of the land of Ægypt, and a title of our Lord by the border thereof:

text.NRSV: It will be a sign and a witness to the Lord of hosts in the land of Egypt; when they cry to the Lord because of oppressors, he will send them a savior, and will defend and deliver them.
text.Rotherham: And it shall become a sign and a witness unto Yahweh of hosts in the land of Egypt,—For they will make outcry unto Yahweh, because of oppressors, That he would send them a saviour—and a great one Then will he deliver them.
text.KJV: And it shall be for a sign and for a witness unto the Lord of hosts in the land of Egypt: for they shall cry unto the Lord because of the oppressors, and he shall send them a saviour, and a great one, and he shall deliver them.
text.NABRE: This will be a sign and witness to the Lord of hosts in the land of Egypt, so that when they cry out to the Lord because of their oppressors, he will send them a savior to defend and deliver them.
text.DRB: it shall be for a sign, and for a testimony to the Lord of hosts in the land of Ægypt. For they shall cry to our Lord at the presence of the afflicter, and he shall send them a saviour and defender to deliver them.

text.NRSV: The Lord will make himself known to the Egyptians; and the Egyptians will know the Lord on that day, and will worship with sacrifice and burnt offering, and they will make vows to the Lord and perform them.
text.Rotherham: Then will, Yahweh, make himself known, to the Egyptians, So shall the Egyptians know, Yahweh, in that day,—And they will offer a sacrifice and a present And will vow a vow unto Yahweh and will perform.
text.KJV: And the Lord shall be known to Egypt, and the Egyptians shall know the Lord in that day, and shall do sacrifice and oblation; yea, they shall vow a vow unto the Lord, and perform it.
text.NABRE: The Lord shall make himself known to Egypt, and the Egyptians shall know the Lord in that day; they shall offer sacrifices and oblations, make vows to the Lord and fulfill them.
text.DRB: And our Lord shall be known of Ægypt, & the Ægyptians shall know our Lord in that day, and shall worship him in hosts and in gifts: and they shall vow vows to our Lord, and pay them.

text.NRSV: The Lord will strike Egypt, striking and healing; they will return to the Lord, and he will listen to their supplications and heal them.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh, will plague Egypt, plague and heal,—And they will turn unto Yahweh And he will be entreated of them and will heal them.
text.KJV: And the Lord shall smite Egypt: he shall smite and heal it: and they shall return even to the Lord, and he shall be intreated of them, and shall heal them.
text.NABRE: Although the Lord shall smite Egypt severely, he shall heal them; they shall turn to the Lord and he shall be moved by their entreaty and heal them.
text.DRB: And our Lord shall smite Ægypt with a stroke, and heal it, and they shall return to our Lord, and he shall be pacified towards them, and heal them.

text.NRSV: On that day there will be a highway from Egypt to Assyria, and the Assyrian will come into Egypt, and the Egyptian into Assyria, and the Egyptians will worship with the Assyrians.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall there be a highway. from Egypt to Assyria, And, the Assyrians, shall come into Egypt, And, the Egyptians, into Assyria; And, the Egyptians shall serve, with the Assyrians.
text.KJV: In that day shall there be a highway out of Egypt to Assyria, and the Assyrian shall come into Egypt, and the Egyptian into Assyria, and the Egyptians shall serve with the Assyrians.
text.NABRE: On that day there shall be a highway from Egypt to Assyria; the Assyrians shall enter Egypt, and the Egyptians enter Assyria, and the Egyptians shall worship with the Assyrians.
text.DRB: In that day there shall be a way from Ægypt to the Assyrians, and the Assyrian shall enter into Ægypt, and the Ægyptians to the Assyrians, & the Ægyptians shall serve Assur.

text.NRSV: On that day Israel will be the third with Egypt and Assyria, a blessing in the midst of the earth,
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall, Israel, be, a third, with Egypt and with Assyria,—A blessing in the midst of the earth:
text.KJV: In that day shall Israel be the third with Egypt and with Assyria, even a blessing in the midst of the land:
text.NABRE: On that day Israel shall be a third party with Egypt and Assyria, a blessing in the midst of the earth,
text.DRB: In that day shall Israel be a third to the Ægyptian and the Assyrian: a blessing in the midst of the earth,

text.NRSV: whom the Lord of hosts has blessed, saying, “Blessed be Egypt my people, and Assyria the work of my hands, and Israel my heritage.”
text.Rotherham: Whom Yahweh of hosts hath blessed saying,—Blessed, be My people—the Egyptians, And the work of my hands—the Assyrians, And mine own inheritance—Israel.
text.KJV: Whom the Lord of hosts shall bless, saying, Blessed be Egypt my people, and Assyria the work of my hands, and Israel mine inheritance.
text.NABRE: when the Lord of hosts gives this blessing: “Blessed be my people Egypt, and the work of my hands Assyria, and my heritage, Israel.”
text.DRB: which the Lord of hosts hath blessed, saying: Blessed be my people of Ægypt, and the work of my hands to the Assyrian: but Israel is mine inheritance.

text.NRSV: In the year that the commander-in-chief, who was sent by King Sargon of Assyria, came to Ashdod and fought against it and took it—
text.Rotherham: In the year that Tartan entered Ashdod, when Sargon king of Assyria, sent him,—and he fought against Ashdod and captured it,—
text.KJV: In the year that Tartan came unto Ashdod, (when Sargon the king of Assyria sent him,) and fought against Ashdod, and took it;
text.NABRE: In the year the general sent by Sargon, king of Assyria, came to Ashdod, fought against it, and captured it—
text.DRB: In the year that Tharthan entered into Azotus, when Sargon the king of the Assyrians had sent him, and he had fought against Azotus, and had taken it:

text.NRSV: at that time the Lord had spoken to Isaiah son of Amoz, saying, “Go, and loose the sackcloth from your loins and take your sandals off your feet,” and he had done so, walking naked and barefoot.
text.Rotherham: At that time, spake Yahweh, through Isaiah son of Amoz, saying, Go, and loose the sackcloth from off thy loins, And thy sandal, draw thou off from thy foot,—And he did so, walking disrobed and barefoot.
text.KJV: At the same time spake the Lord by Isaiah the son of Amoz, saying, Go and loose the sackcloth from off thy loins, and put off thy shoe from thy foot. And he did so, walking naked and barefoot.
text.NABRE: at that time the Lord had spoken through Isaiah, the son of Amoz: Go and take off the sackcloth from your waist, and remove the sandals from your feet. This he did, walking naked and barefoot.
text.DRB: at that time our Lord spake in the hand of Isaie the son of Amos, saying: Go, and loose the sackcloth from off thy loins, and take off thy shoes from thy feet. And he did so going naked, and barefoot.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said, “Just as my servant Isaiah has walked naked and barefoot for three years as a sign and a portent against Egypt and Ethiopia,
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh,—As my servant Isaiah, hath walked, disrobed and barefoot three years as a sign and a wonder against Egypt and against Ethiopia,
text.KJV: And the Lord said, Like as my servant Isaiah hath walked naked and barefoot three years for a sign and wonder upon Egypt and upon Ethiopia;
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said: Just as my servant Isaiah has gone naked and barefoot for three years as a sign and portent against Egypt and Ethiopia,
text.DRB: And our Lord said: As my servant Isaie hath walked, naked and barefoot, it shall be a sign and a wonder of three years upon Ægypt, and upon Æthiopia,

text.NRSV: so shall the king of Assyria lead away the Egyptians as captives and the Ethiopians as exiles, both the young and the old, naked and barefoot, with buttocks uncovered, to the shame of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: So, shall the king of Assyria lead away the captives of Egypt and the exiles of Ethiopia young and old disrobed and barefoot, with their persons behind Uncovered the shame of Egypt.
text.KJV: So shall the king of Assyria lead away the Egyptians prisoners, and the Ethiopians captives, young and old, naked and barefoot, even with their buttocks uncovered, to the shame of Egypt.
text.NABRE: so shall the king of Assyria lead away captives from Egypt, and exiles from Ethiopia, young and old, naked and barefoot, with buttocks uncovered, the shame of Egypt.
text.DRB: so shall the king of the Assyrians lead the captivity of Ægypt, and the transmigration of Æthiopia, young and old, naked and unshod, their buttocks uncovered to the ignominy of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Then they shall be dismayed and confounded because of Ethiopia their hope and Egypt their boast.
text.Rotherham: Thus shall they be confounded and turn pale,—For Ethiopia, their expectation, and For Egypt their boast;
text.KJV: And they shall be afraid and ashamed of Ethiopia their expectation, and of Egypt their glory.
text.NABRE: They shall be dismayed and ashamed because of Ethiopia, their hope, and because of Egypt, their boast.
text.DRB: And they shall fear, & be ashamed of Æthiopia their hope, and of Ægypt their glory.

text.NRSV: On that day the inhabitants of this coastland will say, ‘See, this is what has happened to those in whom we hoped and to whom we fled for help and deliverance from the king of Assyria! And we, how shall we escape?’ ”
text.Rotherham: And the inhabitant of this shore, shall exclaim, in that day, Lo! such, is our expectation, whereunto we fled for help, that we might be delivered from the presence of the king of Assyria! How then shall, we, escape?
text.KJV: And the inhabitant of this isle shall say in that day, Behold, such is our expectation, whither we flee for help to be delivered from the king of Assyria: and how shall we escape?
text.NABRE: The inhabitants of this coastland shall say on that day, “See what has happened to those we hoped in, to whom we fled for help and deliverance from the king of Assyria! What escape is there for us now?”
text.DRB: And the inhabitant of this isle shall say in that day: Lo this was our hope, to whom we fled for help, that they should deliver us from the face of the king of the Assyrians: and how shall we be able to escape?

text.NRSV: The oracle concerning the desert of the sea. As whirlwinds in the Negeb sweep on, it comes from the desert, from a terrible land.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on the desert of the sea: As storm-winds in the South which with a rush from the desert, do come from a terrible land,
text.KJV: The burden of the desert of the sea. As whirlwinds in the south pass through; so it cometh from the desert, from a terrible land.
text.NABRE: Chapter 21 Fall of Babylon Oracle on the wastelands by the sea: Like whirlwinds sweeping through the Negeb, it comes from the desert, from the fearful land.
text.DRB: The burden of the desert sea. As whirlwinds come from the South, it cometh from the desert, from an horrible land.

text.NRSV: A stern vision is declared to me; the betrayer betrays, and the destroyer destroys. Go up, O Elam, lay siege, O Media; all the sighing she has caused I bring to an end.
text.Rotherham: [So] hath, a grievous vision, been told me:—the deceiver, is deceiving. And the spoiler, is spoiling, Go up, O Elam Besiege O Media, All the sighing she hath caused, have I made to cease.
text.KJV: A grievous vision is declared unto me; the treacherous dealer dealeth treacherously, and the spoiler spoileth. Go up, O Elam: besiege, O Media; all the sighing thereof have I made to cease.
text.NABRE: A harsh vision has been announced to me: “The traitor betrays, the despoiler spoils. Go up, O Elam; besiege, O Media; put an end to all its groaning!”
text.DRB: A sore vision was told me: he that is incredulous doth unfaithfully: & he that is a spoiler, wasteth. Come up Ælam, besiege o Mede: I have made all the mourning thereof to cease.

text.NRSV: Therefore my loins are filled with anguish; pangs have seized me, like the pangs of a woman in labor; I am bowed down so that I cannot hear, I am dismayed so that I cannot see.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, are my loins filled with anguish, Pangs, have seized me, as the pangs of her that is giving birth,—I writhe so that I cannot hear, I tremble, so that I cannot see:
text.KJV: Therefore are my loins filled with pain: pangs have taken hold upon me, as the pangs of a woman that travaileth: I was bowed down at the hearing of it; I was dismayed at the seeing of it.
text.NABRE: Therefore my loins are filled with anguish, pangs have seized me like those of a woman in labor; I am too bewildered to hear, too dismayed to look.
text.DRB: Therefore are my loins filled with sorrow, anguish hath possessed me, as the anguish of a woman that travaileth: I fell down when I heard it, I was troubled when I saw it.

text.NRSV: My mind reels, horror has appalled me; the twilight I longed for has been turned for me into trembling.
text.Rotherham: My heart fluttereth, A horror, terrifieth me,—My twilight of pleasure, hath he turned for me into a time of trembling.
text.KJV: My heart panted, fearfulness affrighted me: the night of my pleasure hath he turned into fear unto me.
text.NABRE: My mind reels, shuddering assails me; The twilight I yearned for he has turned into dread.
text.DRB: Mine heart failed, darkness made me astonished: Babylon my beloved is made a miracle unto me.

text.NRSV: They prepare the table, they spread the rugs, they eat, they drink. Rise up, commanders, oil the shield!
text.Rotherham: [Ye thought] to prepare the table—spread the mat—eat—drink! …Arise ye chieftains anoint the shield!
text.KJV: Prepare the table, watch in the watchtower, eat, drink: arise, ye princes, and anoint the shield.
text.NABRE: They set the table, spread out the rugs; they eat, they drink. Rise up, O princes, oil the shield!
text.DRB: Lay the table, look about in the watch tower the eaters and drinkers: arise ye Princes, take shield.

text.NRSV: For thus the Lord said to me: “Go, set a watchman, let him announce what he sees.
text.Rotherham: For, thus, hath My Lord said unto me,—Go, set the watchman, What he seeth, let him tell!
text.KJV: For thus hath the Lord said unto me, Go, set a watchman, let him declare what he seeth.
text.NABRE: For thus my Lord said to me: Go, station a watchman, let him tell what he sees.
text.DRB: For thus hath our Lord said to me: Go, and set a watchman: and what soever he shall see, let him tell.

text.NRSV: When he sees riders, horsemen in pairs, riders on donkeys, riders on camels, let him listen diligently, very diligently.”
text.Rotherham: When he seeth A train of horsemen in double rank, A train of asses, A train of camels, Then shall he hearken attentively with diligent heed.
text.KJV: And he saw a chariot with a couple of horsemen, a chariot of asses, and a chariot of camels; and he hearkened diligently with much heed:
text.NABRE: If he sees a chariot, a pair of horses, Someone riding a donkey, someone riding a camel, Then let him pay heed, very close heed.
text.DRB: And he saw a chariot of two horsemen, a rider upon an ass, and a rider upon a camel: and he beheld them diligently with much looking.

text.NRSV: Then the watcher called out: “Upon a watchtower, O Lord, I stand continually by day, and at my post I am stationed throughout the night.
text.Rotherham: Then cried he. A lion! On the watch, O My Lord, had I been standing continually, by day, And at my post, had I been stationed whole nights;—
text.KJV: And he cried, A lion: My lord, I stand continually upon the watchtower in the daytime, and I am set in my ward whole nights:
text.NABRE: Then the watchman cried, “On the watchtower, my Lord, I stand constantly by day; And I stay at my post through all the watches of the night.
text.DRB: And a lion cried: I am upon the watch tower of our Lord, standing continually by day: and I am upon my watch, standing whole nights.

text.NRSV: See, there they come, riders, horsemen in pairs!” Then he responded, “Fallen, fallen is Babylon; and all the images of her gods lie shattered on the ground.”
text.Rotherham: When lo! here was a train of men coming. With horsemen in double rank,—And one began and said, Fallen! fallen! is Babylon, And all the images of her gods, are smashed to the ground!
text.KJV: And, behold, here cometh a chariot of men, with a couple of horsemen. And he answered and said, Babylon is fallen, is fallen; and all the graven images of her gods he hath broken unto the ground.
text.NABRE: Here he comes— a single chariot, a pair of horses— He calls out and says, ‘Fallen, fallen is Babylon! All the images of her gods are smashed to the ground!’”
text.DRB: Behold this man cometh, the rider upon the chariot of two horsemen, and he answered and said: Fallen, fallen is Babylon, and all the sculptiles of the gods thereof are broken to the ground.

text.NRSV: O my threshed and winnowed one, what I have heard from the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, I announce to you.
text.Rotherham: O thou My threshing! And the grain of my corn-floor! That which I have heard from Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, Have I declared, unto you.
text.KJV: O my threshing, and the corn of my floor: that which I have heard of the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, have I declared unto you.
text.NABRE: To you, who have been threshed, beaten on my threshing floor, What I have heard from the Lord of hosts, The God of Israel, I have announced to you.
text.DRB: O my threshing, and children of my floor, the things that I have heard of the Lord of hosts the God of Israel, I have shewed unto you.

text.NRSV: The oracle concerning Dumah. One is calling to me from Seir, “Watchman, what of the night? Watchman, what of the night?”
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Dumah: Unto me, is one crying—out of Seir, Watchman! how far gone is the night? Watchman, how far gone is the night?
text.KJV: The burden of Dumah. He calleth to me out of Seir, Watchman, what of the night? Watchman, what of the night?
text.NABRE: Dumah Oracle on Dumah: They call to me from Seir, “Watchman, how much longer the night? Watchman, how much longer the night?”
text.DRB: The burden of Duma cryeth to me out of Seir: Watchman what of the night? Watchman what of the night?

text.NRSV: The watchman says: “Morning comes, and also the night. If you will inquire, inquire; come back again.”
text.Rotherham: Said the watchman, There cometh a morning. But also a night,—If ye will enquire, enquire, ye—Come, again!
text.KJV: The watchman said, The morning cometh, and also the night: if ye will enquire, enquire ye: return, come.
text.NABRE: The watchman replies, “Morning has come, and again night. If you will ask, ask; come back again.”
text.DRB: The watchman said: morning is come and night: if you seek, seek: return, come.

text.NRSV: The oracle concerning the desert plain. In the scrub of the desert plain you will lodge, O caravans of Dedanites.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Arabia,—Among the shrubs in Arabia, must ye lodge, Ye caravans of Dedanites.
text.KJV: The burden upon Arabia. In the forest in Arabia shall ye lodge, O ye travelling companies of Dedanim.
text.NABRE: In the Steppe Oracle: in the steppe: In the thicket in the steppe you will spend the night, caravans of Dedanites.
text.DRB: The burden in Arabia. In the forest at even you shall sleep, in the paths of Dedanim.

text.NRSV: Bring water to the thirsty, meet the fugitive with bread, O inhabitants of the land of Tema.
text.Rotherham: To meet the thirsty, bring ye water,—Ye dwellers in the land of Tema; With bread for him, get in advance of him that is in flight!
text.KJV: The inhabitants of the land of Tema brought water to him that was thirsty, they prevented with their bread him that fled.
text.NABRE: Meet the thirsty, bring them water, inhabitants of the land of Tema, greet the fugitives with bread.
text.DRB: Meeting the thirsty bring water, you that inhabit the land of the South, with bread meet him that flyeth.

text.NRSV: For they have fled from the swords, from the drawn sword, from the bent bow, and from the stress of battle.
text.Rotherham: For, before swords, have they fled: Before a sword that is drawn, Before a bow that is bent, And before the stress of war.
text.KJV: For they fled from the swords, from the drawn sword, and from the bent bow, and from the grievousness of war.
text.NABRE: For they have fled from the sword, from the drawn sword; From the taut bow, from the thick of battle.
text.DRB: For they are fled from the face of the swords, from the face of the sword hanging over, from the face of the bow bent, from the face of a grievous battle.

text.NRSV: For thus the Lord said to me: Within a year, according to the years of a hired worker, all the glory of Kedar will come to an end;
text.Rotherham: For, thus, hath My Lord said unto me,—Within a year according to the yearn of a hireling, shall fail all the glory of Kedar;
text.KJV: For thus hath the Lord said unto me, Within a year, according to the years of an hireling, and all the glory of Kedar shall fail:
text.NABRE: For thus the Lord has said to me: In another year, like the years of a hired laborer, all the glory of Kedar shall come to an end.
text.DRB: Because thus saith our Lord to me: Yet in one year, as in the year of an hired man, and all the glory of Cedar shall be taken away.

text.NRSV: and the remaining bows of Kedar’s warriors will be few; for the Lord, the God of Israel, has spoken.
text.Rotherham: And, the remnant of the record of bowmen, The heroes of the sons of Kedar, shall become few; For Yahweh God of Israel, hath spoken.
text.KJV: And the residue of the number of archers, the mighty men of the children of Kedar, shall be diminished: for the Lord God of Israel hath spoken it.
text.NABRE: Few of Kedar’s stalwart archers shall remain, for the Lord, the God of Israel, has spoken.
text.DRB: And the remnant of the number of the strong archers of the children of Cedar shall be diminished: for our Lord the God of Israel hath spoken it.

text.NRSV: The oracle concerning the valley of vision. What do you mean that you have gone up, all of you, to the housetops,
text.Rotherham: The oracle on the valley of vision,—What aileth thee, then, That thou art wholly gone up to the house-tops?
text.KJV: The burden of the valley of vision. What aileth thee now, that thou art wholly gone up to the housetops?
text.NABRE: Chapter 22 The Valley of Vision Oracle on the Valley of Vision: What is the matter with you now, that you have gone up, all of you, to the housetops,
text.DRB: The burden of the vale of vision. What aileth thee, that thou art also wholly gone up into the house tops?

text.NRSV: you that are full of shoutings, tumultuous city, exultant town? Your slain are not slain by the sword, nor are they dead in battle.
text.Rotherham: With tumults, art thou filled, thou citadel in commotion! city exultant! Thy slain, are Not the slain, of the sword, Nor the dead in battle.
text.KJV: Thou that art full of stirs, a tumultuous city, a joyous city: thy slain men are not slain with the sword, nor dead in battle.
text.NABRE: You who were full of noise, tumultuous city, exultant town? Your slain are not slain with the sword, nor killed in battle.
text.DRB: full of clamour, a populous city, rejoicing: thy slain are not slain by the sword, nor dead in battle.

text.NRSV: Your leaders have all fled together; they were captured without the use of a bow. All of you who were found were captured together, though they had fled far away.
text.Rotherham: All thy ruler, having fled, together, by the bow, are taken captive: All found in thee have been taken captive, together, Far away, have they fled.
text.KJV: All thy rulers are fled together, they are bound by the archers: all that are found in thee are bound together, which have fled from far.
text.NABRE: All your leaders fled away together, they were captured without use of bow; All who were found were captured together, though they had fled afar off.
text.DRB: All thy Princes are fled together, and are bound hard: all that were found, are bound together, they are fled far off.

text.NRSV: Therefore I said: “Look away from me, let me weep bitter tears; do not try to comfort me for the destruction of my people.”
text.Rotherham: For this cause, I said—Look away from me, Bitterly, will I weep,—Do not press to comfort me, For the ruin of the daughter of my people.
text.KJV: Therefore said I, Look away from me; I will weep bitterly, labour not to comfort me, because of the spoiling of the daughter of my people.
text.NABRE: That is why I say: Turn away from me, let me weep bitterly; Do not try to comfort me for the ruin of the daughter of my people.
text.DRB: Therefore have I said: Depart from me, I will weep bitterly: labour not to comfort me, for the destruction of the daughter of my people.

text.NRSV: For the Lord God of hosts has a day of tumult and trampling and confusion in the valley of vision, a battering down of walls and a cry for help to the mountains.
text.Rotherham: For a day of confusion and downtreading and perplexity, pertaineth to My Lord, Yahweh of hosts, in the valley of vision, an undermining of walls, and a crying for help to the mountain.
text.KJV: For it is a day of trouble, and of treading down, and of perplexity by the Lord God of hosts in the valley of vision, breaking down the walls, and of crying to the mountains.
text.NABRE: It is a day of panic, rout and confusion, from the Lord, the God of hosts, in the Valley of Vision Walls crash; a cry for help to the mountains.
text.DRB: For it is a day of slaughter, and conculcation, and weepings to our Lord the God of hosts, in the vale of visitation, searching the wall, and magnifical upon the mountain.

text.NRSV: Elam bore the quiver with chariots and horsemen, and Kir uncovered the shield.
text.Rotherham: Yea, Elam, beareth the quiver, With trains of men, [and] horsemen,—And, Kir, hath uncovered the shield.
text.KJV: And Elam bare the quiver with chariots of men and horsemen, and Kir uncovered the shield.
text.NABRE: Elam takes up the quiver, Aram mounts the horses and Kir uncovers the shields.
text.DRB: And Ælam took quiver, chariot of the horseman, and the shield hath made the wall naked.

text.NRSV: Your choicest valleys were full of chariots, and the horsemen took their stand at the gates.
text.Rotherham: And it hath come to pass that the choice of thy vales, are full of chariots; Yea, the horsemen, have set themselves, in array, at the gate.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, that thy choicest valleys shall be full of chariots, and the horsemen shall set themselves in array at the gate.
text.NABRE: Your choice valleys are filled with chariots, horses are posted at the gates—
text.DRB: And thy principal valleys shall be full of chariots, and the horsemen shall place themselves in the gate.

text.NRSV: He has taken away the covering of Judah. On that day you looked to the weapons of the House of the Forest,
text.Rotherham: Then removed he the veil of Judah,—Yea thou didst peer on that day, into the armoury of the forest-house;
text.KJV: And he discovered the covering of Judah, and thou didst look in that day to the armour of the house of the forest.
text.NABRE: and shelter over Judah is removed. On that day you looked to the weapons in the House of the Forest;
text.DRB: And the covering of Juda shall be discovered, and thou shalt see in that day the armoury of the house of the forest.

text.NRSV: and you saw that there were many breaches in the city of David, and you collected the waters of the lower pool.
text.Rotherham: And the breaches in the city of David, ye beheld for they were many,—So ye gathered together the waters of the lower pool;
text.KJV: Ye have seen also the breaches of the city of David, that they are many: and ye gathered together the waters of the lower pool.
text.NABRE: you saw that the breaches in the City of David were many; you collected the water of the lower pool.
text.DRB: And you shall see the breaches of the city of David, because they are multiplied: and you have gathered together the waters of the lower pool,

text.NRSV: You counted the houses of Jerusalem, and you broke down the houses to fortify the wall.
text.Rotherham: And the houses of Jerusalem, ye counted,—And brake down the houses, to fortify the wall;
text.KJV: And ye have numbered the houses of Jerusalem, and the houses have ye broken down to fortify the wall.
text.NABRE: You numbered the houses of Jerusalem, tearing some down to strengthen the wall;
text.DRB: and have numbered the houses of Jerusalem, and destroyed houses to fortify the wall.

text.NRSV: You made a reservoir between the two walls for the water of the old pool. But you did not look to him who did it, or have regard for him who planned it long ago.
text.Rotherham: And a reservoir, ye made between the two walls, for the waters of the ancient pool,—And had no regard unto him that made it, Nor unto him that formed it long ago, had ye respect.
text.KJV: Ye made also a ditch between the two walls for the water of the old pool: but ye have not looked unto the maker thereof, neither had respect unto him that fashioned it long ago.
text.NABRE: you made a reservoir between the two walls for the water of the old pool. But you did not look to the city’s Maker, nor consider the one who fashioned it long ago.
text.DRB: And you have made a lake between the two walls to the water of the old pool: & you looked not up to him that made it, and the worker thereof long before you saw not.

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord God of hosts called for weeping and mourning, for baldness and putting on sackcloth;
text.Rotherham: And, when My Lord Yahweh of hosts called in that day,—for weeping, and for lamentation, and for shaving bare and for girding with sackcloth,
text.KJV: And in that day did the Lord God of hosts call to weeping, and to mourning, and to baldness, and to girding with sackcloth:
text.NABRE: On that day the Lord, the God of hosts, called For weeping and mourning, for shaving the head and wearing sackcloth.
text.DRB: And our Lord the God of hosts shall call in that day to weeping, and to mourning, to baldness, and to girdle of sackcloth:

text.NRSV: but instead there was joy and festivity, killing oxen and slaughtering sheep, eating meat and drinking wine. “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.”
text.Rotherham: Then lo! joy and rejoicing, killing oxen and slaughtering sheep, eating flesh and drinking wine,—Let us eat and drink, For to-morrow, we may die!
text.KJV: And behold joy and gladness, slaying oxen, and killing sheep, eating flesh, and drinking wine: let us eat and drink; for to morrow we shall die.
text.NABRE: But look! instead, there was celebration and joy, slaughtering cattle and butchering sheep, Eating meat and drinking wine: “Eat and drink, for tomorrow we die!”
text.DRB: and behold joy and gladness, to kill calves, and to slay rams, to eat flesh, and to drink wine: Let us eat and drink: for tomorrow we shall die.

text.NRSV: The Lord of hosts has revealed himself in my ears: “Surely this iniquity will not be forgiven you until you die,” says the Lord God of hosts.
text.Rotherham: Therefore did Yahweh reveal himself in mine ears. Surely there shall be no propitiatory-covering put over this iniquity for you until ye die, Saith My Lord Yahweh of hosts.
text.KJV: And it was revealed in mine ears by the Lord of hosts, Surely this iniquity shall not be purged from you till ye die, saith the Lord God of hosts.
text.NABRE: This message was revealed in my hearing from the Lord of hosts: This iniquity will not be forgiven you until you die, says the Lord, the God of hosts.
text.DRB: And the voice of the Lord of hosts was revealed in mine ears: If this iniquity shall be forgiven you until you die, saith our Lord the God of hosts.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God of hosts: Come, go to this steward, to Shebna, who is master of the household, and say to him:
text.Rotherham: Thus, said My Lord Yahweh, of hosts, Come go in unto this steward, Unto Shebna, who is over the house:
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God of hosts, Go, get thee unto this treasurer, even unto Shebna, which is over the house, and say,
text.NABRE: Shebna and Eliakim Thus says the Lord, the God of hosts: Up, go to that official, Shebna, master of the palace,
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord the God of hosts: Go, get thee in to him, that dwelleth in the tabernacle, to Sobna the provost of the temple, and thou shalt say to him:

text.NRSV: What have you to do here, and whom have you here, that you have cut out a tomb here for yourself, cutting a tomb on the height, and carving a habitation for yourself in the rock?
text.Rotherham: What doest thou here? And whom hast thou here? That thou hast hewn for thyself here a sepulchre,—As one hewing on high his sepulchre, Cutting out in the cliff a habitation for himself?
text.KJV: What hast thou here? and whom hast thou here, that thou hast hewed thee out a sepulchre here, as he that heweth him out a sepulchre on high, and that graveth an habitation for himself in a rock?
text.NABRE: “What have you here? Whom have you here, that you have hewn for yourself a tomb here, Hewing a tomb on high, carving a resting place in the rock?”
text.DRB: What doest thou here, or as if thou were somebody here? Because thou hast cut thee out a sepulchre here, thou hast cut out a memorial diligently in an high place, a tabernacle for thee in a rock.

text.NRSV: The Lord is about to hurl you away violently, my man. He will seize firm hold of you,
text.Rotherham: Lo! Yahweh, is about to hurl thee, with a hurl, O mighty man,—And roll thee with a roll;
text.KJV: Behold, the Lord will carry thee away with a mighty captivity, and will surely cover thee.
text.NABRE: The Lord shall hurl you down headlong, mortal man! He shall grip you firmly,
text.DRB: Behold our Lord will cause thee to be carried away, as a cock is carried, and he will lift thee up as a garment.

text.NRSV: whirl you round and round, and throw you like a ball into a wide land; there you shall die, and there your splendid chariots shall lie, O you disgrace to your master’s house!
text.Rotherham: He will, toss, thee, with a toss, like a ball, into a country wide on both hands,—There, shalt thou die, And there shall thy glorious chariots be the contempt of the house of thy lord.
text.KJV: He will surely violently turn and toss thee like a ball into a large country: there shalt thou die, and there the chariots of thy glory shall be the shame of thy lord’s house.
text.NABRE: And roll you up and toss you like a ball into a broad land. There you will die, there with the chariots you glory in, you disgrace to your master’s house!
text.DRB: Crowning he will crown thee with tribulation, he will cast thee as a ball into a broad and large country: there shalt thou die, and there shall the chariot of thy glory be, the ignominy of the house of thy Lord.

text.NRSV: I will thrust you from your office, and you will be pulled down from your post.
text.Rotherham: Thus will I thrust thee out from thine office,—And from thy station, shall one tear thee down.
text.KJV: And I will drive thee from thy station, and from thy state shall he pull thee down.
text.NABRE: I will thrust you from your office and pull you down from your station.
text.DRB: And I will expel thee from thy standing, and depose thee from thy ministry.

text.NRSV: On that day I will call my servant Eliakim son of Hilkiah,
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day, That I will call for my servant, for Eliakim son of Hilkiah,
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass in that day, that I will call my servant Eliakim the son of Hilkiah:
text.NABRE: On that day I will summon my servant Eliakim, son of Hilkiah;
text.DRB: And it shall be in that day: I will call my servant Eliacim the son of Helcias,

text.NRSV: and will clothe him with your robe and bind your sash on him. I will commit your authority to his hand, and he shall be a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem and to the house of Judah.
text.Rotherham: And will clothe him with thy tunic And with thy girdle, will I gird him, And thine authority, will I deliver into his hand,—So shall he become a father To the inhabitant of Jerusalem and To the house of Judah.
text.KJV: And I will clothe him with thy robe, and strengthen him with thy girdle, and I will commit thy government into his hand: and he shall be a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and to the house of Judah.
text.NABRE: I will clothe him with your robe, gird him with your sash, confer on him your authority. He shall be a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and to the house of Judah.
text.DRB: and will clothe him with thy coat, and will strengthen him with thy girdle, and will give thy power into his hand: and he shall be as a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, & to the house of Juda.

text.NRSV: I will place on his shoulder the key of the house of David; he shall open, and no one shall shut; he shall shut, and no one shall open.
text.Rotherham: And I will lay the key of the house of David upon his shoulder,—And he shall open and none shall shut, And shut and none shall open;
text.KJV: And the key of the house of David will I lay upon his shoulder; so he shall open, and none shall shut; and he shall shut, and none shall open.
text.NABRE: I will place the key of the House of David on his shoulder; what he opens, no one will shut, what he shuts, no one will open.
text.DRB: And I will give the key of the house of David upon his shoulder: and he shall open, and there shall be none to shut: and he shall shut, and there shall be none to open.

text.NRSV: I will fasten him like a peg in a secure place, and he will become a throne of honor to his ancestral house.
text.Rotherham: And I will fasten him as a peg in a sure place,—And he shall become a throne of glory, to the house of his father;
text.KJV: And I will fasten him as a nail in a sure place; and he shall be for a glorious throne to his father’s house.
text.NABRE: I will fix him as a peg in a firm place, a seat of honor for his ancestral house;
text.DRB: And I will fasten him as a pin in a sure place, and he shall be for a throne of glory to the house of his father.

text.NRSV: And they will hang on him the whole weight of his ancestral house, the offspring and issue, every small vessel, from the cups to all the flagons.
text.Rotherham: And they shall hang upon him all the weight of his father’s house—The offshoots and the side-twigs—All the small vessels, Both the cups and all the jugs
text.KJV: And they shall hang upon him all the glory of his father’s house, the offspring and the issue, all vessels of small quantity, from the vessels of cups, even to all the vessels of flagons.
text.NABRE: On him shall hang all the glory of his ancestral house: descendants and offspring, all the little dishes, from bowls to jugs.
text.DRB: And they shall hang upon him all the glory of his father's house, diverse kinds of vessels, every little vessel, from vessels of cups even to every instrument of music.

text.NRSV: On that day, says the Lord of hosts, the peg that was fastened in a secure place will give way; it will be cut down and fall, and the load that was on it will perish, for the Lord has spoken.
text.Rotherham: In that day,—Declareth Yahweh of hosts Shall the peg, give way, that was fastened in a sure place,—Yea it shall be cut off and fall And the burden that was upon it shall perish, For, Yahweh, hath spoken!
text.KJV: In that day, saith the Lord of hosts, shall the nail that is fastened in the sure place be removed, and be cut down, and fall; and the burden that was upon it shall be cut off: for the Lord hath spoken it.
text.NABRE: On that day, says the Lord of hosts, the peg fixed in a firm place shall give way, break off and fall, and the weight that hung on it shall be done away with; for the Lord has spoken.
text.DRB: In that day saith the Lord of hosts, shall the pin be taken away, that was fastened in the sure place: and that which hung thereon, shall be broken, and fall, and perish, because our Lord hath spoken.

text.NRSV: The oracle concerning Tyre. Wail, O ships of Tarshish, for your fortress is destroyed. When they came from Cyprus, they learned of it.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Tyre,—Howl! ye ships of Tarshish, For it is laid too waste to be a haven to enter, From the land of Cyprus, hath it been unveiled to them.
text.KJV: The burden of Tyre. Howl, ye ships of Tarshish; for it is laid waste, so that there is no house, no entering in: from the land of Chittim it is revealed to them.
text.NABRE: Chapter 23 Tyre and Sidon Oracle on Tyre: Wail, ships of Tarshish, for your port is destroyed; From the land of the Kittim the news reaches them.
text.DRB: The burden of Tyre. Howl ye ships of the sea, because the house is destroyed, from whence they were wont to come: from the land of Cethim it is revealed to them.

text.NRSV: Be still, O inhabitants of the coast, O merchants of Sidon, your messengers crossed over the sea
text.Rotherham: Be dumb, ye inhabitants of the Coast,—Whom the merchants of Zidon, passing over the sea, once replenished;
text.KJV: Be still, ye inhabitants of the isle; thou whom the merchants of Zidon, that pass over the sea, have replenished.
text.NABRE: Silence! you who dwell on the coast, you merchants of Sidon, Whose messengers crossed the sea
text.DRB: Hold your peace ye that dwell in the isle: the traffickers of Sidon passing over the sea, have replenished thee.

text.NRSV: and were on the mighty waters; your revenue was the grain of Shihor, the harvest of the Nile; you were the merchant of the nations.
text.Rotherham: Yea on mighty waters, was the grain of Shihor, The harvest of the Nile, was her increase,—And so she became a mart of nations.
text.KJV: And by great waters the seed of Sihor, the harvest of the river, is her revenue; and she is a mart of nations.
text.NABRE: over the deep waters, Whose revenue was the grain of Shihor, the harvest of the Nile, you who were the merchant among the nations.
text.DRB: The seed of Nilus in many waters, the harvest of the river was her fruits: & she was made the traffic of the nations.

text.NRSV: Be ashamed, O Sidon, for the sea has spoken, the fortress of the sea, saying: “I have neither labored nor given birth, I have neither reared young men nor brought up young women.”
text.Rotherham: Turn thou pale, O Zidon, For spoken hath the sea, the fortress of the sea saying,—I have neither been in pangs nor given birth I have neither brought up young men nor promoted virgins.
text.KJV: Be thou ashamed, O Zidon: for the sea hath spoken, even the strength of the sea, saying, I travail not, nor bring forth children, neither do I nourish up young men, nor bring up virgins.
text.NABRE: Be ashamed, Sidon, fortress on the sea, for the sea has spoken, “I have not been in labor, nor given birth, nor raised young men, nor reared young women.”
text.DRB: Be ashamed Sidon, for the sea saith, the strength of the sea, saying: I have not travailed, and I have not brought forth, and I have not nourished young men, nor brought virgins to their growth.

text.NRSV: When the report comes to Egypt, they will be in anguish over the report about Tyre.
text.Rotherham: Like the report of Egypt, They shall be in pangs at the like report of Tyre.
text.KJV: As at the report concerning Egypt, so shall they be sorely pained at the report of Tyre.
