text.NABRE: The highways are desolate, travelers have quit the paths, Covenants are broken, witnesses spurned; yet no one gives it a thought.
text.DRB: The ways are dissipated, the passenger by the path hath ceased, the covenant is made frustrate, he hath rejected the cities, he hath not esteemed the men.

text.NRSV: The land mourns and languishes; Lebanon is confounded and withers away; Sharon is like a desert; and Bashan and Carmel shake off their leaves.
text.Rotherham: The land mourneth, languisheth, Lebanon, displayeth shame, is withered,—Sharon, hath become, as the waste plain, And Bashan and Carmel are shaking off their leaves.
text.KJV: The earth mourneth and languisheth: Lebanon is ashamed and hewn down: Sharon is like a wilderness; and Bashan and Carmel shake off their fruits.
text.NABRE: The country languishes in mourning, Lebanon withers with shame; Sharon is like the Arabah, Bashan and Carmel are stripped bare.
text.DRB: The land hath mourned, & languished: Libanus is confounded, & become foul, and Saron is made as a desert: and Basan is shaken, & Carmel.

text.NRSV: “Now I will arise,” says the Lord, “now I will lift myself up; now I will be exalted.
text.Rotherham: Now, will I arise, Saith Yahweh, Now, will I lift myself up, Now, will I be exalted!
text.KJV: Now will I rise, saith the Lord; now will I be exalted; now will I lift up myself.
text.NABRE: Now I will rise up, says the Lord, now exalt myself, now lift myself up.
text.DRB: Now will I rise up, saith our Lord: now will I be exalted, now will I be lifted up.

text.NRSV: You conceive chaff, you bring forth stubble; your breath is a fire that consumes you.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall conceive chaff Ye shall bring forth stubble,—Your own breath, like fire, shall devour you.
text.KJV: Ye shall conceive chaff, ye shall bring forth stubble: your breath, as fire, shall devour you.
text.NABRE: You conceive dry grass, bring forth stubble; my spirit shall consume you like fire.
text.DRB: You shall conceive heat, you shall bring forth stubble: your spirit as fire shall devour you.

text.NRSV: And the peoples will be as if burned to lime, like thorns cut down, that are burned in the fire.”
text.Rotherham: So shall peoples, become, as the burnings of lime,—As thorns lopped off, with fire, shall they be burned.
text.KJV: And the people shall be as the burnings of lime: as thorns cut up shall they be burned in the fire.
text.NABRE: The peoples shall be burned to lime, thorns cut down to burn in fire.
text.DRB: And the peoples shall be as ashes of a great fire, thorns gathered together shall be burned with fire.

text.NRSV: Hear, you who are far away, what I have done; and you who are near, acknowledge my might.
text.Rotherham: Hear—ye that are far off. what I have done, And know—ye that are near my might:
text.KJV: Hear, ye that are far off, what I have done; and, ye that are near, acknowledge my might.
text.NABRE: Hear, you who are far off, what I have done; you who are near, acknowledge my might.
text.DRB: Hear ye that are far off, what things I have done, and ye that are near, know my strength.

text.NRSV: The sinners in Zion are afraid; trembling has seized the godless: “Who among us can live with the devouring fire? Who among us can live with everlasting burnings?”
text.Rotherham: Terror-stricken in Zion,—are sinners, Shuddering hath seized the impious,—Who among us can sojourn with a fire that devoureth? Who among us can sojourn with burnings age-abiding?
text.KJV: The sinners in Zion are afraid; fearfulness hath surprised the hypocrites. Who among us shall dwell with the devouring fire? who among us shall dwell with everlasting burnings?
text.NABRE: In Zion sinners are in dread, trembling grips the impious: “Who of us can live with consuming fire? who of us can live with everlasting flames?”
text.DRB: The sinners are terrified in Sion, trembling hath possessed the hypocrites. Which of you can dwell with devouring fire? Which of you shall dwell with everlasting heats?

text.NRSV: Those who walk righteously and speak uprightly, who despise the gain of oppressions, who wave their hands and do not take a bribe, who stop their ears from hearing of bloodshed and shut their eyes from looking on evil,
text.Rotherham: He that walketh righteously, And speaketh uprightly,—He that refuseth the gain of exactions That shaketh his hands free from holding a bribe, That stoppeth his ear from hearkening to deeds of blood, And shutteth his eyes from giving countenance to wrong,
text.KJV: He that walketh righteously, and speaketh uprightly; he that despiseth the gain of oppressions, that shaketh his hands from holding of bribes, that stoppeth his ears from hearing of blood, and shutteth his eyes from seeing evil;
text.NABRE: Whoever walks righteously and speaks honestly, who spurns what is gained by oppression, Who waves off contact with a bribe, who stops his ears so as not to hear of bloodshed, who closes his eyes so as not to look on evil—
text.DRB: He that walketh in justices, and speaketh truth, that casteth away avarice of oppression, and shaketh his hands from all gift, that stoppeth his ears lest he hear blood, and shutteth his eyes that he may see no evil.

text.NRSV: they will live on the heights; their refuge will be the fortresses of rocks; their food will be supplied, their water assured.
text.Rotherham: He, the heights, shall inhabit, A stronghold of crags, shall be his refuge,—His bread, hath been delivered, His waters, have been made sure,
text.KJV: He shall dwell on high: his place of defence shall be the munitions of rocks: bread shall be given him; his waters shall be sure.
text.NABRE: That one shall dwell on the heights, with fortresses of rock for stronghold, food and drink in steady supply.
text.DRB: This man shall dwell on high, the munitions of rocks shall be his highness: bread is given to him, his waters are faithful.

text.NRSV: Your eyes will see the king in his beauty; they will behold a land that stretches far away.
text.Rotherham: Of a king, in his beauty, shall thine eyes have vision: They shall see a land that stretcheth afar.
text.KJV: Thine eyes shall see the king in his beauty: they shall behold the land that is very far off.
text.NABRE: Your eyes will see a king in his splendor, they will look upon a vast land.
text.DRB: His eyes shall see the king in his beauty, they shall see the land far off.

text.NRSV: Your mind will muse on the terror: “Where is the one who counted? Where is the one who weighed the tribute? Where is the one who counted the towers?”
text.Rotherham: Thy heart, may murmur in terror,—Where is the scribe? Where—the receiver? Where—he that maketh a list of the towers?
text.KJV: Thine heart shall meditate terror. Where is the scribe? where is the receiver? where is he that counted the towers?
text.NABRE: Your mind will dwell on the terror: “Where is the one who counted, where the one who weighed? Where the one who counted the towers?”
text.DRB: Thy heart shall meditate fear: where is the learned? Where is he that pondereth the words of the law? Where the teacher of little ones?

text.NRSV: No longer will you see the insolent people, the people of an obscure speech that you cannot comprehend, stammering in a tongue that you cannot understand.
text.Rotherham: The fierce people, shalt thou not see,—The people of too deep a lip to be understood, of too barbarous a tongue for thee to comprehend.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not see a fierce people, a people of a deeper speech than thou canst perceive; of a stammering tongue, that thou canst not understand.
text.NABRE: You shall no longer see a defiant people, a people of speech too obscure to comprehend, stammering in a tongue not understood.
text.DRB: The unwise people thou shalt not see, the people of profound speech: so that thou canst not understand the eloquence of his tongue, in whom there is no wisdom.

text.NRSV: Look on Zion, the city of our appointed festivals! Your eyes will see Jerusalem, a quiet habitation, an immovable tent, whose stakes will never be pulled up, and none of whose ropes will be broken.
text.Rotherham: Look thou on Zion, the city of our appointed feast,—Thine own eyes, shall see Jerusalem—A home of comfort A tent which shall not be packed up—Whose pins shall not be pulled out, for ever, And none of, whose cords, shall be broken.
text.KJV: Look upon Zion, the city of our solemnities: thine eyes shall see Jerusalem a quiet habitation, a tabernacle that shall not be taken down; not one of the stakes thereof shall ever be removed, neither shall any of the cords thereof be broken.
text.NABRE: Look to Zion, the city of our festivals; your eyes shall see Jerusalem as a quiet abode, a tent not to be struck, Whose pegs will never be pulled up, nor any of its ropes severed.
text.DRB: Look upon Sion the city of our solemnity: thine eyes shall see Jerusalem, a rich habitation, a tabernacle that cannot be transferred: neither shall the nails thereof be taken away for ever, and all the cords thereof shall not be broken:

text.NRSV: But there the Lord in majesty will be for us a place of broad rivers and streams, where no galley with oars can go, nor stately ship can pass.
text.Rotherham: But, there shall Yahweh be our majestic one, A place of rivers—streams broad on both hands, Wherein shall go no galley with oars, Neither shall majestic ship traverse it;
text.KJV: But there the glorious Lord will be unto us a place of broad rivers and streams; wherein shall go no galley with oars, neither shall gallant ship pass thereby.
text.NABRE: Indeed the Lord in majesty will be there for us a place of rivers and wide streams on which no galley may go, where no majestic ship may pass.
text.DRB: because only there our Lord is magnifical: a place of floods, rivers most broad and wide: no ship of rowers shall pass by it, neither shall the great galley pass thereby.

text.NRSV: For the Lord is our judge, the Lord is our lawgiver, the Lord is our king; he will save us.
text.Rotherham: For, Yahweh is our judge, Yahweh, is our lawgiver,—Yahweh, is our king, He will save us!
text.KJV: For the Lord is our judge, the Lord is our lawgiver, the Lord is our king; he will save us.
text.NABRE: For the Lord is our judge, the Lord is our lawgiver, the Lord is our king; he it is who will save us.
text.DRB: For the Lord is our judge, the Lord is our law maker, the Lord is our King: he will save us.

text.NRSV: Your rigging hangs loose; it cannot hold the mast firm in its socket, or keep the sail spread out. Then prey and spoil in abundance will be divided; even the lame will take the booty.
text.Rotherham: Loosed are thy ropes,—They cannot strengthen the socket of their mast They have not unfurled a sail Now, can be apportioned spoil, in abundance, The lame, have captured prey!
text.KJV: Thy tacklings are loosed; they could not well strengthen their mast, they could not spread the sail: then is the prey of a great spoil divided; the lame take the prey.
text.NABRE: The rigging hangs slack; it cannot hold the mast in place, nor keep the sail spread out. Then the blind will divide great spoils and the lame will carry off the loot.
text.DRB: Thy cords are loosed, and they shall not prevail: thy mast shall be so, that thou canst not spread the sign. Then shall the spoils of many preys be divided: the lame shall take the spoil.

text.NRSV: And no inhabitant will say, “I am sick”; the people who live there will be forgiven their iniquity.
text.Rotherham: Neither shall the inhabitant say. I am sick,—the people who dwell therein, have been forgiven iniquity.
text.KJV: And the inhabitant shall not say, I am sick: the people that dwell therein shall be forgiven their iniquity.
text.NABRE: No one who dwells there will say, “I am sick”; the people who live there will be forgiven their guilt.
text.DRB: Neither shall the neighbour say: I am feeble. The people that dwelleth therein, iniquity shall be taken away from them.

text.NRSV: Draw near, O nations, to hear; O peoples, give heed! Let the earth hear, and all that fills it; the world, and all that comes from it.
text.Rotherham: Come near ye nations to hear, And, ye races, attend,—Let the earth hear, and the fulness thereof, The world and all things produced therefrom:
text.KJV: Come near, ye nations, to hear; and hearken, ye people: let the earth hear, and all that is therein; the world, and all things that come forth of it.
text.NABRE: F. The Lord, Zion’s Avenger Chapter 34 Judgment upon Edom Come near, nations, and listen; be attentive, you peoples! Let the earth and what fills it listen, the world and all it produces.
text.DRB: Come near ye Gentiles, and hear, and ye peoples attend: let the earth hear, & the fullness thereof, the round world, and every spring thereof.

text.NRSV: For the Lord is enraged against all the nations, and furious against all their hoards; he has doomed them, has given them over for slaughter.
text.Rotherham: That Yahweh hath wrath against all the nations, and indignation against all their host,—He hath devoted them to destruction He hath delivered them to slaughter;
text.KJV: For the indignation of the Lord is upon all nations, and his fury upon all their armies: he hath utterly destroyed them, he hath delivered them to the slaughter.
text.NABRE: The Lord is angry with all the nations, enraged against all their host; He has placed them under the ban, given them up to slaughter.
text.DRB: Because the indignation of our Lord is upon all Gentiles, and fury upon all their hosts: he hath killed them, & given them into slaughter.

text.NRSV: Their slain shall be cast out, and the stench of their corpses shall rise; the mountains shall flow with their blood.
text.Rotherham: And their slain shall be cast out, And their carcases, the stench of them shall ascend,—And, the mountains, shall melt away with their blood.
text.KJV: Their slain also shall be cast out, and their stink shall come up out of their carcases, and the mountains shall be melted with their blood.
text.NABRE: Their slain shall be cast out, their corpses shall send up a stench; the mountains shall run with their blood,
text.DRB: Their slain shall be cast forth, and out of their carcasses shall rise a stink: the mountains shall melt with their blood.

text.NRSV: All the host of heaven shall rot away, and the skies roll up like a scroll. All their host shall wither like a leaf withering on a vine, or fruit withering on a fig tree.
text.Rotherham: Then shall be dissolved all the host of the heavens, And the heavens shall roll up as a scroll,—Yea, all their host, shall fade—Like the fading and falling of a leaf from a vine, and Like what fadeth and falleth from a fig-tree.
text.KJV: And all the host of heaven shall be dissolved, and the heavens shall be rolled together as a scroll: and all their host shall fall down, as the leaf falleth off from the vine, and as a falling fig from the fig tree.
text.NABRE: All the host of heaven shall rot; the heavens shall be rolled up like a scroll. All their host shall wither away, as the leaf wilts on the vine, or as the fig withers on the tree.
text.DRB: And all the host of the heavens shall melt away, and the heavens shall be folded together as a book: and all their host shall fall away as the leaf falleth from the vine, & from the fig tree.

text.NRSV: When my sword has drunk its fill in the heavens, see, it descends upon Edom, upon the people I have doomed to judgment.
text.Rotherham: For my sword hath been sated in the heavens,—Lo! upon Edom, shall it descend, Even on the people whom I have devoted to justice.
text.KJV: For my sword shall be bathed in heaven: behold, it shall come down upon Idumea, and upon the people of my curse, to judgment.
text.NABRE: When my sword has drunk its fill in the heavens, it shall come down upon Edom for judgment, upon a people under my ban.
text.DRB: Because my sword is inebriated in heaven: behold it shall descend upon Idumæa, and upon the peoples of my slaughter to judgement.

text.NRSV: The Lord has a sword; it is sated with blood, it is gorged with fat, with the blood of lambs and goats, with the fat of the kidneys of rams. For the Lord has a sacrifice in Bozrah, a great slaughter in the land of Edom.
text.Rotherham: A sword, hath Yahweh—Glutted with blood Sated with fat,—With the blood of well-fed lambs and he-goats, With the fat of the kidneys of rams,—For, a sacrifice, hath Yahweh in Bozrah, Yea a great slaughter in the land of Edom;
text.KJV: The sword of the Lord is filled with blood, it is made fat with fatness, and with the blood of lambs and goats, with the fat of the kidneys of rams: for the Lord hath a sacrifice in Bozrah, and a great slaughter in the land of Idumea.
text.NABRE: The Lord has a sword sated with blood, greasy with fat, With the blood of lambs and goats, with the fat of rams’ kidneys; For the Lord has a sacrifice in Bozrah, a great slaughter in the land of Edom.
text.DRB: The sword of our Lord is filled with blood, it is fatted of the blood of lambs and buckgoats, of the blood of rams full of marrow: for the victim of our Lord is in Bosra, & a great slaughter in the land of Edom.

text.NRSV: Wild oxen shall fall with them, and young steers with the mighty bulls. Their land shall be soaked with blood, and their soil made rich with fat.
text.Rotherham: Then shall buffaloes come down with them, And bullocks with bulls,—So shall their land be soaked with blood, And, their dust, with fat, shall be enriched.
text.KJV: And the unicorns shall come down with them, and the bullocks with the bulls; and their land shall be soaked with blood, and their dust made fat with fatness.
text.NABRE: Wild oxen shall be struck down with fatlings, and bullocks with bulls; Their land shall be soaked with blood, and their soil greasy with fat.
text.DRB: And the unicorns shall come down with them, & the bulls with the mighty: their land shall be drunk with blood, & their ground with the fatness of fat ones.

text.NRSV: For the Lord has a day of vengeance, a year of vindication by Zion’s cause.
text.Rotherham: For a day of avenging, hath Yahweh,—A year of requitals for the quarrel of Zion.
text.KJV: For it is the day of the Lord’s vengeance, and the year of recompences for the controversy of Zion.
text.NABRE: For the Lord has a day of vengeance, a year of requital for the cause of Zion.
text.DRB: Because it is the day of the revenge of our Lord, the year of retributions of the judgement of Sion.

text.NRSV: And the streams of Edom shall be turned into pitch, and her soil into sulfur; her land shall become burning pitch.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the torrents thereof be turned into pitch, And the dust thereof, into brimstone,—So shall her land become burning pitch:
text.KJV: And the streams thereof shall be turned into pitch, and the dust thereof into brimstone, and the land thereof shall become burning pitch.
text.NABRE: Edom’s streams shall be changed into pitch, its soil into sulfur, and its land shall become burning pitch;
text.DRB: And the torrents thereof shall be turned into pitch, and the ground thereof into brimstone: and the land thereof shall be into burning pitch.

text.NRSV: Night and day it shall not be quenched; its smoke shall go up forever. From generation to generation it shall lie waste; no one shall pass through it forever and ever.
text.Rotherham: Neither night nor day, shall it be quenched, To times age-abiding, shall ascend the smoke thereof,—From generation to generation, shall it be waste, Never, never, shall any pass through it:
text.KJV: It shall not be quenched night nor day; the smoke thereof shall go up for ever: from generation to generation it shall lie waste; none shall pass through it for ever and ever.
text.NABRE: Night and day it shall not be quenched, its smoke shall rise forever. From generation to generation it shall lie waste, never again shall anyone pass through it.
text.DRB: Night and day it shall not be quenched, the smoke thereof shall go up for ever: from generation unto generation it shall be desolate, there shall none pass by it world without end.

text.NRSV: But the hawk and the hedgehog shall possess it; the owl and the raven shall live in it. He shall stretch out over it the line of confusion, and the plummet of chaos over its nobles.
text.Rotherham: That the vomiting pelican and the bittern may possess it: And the great owl and the raven, dwell therein; Then will he stretch out over it The line of desolation, and The plummet of emptiness.
text.KJV: But the cormorant and the bittern shall possess it; the owl also and the raven shall dwell in it: and he shall stretch out upon it the line of confusion, and the stones of emptiness.
text.NABRE: But the desert owl and hoot owl shall possess it, the screech owl and raven shall dwell in it. The Lord will stretch over it the measuring line of chaos, the plumb line of confusion.
text.DRB: The onocrotalus, and hedgehog shall possess it: & the ibis, and the raven shall dwell in it: & a measure shall be stretched out upon it, to bring it to nothing, and a plum line unto desolation.

text.NRSV: They shall name it No Kingdom There, and all its princes shall be nothing.
text.Rotherham: Her nobles (but none, are, there)! unto royalty, will call,—All, all her princes, shall become nought.
text.KJV: They shall call the nobles thereof to the kingdom, but none shall be there, and all her princes shall be nothing.
text.NABRE: Its nobles shall be no more, nor shall kings be proclaimed there; all its princes are gone.
text.DRB: The nobles thereof shall not be there: they shall call rather upon the king, and all the princes thereof shall be as nothing.

text.NRSV: Thorns shall grow over its strongholds, nettles and thistles in its fortresses. It shall be the haunt of jackals, an abode for ostriches.
text.Rotherham: Then shall come up, in her palaces thorns Nettles and thistles in her fortresses,—And she shall become A home for wild dogs, An enclosure for ostriches;
text.KJV: And thorns shall come up in her palaces, nettles and brambles in the fortresses thereof: and it shall be an habitation of dragons, and a court for owls.
text.NABRE: Its castles shall be overgrown with thorns, its fortresses with thistles and briers. It shall become an abode for jackals, a haunt for ostriches.
text.DRB: And thorns and nettles shall grow up in the houses thereof, & the thistles in the munitions thereof: and it shall be the couch of dragons, and the pasture of ostriches.

text.NRSV: Wildcats shall meet with hyenas, goat-demons shall call to each other; there too Lilith shall repose, and find a resting place for herself.
text.Rotherham: Then shall criers meet with howlers, And, the shaggy creature, unto his fellow, shall call,—Only, there, shall, the night-spectre, Make her settlement, And find for herself a place of rest:
text.KJV: The wild beasts of the desert shall also meet with the wild beasts of the island, and the satyr shall cry to his fellow; the screech owl also shall rest there, and find for herself a place of rest.
text.NABRE: Wildcats shall meet with desert beasts, satyrs shall call to one another; There shall the lilith repose, and find for herself a place to rest.
text.DRB: And spirits shall meet the onocentaurus, and the satyr shall cry one to the other, there hath the lamia lien, and found herself rest.

text.NRSV: There shall the owl nest and lay and hatch and gather her young in her shadow; there too the buzzards shall gather, each one with its mate.
text.Rotherham: There, shall, the arrow-snake, Make her nest and lay, And hatch and gather under her shadow,—Only, there, shall be gathered the falcons, every one with her mate.
text.KJV: There shall the great owl make her nest, and lay, and hatch, and gather under her shadow: there shall the vultures also be gathered, every one with her mate.
text.NABRE: There the hoot owl shall nest and lay eggs, hatch them out and gather them in her shadow; There shall the kites assemble, each with its mate.
text.DRB: There hath the hedgehog had an hole, and brought up whelps, and digged round about, and cherished them in the shadow thereof: thither are the kites gathered together, one to another.

text.NRSV: Seek and read from the book of the Lord: Not one of these shall be missing; none shall be without its mate. For the mouth of the Lord has commanded, and his spirit has gathered them.
text.Rotherham: Seek ye out of the scroll of Yahweh, and read, Not, one from among them, is lacking, None, hath missed, her mate,—For, a mouth, hath, itself commanded, And, his spirit, hath itself gathered them:
text.KJV: Seek ye out of the book of the Lord, and read: no one of these shall fail, none shall want her mate: for my mouth it hath commanded, and his spirit it hath gathered them.
text.NABRE: Search through the book of the Lord and read: not one of these shall be lacking, For the mouth of the Lord has ordered it, and his spirit gathers them there.
text.DRB: Search ye diligently in the book of our Lord, and read: one of them hath not wanted, one hath not sought for the other, because that which proceedeth out of my mouth, he hath commanded, and his spirit the same hath gathered them.

text.NRSV: He has cast the lot for them, his hand has apportioned it to them with the line; they shall possess it forever, from generation to generation they shall live in it.
text.Rotherham: Yea he himself, hath cast for them a lot, And his own hand, hath given to them a portion by line,—Unto times age-abiding, shall they possess it, To generation after generation, shall they dwell therein.
text.KJV: And he hath cast the lot for them, and his hand hath divided it unto them by line: they shall possess it for ever, from generation to generation shall they dwell therein.
text.NABRE: It is he who casts the lot for them; his hand measures off their portions; They shall possess it forever, and dwell in it from generation to generation.
text.DRB: And he hath cast them a lot, and his hand hath divided it unto them by measure: they shall possess it for ever, in generation and generation they shall dwell therein.

text.NRSV: The wilderness and the dry land shall be glad, the desert shall rejoice and blossom; like the crocus
text.Rotherham: Wilderness and parched land, shall be glad for them,—And the waste plain, shall exult, and blossom as the lily:
text.KJV: The wilderness and the solitary place shall be glad for them; and the desert shall rejoice, and blossom as the rose.
text.NABRE: Chapter 35 Israel’s Deliverance The wilderness and the parched land will exult; the Arabah will rejoice and bloom;
text.DRB: The desert and the land without passage shall be glad, and the wilderness shall rejoice, & shall flourish as the lily.

text.NRSV: it shall blossom abundantly, and rejoice with joy and singing. The glory of Lebanon shall be given to it, the majesty of Carmel and Sharon. They shall see the glory of the Lord, the majesty of our God.
text.Rotherham: It shall, blossom abundantly, and exult, Yea with exultation and shouts of triumph, The glory of Lebanon, hath been given to it, The splendour of Carmel, and Sharon,—They, shall see the glory of Yahweh, the splendour of our God.
text.KJV: It shall blossom abundantly, and rejoice even with joy and singing: the glory of Lebanon shall be given unto it, the excellency of Carmel and Sharon, they shall see the glory of the Lord, and the excellency of our God.
text.NABRE: Like the crocus it shall bloom abundantly, and rejoice with joyful song. The glory of Lebanon will be given to it, the splendor of Carmel and Sharon; They will see the glory of the Lord, the splendor of our God.
text.DRB: Springing it shall spring, & shall rejoice joyful and praising: the glory of Libanus is given to it, the beauty of Carmel, & Saron, they shall see the glory of our Lord, and the beauty of our God.

text.NRSV: Strengthen the weak hands, and make firm the feeble knees.
text.Rotherham: Strengthen ye the weak hands,—The trembling knees, make ye firm:
text.KJV: Strengthen ye the weak hands, and confirm the feeble knees.
text.NABRE: Strengthen hands that are feeble, make firm knees that are weak,
text.DRB: Encourage ye the loose hands, and strengthen the weak knees.

text.NRSV: Say to those who are of a fearful heart, “Be strong, do not fear! Here is your God. He will come with vengeance, with terrible recompense. He will come and save you.”
text.Rotherham: Say to the hurried in heart, Be strong, Do not fear,—Lo! your God, with avenging, doth come, With the recompence of God, He, doth come to save you.
text.KJV: Say to them that are of a fearful heart, Be strong, fear not: behold, your God will come with vengeance, even God with a recompence; he will come and save you.
text.NABRE: Say to the fearful of heart: Be strong, do not fear! Here is your God, he comes with vindication; With divine recompense he comes to save you.
text.DRB: Say to the faint hearted: Take courage, and fear not: behold your God shall bring revenge of retribution: God himself will come and will save you.

text.NRSV: Then the eyes of the blind shall be opened, and the ears of the deaf unstopped;
text.Rotherham: Then, shall be opened the eyes of the blind,—And, the ears of the deaf, be unstopped:
text.KJV: Then the eyes of the blind shall be opened, and the ears of the deaf shall be unstopped.
text.NABRE: Then the eyes of the blind shall see, and the ears of the deaf be opened;
text.DRB: Then shall the eyes of the blind be opened, and the ears of the deaf shall be open.

text.NRSV: then the lame shall leap like a deer, and the tongue of the speechless sing for joy. For waters shall break forth in the wilderness, and streams in the desert;
text.Rotherham: Then, shall leap as a hart the lame, Then shall shout the tongue of the dumb, For, there have broken forth—In the desert—waters, And streams, in the waste plain:
text.KJV: Then shall the lame man leap as an hart, and the tongue of the dumb sing: for in the wilderness shall waters break out, and streams in the desert.
text.NABRE: Then the lame shall leap like a stag, and the mute tongue sing for joy. For waters will burst forth in the wilderness, and streams in the Arabah.
text.DRB: Then shall the lame leap as an hart, and the tongue of the dumb shall be opened: because waters are cut out in the desert, and torrents in the wilderness.

text.NRSV: the burning sand shall become a pool, and the thirsty ground springs of water; the haunt of jackals shall become a swamp, the grass shall become reeds and rushes.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the glowing sand, become a lake, And thirsty ground—springs of water,—In the home of the wild dog—its lair, Shall be an enclosure for cane and paper—reed.
text.KJV: And the parched ground shall become a pool, and the thirsty land springs of water: in the habitation of dragons, where each lay, shall be grass with reeds and rushes.
text.NABRE: The burning sands will become pools, and the thirsty ground, springs of water; The abode where jackals crouch will be a marsh for the reed and papyrus.
text.DRB: And that which was dry land, shall be as a pool, and the thirsty ground as fountains of waters. In the dens wherein dragons dwelt before, shall spring up the greenness of reed and bulrush.

text.NRSV: A highway shall be there, and it shall be called the Holy Way; the unclean shall not travel on it, but it shall be for God’s people; no traveler, not even fools, shall go astray.
text.Rotherham: And there shall be there, a raised way—even a high road, And the Highroad of Holiness, shall it be called, There shall not pass over it one who is unclean; But He Himself, shall be one of them travelling the road, And the perverse, shall not stray [thereinto].
text.KJV: And an highway shall be there, and a way, and it shall be called The way of holiness; the unclean shall not pass over it; but it shall be for those: the wayfaring men, though fools, shall not err therein.
text.NABRE: A highway will be there, called the holy way; No one unclean may pass over it, but it will be for his people; no traveler, not even fools, shall go astray on it.
text.DRB: And a path and a way shall be there, and it shall be called the holy way: the polluted shall not pass by it, and this shall be unto you a direct way, so that fools cannot err by it.

text.NRSV: No lion shall be there, nor shall any ravenous beast come up on it; they shall not be found there, but the redeemed shall walk there.
text.Rotherham: There shall be, there, no lion, Nor shall ravenous beast, go up thereon, It shall not be found, there,—Thus, shall travel the redeemed;
text.KJV: No lion shall be there, nor any ravenous beast shall go up thereon, it shall not be found there; but the redeemed shall walk there:
text.NABRE: No lion shall be there, nor any beast of prey approach, nor be found. But there the redeemed shall walk,
text.DRB: The lion shall not be there, and the naughty beast shall not go up by it, nor be found there: and they shall walk that shall be delivered.

text.NRSV: And the ransomed of the Lord shall return, and come to Zion with singing; everlasting joy shall be upon their heads; they shall obtain joy and gladness, and sorrow and sighing shall flee away.
text.Rotherham: And the ransomed of Yahweh! shall return, And shall enter Zion with shouting, With gladness age-abiding, upon their head, Joy and gladness shall overtake them, And sorrow and sighing, shall flee away.
text.KJV: And the ransomed of the Lord shall return, and come to Zion with songs and everlasting joy upon their heads: they shall obtain joy and gladness, and sorrow and sighing shall flee away.
text.NABRE: And the ransomed of the Lord shall return, and enter Zion singing, crowned with everlasting joy; They meet with joy and gladness, sorrow and mourning flee away.
text.DRB: And the redeemed of our Lord shall be converted, and shall come into Sion with praise, and everlasting joy shall be upon their head: they shall obtain joy and gladness, and sorrow and mourning shall fly away.

text.NRSV: In the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah, King Sennacherib of Assyria came up against all the fortified cities of Judah and took them.
text.Rotherham: Now it came to pass in the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah, that Sennacherib king of Assyria came up, against all the fortified cities of Judah and took them.
text.KJV: Now it came to pass in the fourteenth year of king Hezekiah, that Sennacherib king of Assyria came up against all the defenced cities of Judah, and took them.
text.NABRE: G. Historical Appendix Chapter 36 Invasion of Sennacherib. In the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah, Sennacherib, king of Assyria, went up against all the fortified cities of Judah and captured them.
text.DRB: And it came to pass in the fourteenth year of king Ezechias, Sennacherib king of the Assyrians came up against all the fenced cities of Juda, and took them.

text.NRSV: The king of Assyria sent the Rabshakeh from Lachish to King Hezekiah at Jerusalem, with a great army. He stood by the conduit of the upper pool on the highway to the Fuller’s Field.
text.Rotherham: Then did the king of Assyria send Rabshakeh from Lachish to Jerusalem unto King Hezekiah with a heavy force,—and he took his stand by the upper channel of the pool, in the highway of the fuller’s field.
text.KJV: And the king of Assyria sent Rabshakeh from Lachish to Jerusalem unto king Hezekiah with a great army. And he stood by the conduit of the upper pool in the highway of the fuller’s field.
text.NABRE: From Lachish the king of Assyria sent his commander with a great army to King Hezekiah in Jerusalem. When he stopped at the conduit of the upper pool, on the highway of the fuller’s field,
text.DRB: And the king of the Assyrians sent Rabsaces from Lachis to Jerusalem, to king Ezechias with a great army, and he stood by the water conduit of the upper pool in the way of the fuller's field.

text.NRSV: And there came out to him Eliakim son of Hilkiah, who was in charge of the palace, and Shebna the secretary, and Joah son of Asaph, the recorder.
text.Rotherham: And there went out to him—Eliakim son of Hilkiah who was over the household,—and Shebna, the scribe, and Joah son of Asaph the recorder.
text.KJV: Then came forth unto him Eliakim, Hilkiah’s son, which was over the house, and Shebna the scribe, and Joah, Asaph’s son, the recorder.
text.NABRE: there came out to him the master of the palace, Eliakim, son of Hilkiah, and Shebna the scribe, and the chancellor, Joah, son of Asaph.
text.DRB: And there came out to him Eliacim the son of Helcias, who was over the house, and Sobna the scribe, and Joahe the son of Asaph the commenter.

text.NRSV: The Rabshakeh said to them, “Say to Hezekiah: Thus says the great king, the king of Assyria: On what do you base this confidence of yours?
text.Rotherham: And Rabshakeh said unto them, Pray you say unto Hezekiah,—Thus, saith the great king, the king of Assyria, What is this trust, wherewith thou dost trust?
text.KJV: And Rabshakeh said unto them, Say ye now to Hezekiah, Thus saith the great king, the king of Assyria, What confidence is this wherein thou trustest?
text.NABRE: The commander said to them, “Tell Hezekiah: Thus says the great king, the king of Assyria: On what do you base this trust of yours?
text.DRB: And Rabsaces said to them: Tell Ezechias: Thus saith the great king, the king of the Assyrians: What is this confidence, whereupon thou dost trust?

text.NRSV: Do you think that mere words are strategy and power for war? On whom do you now rely, that you have rebelled against me?
text.Rotherham: I have said [sayest thou] —they are only words of the lips—Counsel and might have I for the war,—Now, upon whom dost thou trust, that thou hast rebelled against me?
text.KJV: I say, sayest thou, (but they are but vain words) I have counsel and strength for war: now on whom dost thou trust, that thou rebellest against me?
text.NABRE: Do you think mere words substitute for strategy and might in war? In whom, then, do you place your trust, that you rebel against me?
text.DRB: or by what counsel or force art thou disposed to rebel? Upon whom hast thou confidence, that thou art revolted from me?

text.NRSV: See, you are relying on Egypt, that broken reed of a staff, which will pierce the hand of anyone who leans on it. Such is Pharaoh king of Egypt to all who rely on him.
text.Rotherham: Lo! thou dost trust on the support of this bruised cane, on Egypt, whereon if a man lean it will enter his hand and lay it open,—So, is Pharaoh king of Egypt to all who trust upon him.
text.KJV: Lo, thou trustest in the staff of this broken reed, on Egypt; whereon if a man lean, it will go into his hand, and pierce it: so is Pharaoh king of Egypt to all that trust in him.
text.NABRE: Do you trust in Egypt, that broken reed of a staff which pierces the hand of anyone who leans on it? That is what Pharaoh, king of Egypt, is to all who trust in him.
text.DRB: Lo thou dost trust upon this broken staff of reed, upon Ægypt: upon which if a man lean, it will enter into his hand, and pierce it: so is Pharao the king of Ægypt to all that trust in him.

text.NRSV: But if you say to me, ‘We rely on the Lord our God,’ is it not he whose high places and altars Hezekiah has removed, saying to Judah and to Jerusalem, ‘You shall worship before this altar’?
text.Rotherham: But, if thou shouldst say unto me, In Yahweh our God, do we trust, Then is that not he whose high places and whose altars, Hezekiah hath removed, and said unto Judah and unto Jerusalem, Before this altar, shall ye bow yourselves down?
text.KJV: But if thou say to me, We trust in the Lord our God: is it not he, whose high places and whose altars Hezekiah hath taken away, and said to Judah and to Jerusalem, Ye shall worship before this altar?
text.NABRE: Or do you say to me: It is in the Lord, our God, we trust? Is it not he whose high places and altars Hezekiah has removed, commanding Judah and Jerusalem, ‘Worship before this altar’?
text.DRB: But if thou wilt answer me: We trust in our Lord God: is it not he whose excelses and altars Ezechias hath taken away, and he said to Juda and Jerusalem: Before this altar shall you adore?

text.NRSV: Come now, make a wager with my master the king of Assyria: I will give you two thousand horses, if you are able on your part to set riders on them.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, pledge thyself I pray thee with my lord, the king of Assyria,—That I supply thee with two thousand horses, If thou on thy part be able to set riders upon them;
text.KJV: Now therefore give pledges, I pray thee, to my master the king of Assyria, and I will give thee two thousand horses, if thou be able on thy part to set riders upon them.
text.NABRE: “Now, make a wager with my lord, the king of Assyria: I will give you two thousand horses, if you are able to put riders on them.
text.DRB: And now deliver thyself to my lord the king of the Assyrians, and I will give thee two thousand horses, neither shall thou be able of thyself to give riders for them.

text.NRSV: How then can you repulse a single captain among the least of my master’s servants, when you rely on Egypt for chariots and for horsemen?
text.Rotherham: How then wilt thou turn away the face of one pasha of the least of my lord’s servants? Or hast thou on thy part trusted upon Egypt, for chariots and for horsemen?
text.KJV: How then wilt thou turn away the face of one captain of the least of my master’s servants, and put thy trust on Egypt for chariots and for horsemen?
text.NABRE: How then can you turn back even a captain, one of the least servants of my lord, trusting, as you do, in Egypt for chariots and horses?
text.DRB: And how wilt thou abide the face of the judge of one place, of the lesser servants of my lord? But if thou trust in Ægypt, in chariots and in horsemen:

text.NRSV: Moreover, is it without the Lord that I have come up against this land to destroy it? The Lord said to me, Go up against this land, and destroy it.”
text.Rotherham: But, now, is it, without Yahweh, that I have come up against this land to destroy it? Yahweh himself, said unto me, Go thou up against this land and destroy it!
text.KJV: And am I now come up without the Lord against this land to destroy it? the Lord said unto me, Go up against this land, and destroy it.
text.NABRE: Did I come up to destroy this land without the Lord? The Lord himself said to me, Go up and destroy that land!”
text.DRB: and now am I come up without the Lord unto this land to destroy it? The Lord saith to me: Go up against this land, and destroy it.

text.NRSV: Then Eliakim, Shebna, and Joah said to the Rabshakeh, “Please speak to your servants in Aramaic, for we understand it; do not speak to us in the language of Judah within the hearing of the people who are on the wall.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Eliakim, and Shebna, and Joah, unto Rabshakeh—Speak we pray thee unto thy servants in the Syrian language, for we, can, understand, it,—and do not speak unto us in the Jews’ language, in the ears of the people who are upon the wall.
text.KJV: Then said Eliakim and Shebna and Joah unto Rabshakeh, Speak, I pray thee, unto thy servants in the Syrian language; for we understand it: and speak not to us in the Jews’ language, in the ears of the people that are on the wall.
text.NABRE: Then Eliakim and Shebna and Joah said to the commander, “Please speak to your servants in Aramaic; we understand it. Do not speak to us in the language of Judah within earshot of the people who are on the wall.”
text.DRB: And Eliacim, and Sobna, and Joahe said to Rabsaces: Speak to thy servants in the Syrian tongue: for we understand it: speak not to us in the Jews' language in the ears of the people, that is upon the wall.

text.NRSV: But the Rabshakeh said, “Has my master sent me to speak these words to your master and to you, and not to the people sitting on the wall, who are doomed with you to eat their own dung and drink their own urine?”
text.Rotherham: But Rabshakeh said—Is it unto thy lord and unto thee, that my lord hath sent me, to speak these things? Is it not concerning the men who are tarrying upon the wall, that they may eat and drink what cometh from them, with you?
text.KJV: But Rabshakeh said, Hath my master sent me to thy master and to thee to speak these words? hath he not sent me to the men that sit upon the wall, that they may eat their own dung, and drink their own piss with you?
text.NABRE: But the commander replied, “Was it to your lord and to you that my lord sent me to speak these words? Was it not rather to those sitting on the wall, who, with you, will have to eat their own excrement and drink their own urine?”
text.DRB: And Rabsaces said to them: Why, did my lord send me to thy lord and to thee, to speak all these words; and not rather to the men, that sit on the wall; that they may eat their own dung, and drink the urine of their feet with you?

text.NRSV: Then the Rabshakeh stood and called out with a loud voice in the language of Judah, “Hear the words of the great king, the king of Assyria!
text.Rotherham: So then Rabshakeh took his stand, and cried out with a loud voice, in the Jews’ language, and said, Hear ye the words of the great king, the king of Assyria:
text.KJV: Then Rabshakeh stood, and cried with a loud voice in the Jews’ language, and said, Hear ye the words of the great king, the king of Assyria.
text.NABRE: Then the commander stepped forward and cried out in a loud voice in the language of Judah, “Listen to the words of the great king, the king of Assyria.
text.DRB: And Rabsaces stood, and cried with a loud voice in the Jews' language, and said: Hear ye the words of the great king, the king of Assyrians.

text.NRSV: Thus says the king: ‘Do not let Hezekiah deceive you, for he will not be able to deliver you.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith the king, Let not Hezekiah deceive you,—for he shall not be able to deliver you.
text.KJV: Thus saith the king, Let not Hezekiah deceive you: for he shall not be able to deliver you.
text.NABRE: Thus says the king: Do not let Hezekiah deceive you, for he cannot rescue you.
text.DRB: Thus saith the king: Let not Ezechias seduce you, for he shall not be able to deliver you.

text.NRSV: Do not let Hezekiah make you rely on the Lord by saying, The Lord will surely deliver us; this city will not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.’
text.Rotherham: Neither let Hezekiah cause you to trust in Yahweh saying, Yahweh, will certainly deliver, us,—this city shall not be given over, into the hand of the king of Assyria.
text.KJV: Neither let Hezekiah make you trust in the Lord, saying, The Lord will surely deliver us: this city shall not be delivered into the hand of the king of Assyria.
text.NABRE: And do not let Hezekiah induce you to trust in the Lord, saying, ‘The Lord will surely rescue us, and this city will not be handed over to the king of Assyria.’
text.DRB: And let not Ezechias give you confidence upon the Lord, saying: Our Lord delivering will deliver us, this city shall not be given into the hand of the king of Assyrians.

text.NRSV: Do not listen to Hezekiah; for thus says the king of Assyria: ‘Make your peace with me and come out to me; then everyone of you will eat from your own vine and your own fig tree and drink water from your own cistern,
text.Rotherham: Do not hearken unto Hezekiah,—for, thus, saith the king of Assyria, Deal with me thankfully, and come out unto me, Then shall ye eat, Every one of his own vine and Every one of his own fig-tree, And drink every one the Waters of his own cistern:
text.KJV: Hearken not to Hezekiah: for thus saith the king of Assyria, Make an agreement with me by a present, and come out to me: and eat ye every one of his vine, and every one of his fig tree, and drink ye every one the waters of his own cistern;
text.NABRE: Do not listen to Hezekiah, for thus says the king of Assyria: Make peace with me and surrender to me! Eat, each of you, from your vine, each from your own fig tree. Drink water, each from your own well,
text.DRB: Hear not Ezechias: for thus saith the king of Assyrians, do a blessing with me, and come forth to me, and eat ye every man of his own vineyard, and every man of his own figtree, and drink ye every man the water of his own cistern,

text.NRSV: until I come and take you away to a land like your own land, a land of grain and wine, a land of bread and vineyards.
text.Rotherham: Until I come and take you, into A land like your own land,—A land of corn and new wine, A land of bread and vineyards:—
text.KJV: Until I come and take you away to a land like your own land, a land of corn and wine, a land of bread and vineyards.
text.NABRE: until I arrive and take you to a land like your own, A land of grain and wine, a land of bread and vineyards.
text.DRB: till I come and take you away to a land, that is as your own, a land of corn and of wine, a land of bread and vineyards.

text.NRSV: Beware that Hezekiah does not mislead you by saying, The Lord will deliver us. Has any of the gods of the nations delivered their land out of the hand of the king of Assyria?
text.Rotherham: Lest Hezekiah, persuade you, saying, Yahweh will deliver us! Have the gods of the nations, delivered,—any one of them—his country, out of the hand of the king of Assyria?
text.KJV: Beware lest Hezekiah persuade you, saying, The Lord will deliver us. Hath any of the gods of the nations delivered his land out of the hand of the king of Assyria?
text.NABRE: Do not let Hezekiah seduce you by saying, ‘The Lord will rescue us.’ Has any of the gods of the nations rescued his land from the power of the king of Assyria?
text.DRB: Neither let Ezechias trouble you, saying: Our Lord will deliver us. Why, have the gods of the nations delivered every one their land out of the hand of the king of Assyrians?

text.NRSV: Where are the gods of Hamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim? Have they delivered Samaria out of my hand?
text.Rotherham: Where are the gods of Hamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim? If indeed they had only delivered Samaria, out of my hand!
text.KJV: Where are the gods of Hamath and Arphad? where are the gods of Sepharvaim? and have they delivered Samaria out of my hand?
text.NABRE: Where are the gods of Hamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim? Where are the gods of Samaria? Have they saved Samaria from my power?
text.DRB: Where is the god of Emath, and Arphad? Where is the god of Sepharvaim? Have they delivered Samaria out of my hand?

text.NRSV: Who among all the gods of these countries have delivered their countries out of my hand, that the Lord should deliver Jerusalem out of my hand?’ ”
text.Rotherham: Who are they, among all the gods of these countries that have delivered their country out of my hand? That, Yahweh, should deliver, Jerusalem, out of my hand!
text.KJV: Who are they among all the gods of these lands, that have delivered their land out of my hand, that the Lord should deliver Jerusalem out of my hand?
text.NABRE: Who among all the gods of these lands ever rescued their land from my power, that the Lord should save Jerusalem from my power?”
text.DRB: Who is there of all the gods of these lands, which hath delivered their land out of my hand, that the Lord can deliver Jerusalem out of my hand?

text.NRSV: But they were silent and answered him not a word, for the king’s command was, “Do not answer him.”
text.Rotherham: But they held their peace, and answered him not a word,—for the command of the king, it was, saying,—Ye must not answer him.
text.KJV: But they held their peace, and answered him not a word: for the king’s commandment was, saying, Answer him not.
text.NABRE: But they remained silent and did not answer at all, for the king’s command was, “Do not answer him.”
text.DRB: And they held their peace, and answered him not a word. For the king had commanded, saying: Answer him not.

text.NRSV: Then Eliakim son of Hilkiah, who was in charge of the palace, and Shebna the secretary, and Joah son of Asaph, the recorder, came to Hezekiah with their clothes torn, and told him the words of the Rabshakeh.
text.Rotherham: Then came in—Eliakim son of Hilkiah who was over the household, and Shebna the scribe, and Joah son of Asaph, the recorder, unto Hezekiah, with rent clothes,—and they told him the words of Rabshakeh.
text.KJV: Then came Eliakim, the son of Hilkiah, that was over the household, and Shebna the scribe, and Joah, the son of Asaph, the recorder, to Hezekiah with their clothes rent, and told him the words of Rabshakeh.
text.NABRE: Then the master of the palace, Eliakim, son of Hilkiah, Shebna the scribe, and the chancellor Joah, son of Asaph, came to Hezekiah with their garments torn, and reported to him the words of the commander.
text.DRB: And Eliacim the son of Helcias, that was over the house, and Sobna the scribe, and Joahe the son of Asaph the commenter, went in to Ezechias their garments rent, and told him the words of Rabsaces.

text.NRSV: When King Hezekiah heard it, he tore his clothes, covered himself with sackcloth, and went into the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when King Hezekiah heard it, that he rent his clothes,—and covered himself with sackcloth, and entered the house of Yahweh;
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when king Hezekiah heard it, that he rent his clothes, and covered himself with sackcloth, and went into the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: When King Hezekiah heard this, he tore his garments, covered himself with sackcloth, and went into the house of the Lord.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when king Ezechias had heard it, he rent his garments, and was wrapped in sack cloth, and entered into the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: And he sent Eliakim, who was in charge of the palace, and Shebna the secretary, and the senior priests, covered with sackcloth, to the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz.
text.Rotherham: and sent Eliakim who was over the household and Shebna the scribe and the elders of the priests, covered with sackcloth,—unto Isaiah the prophet, Son of Amoz;
text.KJV: And he sent Eliakim, who was over the household, and Shebna the scribe, and the elders of the priests covered with sackcloth, unto Isaiah the prophet the son of Amoz.
text.NABRE: He sent Eliakim, the master of the palace, and Shebna the scribe, and the elders of the priests, covered with sackcloth, to tell the prophet Isaiah, son of Amoz,
text.DRB: And he sent Eliacim which was over the house, and Sobna the scribe and the ancients of the priests covered with sackcloths to Isaie, the son of Amos the Prophet.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Thus says Hezekiah, This day is a day of distress, of rebuke, and of disgrace; children have come to the birth, and there is no strength to bring them forth.
text.Rotherham: and they said unto him, Thus, saith Hezekiah, A day of distress and rebuke and reviling, is this day,—For children are come to the birth, and, strength, is there none to bring forth.
text.KJV: And they said unto him, Thus saith Hezekiah, This day is a day of trouble, and of rebuke, and of blasphemy: for the children are come to the birth, and there is not strength to bring forth.
text.NABRE: “Thus says Hezekiah: A day of distress and rebuke, a day of disgrace is this day! Children are due to come forth, but the strength to give birth is lacking.
text.DRB: and they said to him: Thus saith Ezechias: This day is a day of tribulation and of rebuke, and of blasphemy: because the children are come even to the birth, and there is not strength to bring forth.

text.NRSV: It may be that the Lord your God heard the words of the Rabshakeh, whom his master the king of Assyria has sent to mock the living God, and will rebuke the words that the Lord your God has heard; therefore lift up your prayer for the remnant that is left.”
text.Rotherham: It may be, that Yahweh thy God will hear the words of Rabshakeh whom the king of Assyria his lord, hath sent to reproach a Living God, and will rebuke the words which Yahweh thy God hath heard,—Wherefore lift thou up a prayer, for the remnant that remaineth.
text.KJV: It may be the Lord thy God will hear the words of Rabshakeh, whom the king of Assyria his master hath sent to reproach the living God, and will reprove the words which the Lord thy God hath heard: wherefore lift up thy prayer for the remnant that is left.
text.NABRE: Perhaps the Lord, your God, will hear the words of the commander, whom his lord, the king of Assyria, sent to taunt the living God, and will rebuke him for the words which the Lord, your God, has heard. So lift up a prayer for the remnant that is here.”
text.DRB: If by any means our Lord thy God will hear the words of Rabsaces, whom the king of Assyrians his lord hath sent to blaspheme the living God, and to upbraid with words which our Lord thy God hath heard: lift up prayer therefore for the remnant that are left.

text.NRSV: When the servants of King Hezekiah came to Isaiah,
text.Rotherham: So the servants of King Hezekiah came unto Isaiah.
text.KJV: So the servants of king Hezekiah came to Isaiah.
text.NABRE: When the servants of King Hezekiah had come to Isaiah,
text.DRB: And the servants of Ezechias came to Isaie.

text.NRSV: Isaiah said to them, “Say to your master, ‘Thus says the Lord: Do not be afraid because of the words that you have heard, with which the servants of the king of Assyria have reviled me.
text.Rotherham: And Isaiah said unto them, Thus, shall ye, surely say, unto your lord,—Thus, saith Yahweh—Be not thou afraid because of the words which thou hast heard, wherewith the servants of the king of Assyria have reviled, Me.
text.KJV: And Isaiah said unto them, Thus shall ye say unto your master, Thus saith the Lord, Be not afraid of the words that thou hast heard, wherewith the servants of the king of Assyria have blasphemed me.
text.NABRE: he said to them: “Tell this to your lord: Thus says the Lord: Do not be frightened by the words you have heard, by which the deputies of the king of Assyria have blasphemed me.
text.DRB: And Isaie said to them: Thus shall you say to your master: Thus saith our Lord: Fear not at the face of the words, which thou hast heard, wherewith the servants of the king of the Assyrians have blasphemed me.

text.NRSV: I myself will put a spirit in him, so that he shall hear a rumor, and return to his own land; I will cause him to fall by the sword in his own land.’ ”
text.Rotherham: Behold me! about to let go against him a blast of alarm, and when he heareth the report, then will he return to his own country,—and I will cause him to fall by the sword in his own land.
text.KJV: Behold, I will send a blast upon him, and he shall hear a rumour, and return to his own land; and I will cause him to fall by the sword in his own land.
text.NABRE: I am putting in him such a spirit that when he hears a report he will return to his land. I will make him fall by the sword in his land.”
text.DRB: Behold, I will give him a spirit, and he shall hear a message, and shall return to his country, and I will make him fall by the sword in his own country.

text.NRSV: The Rabshakeh returned, and found the king of Assyria fighting against Libnah; for he had heard that the king had left Lachish.
text.Rotherham: So Rabshakeh returned, and found the king of Assyria, warring against Libnah,—for he had heard, that he had broken up from Lachish.
text.KJV: So Rabshakeh returned, and found the king of Assyria warring against Libnah: for he had heard that he was departed from Lachish.
text.NABRE: When the commander, on his return, heard that the king of Assyria had withdrawn from Lachish, he found him besieging Libnah.
text.DRB: And Rabsaces returned, and found the king of Assyrians fighting against Lobna. For he heard that he was departed from Lachis,

text.NRSV: Now the king heard concerning King Tirhakah of Ethiopia, “He has set out to fight against you.” When he heard it, he sent messengers to Hezekiah, saying,
text.Rotherham: And he heard it reported concerning Tirhakah king of Ethiopia, saying, He hath come forth to fight with thee,—so when he heard it, he sent messengers unto Hezekiah, saying:
text.KJV: And he heard say concerning Tirhakah king of Ethiopia, He is come forth to make war with thee. And when he heard it, he sent messengers to Hezekiah, saying,
text.NABRE: The king of Assyria heard a report: “Tirhakah, king of Ethiopia, has come out to fight against you.” Again he sent messengers to Hezekiah to say:
text.DRB: and he heard of Tharaca the king of Æthiopia, them that said: He is come forth to fight against thee. Which when he had heard, he sent messengers to Ezechias, saying:

text.NRSV: “Thus shall you speak to King Hezekiah of Judah: Do not let your God on whom you rely deceive you by promising that Jerusalem will not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.
text.Rotherham: Thus, shall ye surely speak unto Hezekiah king of Judah, saying, Let not thy God, in whom, thou, art trusting, beguile thee, saying,—Jerusalem, shall not be given over, into the hand of the king of Assyria.
text.KJV: Thus shall ye speak to Hezekiah king of Judah, saying, Let not thy God, in whom thou trustest, deceive thee, saying, Jerusalem shall not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.
text.NABRE: “Thus shall you say to Hezekiah, king of Judah: Do not let your God in whom you trust deceive you by saying, ‘Jerusalem will not be handed over to the king of Assyria.’
text.DRB: Thus shall you say to Ezechias the king of Juda, speaking: Let not thy God deceive thee, in whom thou hast confidence, saying: Jerusalem shall not be given into the hand of the king of the Assyrians.

text.NRSV: See, you have heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all lands, destroying them utterly. And shall you be delivered?
text.Rotherham: Lo! thou, thyself, hast heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all the lands in devoting them to destruction,—and shalt, thou, be delivered?
text.KJV: Behold, thou hast heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all lands by destroying them utterly; and shalt thou be delivered?
text.NABRE: You, certainly, have heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all the lands: they put them under the ban! And are you to be delivered?
text.DRB: Lo thou hast heard all things the kings of the Assyrians have done to all countries, which they have subverted, & canst thou be delivered?

text.NRSV: Have the gods of the nations delivered them, the nations that my predecessors destroyed, Gozan, Haran, Rezeph, and the people of Eden who were in Telassar?
text.Rotherham: Did the gods of the nations, deliver them, whom my fathers destroyed,—Gozan, and Haran,—and Rezeph, and the sons of Eden, who were in Telassar?
text.KJV: Have the gods of the nations delivered them which my fathers have destroyed, as Gozan, and Haran, and Rezeph, and the children of Eden which were in Telassar?
text.NABRE: Did the gods of the nations whom my fathers destroyed deliver them—Gozan, Haran, Rezeph, and the Edenites in Telassar?
text.DRB: Why, have the gods of the nations delivered them, whom my fathers have subverted, Gozam, and Haram, and Reseph, and the children of Eden, that were in Thalassar?

text.NRSV: Where is the king of Hamath, the king of Arpad, the king of the city of Sepharvaim, the king of Hena, or the king of Ivvah?”
text.Rotherham: Where are the king of Hamath, and the king of Arpad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim,—of Hena, and Ivvah?
text.KJV: Where is the king of Hamath, and the king of Arphad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim, Hena, and Ivah?
text.NABRE: Where are the king of Hamath, the king of Arpad, or a king of the cities Sepharvaim, Hena or Ivvah?”
text.DRB: Where is the king of Emath, and the king of Arphad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim, of Ana, & Ava?

text.NRSV: Hezekiah received the letter from the hand of the messengers and read it; then Hezekiah went up to the house of the Lord and spread it before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, when Hezekiah had received the letter at the hand of the messengers, and had read it, then went he up to the house of Yahweh, and Hezekiah spread it out, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Hezekiah received the letter from the hand of the messengers, and read it: and Hezekiah went up unto the house of the Lord, and spread it before the Lord.
text.NABRE: Hezekiah took the letter from the hand of the messengers and read it; then he went up to the house of the Lord, and spreading it out before the Lord,
text.DRB: And Ezechias took the letters of the hand of the messengers, & read them, and went up into the house of our Lord, and Ezechias laid them open before our Lord.

text.NRSV: And Hezekiah prayed to the Lord, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Hezekiah prayed unto Yahweh saying:
text.KJV: And Hezekiah prayed unto the Lord, saying,
text.NABRE: Hezekiah prayed to the Lord:
text.DRB: And Ezechias prayed to our Lord, saying:

text.NRSV: “O Lord of hosts, God of Israel, who are enthroned on the cherubim, you are God, you alone, of all the kingdoms of the earth; you have made heaven and earth.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh of hosts, God of Israel—inhabiting the cherubim, Thou thyself, art GOD, even thou alone, for all the kingdoms of the earth: Thou, didst make the heavens and the earth.
text.KJV: O Lord of hosts, God of Israel, that dwellest between the cherubims, thou art the God, even thou alone, of all the kingdoms of the earth: thou hast made heaven and earth.
text.NABRE: “ Lord of hosts, God of Israel, enthroned on the cherubim! You alone are God over all the kingdoms of the earth. It is you who made the heavens and the earth.
text.DRB: O Lord of hosts God of Israel, which sittest upon the Cherubs, thou art the only God of all the kingdoms of the earth, thou hast made heaven and earth.

text.NRSV: Incline your ear, O Lord, and hear; open your eyes, O Lord, and see; hear all the words of Sennacherib, which he has sent to mock the living God.
text.Rotherham: Bow down, O Yahweh, thine ear—and hear, Open, O Yahweh, thine eyes—and see,—Yea hear thou all the words of Sennacherib, who hath sent—to reproach a Living God.
text.KJV: Incline thine ear, O Lord, and hear; open thine eyes, O Lord, and see: and hear all the words of Sennacherib, which hath sent to reproach the living God.
text.NABRE: Incline your ear, Lord, and listen! open your eyes, Lord, and see! Hear all the words Sennacherib has sent to taunt the living God.
text.DRB: Incline o Lord thine ear, & hear: open o Lord thine eyes, and see, and hear all the words of Sennacherib, which he hath sent to blaspheme the living God.

text.NRSV: Truly, O Lord, the kings of Assyria have laid waste all the nations and their lands,
text.Rotherham: Of a truth, O Yahweh,—the kings of Assyria have devoted to destruction all the countries, and their land;
text.KJV: Of a truth, Lord, the kings of Assyria have laid waste all the nations, and their countries,
text.NABRE: Truly, O Lord, the kings of Assyria have laid waste the nations and their lands.
text.DRB: For in very deed, o Lord, the kings of the Assyrians have made lands desolate, and the countries of the same.

text.NRSV: and have cast their gods into the fire, for they were no gods, but the work of human hands, wood and stone; therefore they were destroyed.
text.Rotherham: and have put their gods in the fire,—for, no-gods, were they, but the work of the hands of men wood and stone and so they destroyed them.
text.KJV: And have cast their gods into the fire: for they were no gods, but the work of men’s hands, wood and stone: therefore they have destroyed them.
text.NABRE: They gave their gods to the fire —they were not gods at all, but the work of human hands— Wood and stone, they destroyed them.
text.DRB: And they have given their gods to fire: for they were not gods, but the works of men's hands, wood and stone: & they brake them in pieces.

text.NRSV: So now, O Lord our God, save us from his hand, so that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that you alone are the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O Yahweh our God, save us, out of his hand,—That all the kingdoms of the earth, may know, That, thou, art Yahweh, thou alone.
text.KJV: Now therefore, O Lord our God, save us from his hand, that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that thou art the Lord, even thou only.
text.NABRE: Therefore, Lord, our God, save us from this man’s power, That all the kingdoms of the earth may know that you alone, Lord, are God.”
text.DRB: And now, o Lord our God, save us out of his hand: and let all the kingdoms of the earth know, that thou only art the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Isaiah son of Amoz sent to Hezekiah, saying: “Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: Because you have prayed to me concerning King Sennacherib of Assyria,
text.Rotherham: Then Isaiah son of Amoz sent unto Hezekiah saying,—Thus, saith Yahweh, God of Israel, In that thou hast prayed unto me concerning Sennacherib king of Assyria,
text.KJV: Then Isaiah the son of Amoz sent unto Hezekiah, saying, Thus saith the Lord God of Israel, Whereas thou hast prayed to me against Sennacherib king of Assyria:
text.NABRE: Then Isaiah, son of Amoz, sent this message to Hezekiah: “Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel, to whom you have prayed concerning Sennacherib, king of Assyria: I have listened!
text.DRB: And Isaie the son of Amos sent to Ezechias, saying: Thus saith our Lord the God of Israel: For the things that thou prayed me concerning Sennacherib the king of Assyrians:

text.NRSV: this is the word that the Lord has spoken concerning him: She despises you, she scorns you— virgin daughter Zion; she tosses her head—behind your back, daughter Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: this, is the word which Yahweh hath spoken concerning him,—The virgin daughter of Zion, laugheth thee to scorn—mocketh thee, The daughter of Jerusalem after thee doth wag her head,
text.KJV: This is the word which the Lord hath spoken concerning him; The virgin, the daughter of Zion, hath despised thee, and laughed thee to scorn; the daughter of Jerusalem hath shaken her head at thee.
text.NABRE: This is the word the Lord has spoken concerning him: She despises you, laughs you to scorn, the virgin daughter Zion; Behind you she wags her head, daughter Jerusalem.
text.DRB: this is the word which our Lord hath spoken upon him: The virgin daughter of Sion hath despised thee, and scorned thee: the daughter of Jerusalem hath wagged the head after thee.

text.NRSV: “Whom have you reviled and blasphemed? Against whom have you raised your voice and haughtily lifted your eyes? Against the Holy One of Israel!
text.Rotherham: Whom, hast thou reproached and insulted? And, against whom, hast thou lifted high thy voice? Yea thou hast proudly raised thine eyes, against the Holy One of Israel.
text.KJV: Whom hast thou reproached and blasphemed? and against whom hast thou exalted thy voice, and lifted up thine eyes on high? even against the Holy One of Israel.
text.NABRE: Whom have you insulted and blasphemed, at whom have you raised your voice And lifted up your eyes on high? At the Holy One of Israel!
text.DRB: Whom hast thou upbraided, and whom hast thou blasphemed, and upon whom hast thou exalted voice, and lifted up the height of thine eyes? To the holy one of Israel.

text.NRSV: By your servants you have mocked the Lord, and you have said, ‘With my many chariots I have gone up the heights of the mountains, to the far recesses of Lebanon; I felled its tallest cedars, its choicest cypresses; I came to its remotest height, its most fruitful forest.
text.Rotherham: Through thy servants, thou hast reproached My Lord and hast said,—With my multitude of chariots, have I ascended The height of the mountains The recesses of Lebanon, That I may cut down Its tallest cedars Its choicest firs, That I may enter Its highest summit, Its thick garden forest:
text.KJV: By thy servants hast thou reproached the Lord, and hast said, By the multitude of my chariots am I come up to the height of the mountains, to the sides of Lebanon; and I will cut down the tall cedars thereof, and the choice fir trees thereof: and I will enter into the height of his border, and the forest of his Carmel.
text.NABRE: Through the mouths of your messengers you have insulted the Lord when you said: ‘With my many chariots I went up to the tops of the peaks, to the recesses of Lebanon, To cut down its lofty cedars, its choice cypresses; I reached the farthest shelter, the forest ranges.
text.DRB: By the hand of thy servants thou hast upbraided our Lord, & hast said: In the multitude of my chariots have I climbed the height of mountains, the tops of Libanus: & I will cut down the high cedars thereof, & the chosen fir trees thereof, and will enter to the top of the height thereof, to the forest of his Carmel.

text.NRSV: I dug wells and drank waters, I dried up with the sole of my foot all the streams of Egypt.’
text.Rotherham: I, have digged, and drunk waters,—That I may dry up, with the soles of my feet, all the Nile-streams of Egypt.
text.KJV: I have digged, and drunk water; and with the sole of my feet have I dried up all the rivers of the besieged places.
text.NABRE: I myself dug wells and drank foreign water; Drying up all the rivers of Egypt beneath the soles of my feet.’
text.DRB: I have digged, and drunken the water, and have dried up with the step of my foot all the rivers of the rampiers.

text.NRSV: “Have you not heard that I determined it long ago? I planned from days of old what now I bring to pass, that you should make fortified cities crash into heaps of ruins,
text.Rotherham: Hast thou not heard—That, long ago, that, is what I appointed, And from days of old, devised? Now, have I brought it to pass, That thou mightest Serve to lay waste, in desolate ruins, fortified cities;
text.KJV: Hast thou not heard long ago, how I have done it; and of ancient times, that I have formed it? now have I brought it to pass, that thou shouldest be to lay waste defenced cities into ruinous heaps.
text.NABRE: Have you not heard? A long time ago I prepared it, from days of old I planned it, Now I have brought it about: You are here to reduce fortified cities to heaps of ruins,
text.DRB: Why, hast thou not heard, what I have done to him of old? From ancient days have I formed it: and now I have brought it to effect: and it is made to the rooting out of little hills bickering together, and of fenced cities.

text.NRSV: while their inhabitants, shorn of strength, are dismayed and confounded; they have become like plants of the field and like tender grass, like grass on the housetops, blighted before it is grown.
text.Rotherham: And their inhabitants, being powerless, were overthrown and put to shame—They became—Grass of the field and Young herbage, Grass on housetops, and Seed parched before it came up.
text.KJV: Therefore their inhabitants were of small power, they were dismayed and confounded: they were as the grass of the field, and as the green herb, as the grass on the housetops, and as corn blasted before it be grown up.
text.NABRE: Their people powerless, dismayed and distraught, They are plants of the field, green growth, thatch on the rooftops, Grain scorched by the east wind.
text.DRB: The inhabitants of them with shortened hand have trembled, and are confounded: they are become as hay of the field, and grass of the pasture ground, and herb of the housetops which hath withered before it was ripe.

text.NRSV: “I know your rising up and your sitting down, your going out and your coming in, and your raging against me.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, thine abode and thy coming out and thy going in, I know,—and thy raging against me.
text.KJV: But I know thy abode, and thy going out, and thy coming in, and thy rage against me.
text.NABRE: I know when you stand or sit, when you come or go, and how you rage against me.
text.DRB: I have known thy habitation, and thy going out, and thy coming in, and thy madness against me.

text.NRSV: Because you have raged against me and your arrogance has come to my ears, I will put my hook in your nose and my bit in your mouth; I will turn you back on the way by which you came.
text.Rotherham: Because, thy raging against me, and thy contempt, have come up into mine ears, Therefore will I put My ring in thy nose, and My bit in thy lips, And will turn thee back, by the way by which thou camest.
text.KJV: Because thy rage against me, and thy tumult, is come up into mine ears, therefore will I put my hook in thy nose, and my bridle in thy lips, and I will turn thee back by the way by which thou camest.
text.NABRE: Because you rage against me and your smugness has reached my ears, I will put my hook in your nose and my bit in your mouth, And make you leave by the way you came.
text.DRB: When thou didst rage against me, thy pride ascended into mine ears: therefore I will put a ring in thy nostrils, and a bit in thy lips, and will bring thee back into the way, by which thou camest.

text.NRSV: “And this shall be the sign for you: This year eat what grows of itself, and in the second year what springs from that; then in the third year sow, reap, plant vineyards, and eat their fruit.
text.Rotherham: And, this unto thee, is the sign, Eating this year, the growth of scattered seeds, And in the second year, that which shooteth up of itself,—Then in the third year, Sow ye—and reap, and Plant ye vineyards, and eat the fruit thereof.
text.KJV: And this shall be a sign unto thee, Ye shall eat this year such as groweth of itself; and the second year that which springeth of the same: and in the third year sow ye, and reap, and plant vineyards, and eat the fruit thereof.
text.NABRE: This shall be a sign for you: This year you shall eat the aftergrowth, next year, what grows of itself; But in the third year, sow and reap, plant vineyards and eat their fruit!
text.DRB: But to thee this shall be a sign: eat this year the things that grow to themselves, and in the second year eat fruits: but in the third year sow & reap, & plant vineyards, and eat the fruit of them.

text.NRSV: The surviving remnant of the house of Judah shall again take root downward, and bear fruit upward;
text.Rotherham: Then shall the escaped of the house of Judah that remain, again, Take root downward,—And bear fruit upward.
text.KJV: And the remnant that is escaped of the house of Judah shall again take root downward, and bear fruit upward:
text.NABRE: The remaining survivors of the house of Judah shall again strike root below and bear fruit above.
text.DRB: And that which shall be saved of the house of Juda, and which is left, shall take root downward, and shall bear fruit upward:

text.NRSV: for from Jerusalem a remnant shall go out, and from Mount Zion a band of survivors. The zeal of the Lord of hosts will do this.
text.Rotherham: For, out of Jerusalem, shall come forth a remnant, And that which hath escaped, out of Mount Zion,—the jealousy of Yahweh of hosts, will perform, this.
text.KJV: For out of Jerusalem shall go forth a remnant, and they that escape out of mount Zion: the zeal of the Lord of hosts shall do this.
text.NABRE: For out of Jerusalem shall come a remnant, and from Mount Zion, survivors. The zeal of the Lord of hosts shall do this.
text.DRB: because out of Jerusalem there shall a remnant go forth, and salvation from mount Sion: the zeal of the Lord of hosts shall do this.

text.NRSV: “Therefore thus says the Lord concerning the king of Assyria: He shall not come into this city, shoot an arrow there, come before it with a shield, or cast up a siegework against it.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, thus, saith Yahweh, concerning the king of Assyria, He shall not enter this city, Nor shoot there, an arrow,—Nor attack it with shield, Nor cast up against it a mound:
