text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord concerning the king of Assyria, He shall not come into this city, nor shoot an arrow there, nor come before it with shields, nor cast a bank against it.
text.NABRE: Therefore, thus says the Lord about the king of Assyria: He shall not come as far as this city, nor shoot there an arrow, nor confront it with a shield, Nor cast up a siege-work against it.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord concerning the king of the Assyrians: He shall not enter this city, and he shall not shoot arrow there, and shield shall not occupy it, and he shall not cast rampier about it:

text.NRSV: By the way that he came, by the same he shall return; he shall not come into this city, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: By the way that he Came in, By the Same, shall he return,—And into this city, shall he not enter, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: By the way that he came, by the same shall he return, and shall not come into this city, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: By the way he came he shall leave, never coming as far as this city, oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: In the way that he came, by the same he shall return, and into this city he shall not enter, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: For I will defend this city to save it, for my own sake and for the sake of my servant David.”
text.Rotherham: Thus will I throw a covering over this city to save it,—For mine own sake, And for the sake of David my servant.
text.KJV: For I will defend this city to save it for mine own sake, and for my servant David’s sake.
text.NABRE: I will shield and save this city for my own sake and the sake of David my servant.”
text.DRB: And I will protect this city, that I may save it for mine own sake, and for David's sake my servant.

text.NRSV: Then the angel of the Lord set out and struck down one hundred eighty-five thousand in the camp of the Assyrians; when people got up early in the morning, there were all the dead bodies.
text.Rotherham: Then went forth the messenger of Yahweh, and smote—in the camp of the Assyrians—a hundred and eighty-five thousand,—and when men arose early in the morning, lo! they were all, dead bodies!
text.KJV: Then the angel of the Lord went forth, and smote in the camp of the Assyrians a hundred and fourscore and five thousand: and when they arose early in the morning, behold, they were all dead corpses.
text.NABRE: Then the angel of the Lord went forth and struck down one hundred and eighty-five thousand in the Assyrian camp. Early the next morning, there they were, all those corpses, dead!
text.DRB: And the Angel of our Lord came forth, and struck in the camp of the Assyrians an hundred eighty five thousand. And they arose in the morning, and behold, all were carcasses of dead men.

text.NRSV: Then King Sennacherib of Assyria left, went home, and lived at Nineveh.
text.Rotherham: So Sennacherib the king of Assyria brake up, and went his way, and returned,—and remained in Nineveh.
text.KJV: So Sennacherib king of Assyria departed, and went and returned, and dwelt at Nineveh.
text.NABRE: So Sennacherib, the king of Assyria, broke camp, departed, returned home, and stayed in Nineveh.
text.DRB: And he went out, and departed, and Sennacherib the king of the Assyrians returned, and dwelt in Ninive.

text.NRSV: As he was worshiping in the house of his god Nisroch, his sons Adrammelech and Sharezer killed him with the sword, and they escaped into the land of Ararat. His son Esar-haddon succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he was bowing down in the house of Nisroch his god, that, Adrammelech and Sharezer his sons, smote him with the sword, howbeit, they, escaped into the land of Ararat,—and, Esarhaddon his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, as he was worshipping in the house of Nisroch his god, that Adrammelech and Sharezer his sons smote him with the sword; and they escaped into the land of Armenia: and Esar–haddon his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: When he was worshiping in the temple of his god Nisroch, his sons Adrammelech and Sharezer struck him down with the sword and fled into the land of Ararat. His son Esarhaddon reigned in his place.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when he adored in the temple of Nesroch his god, Adramelech & Sarasar his sons struck him with the sword: and they fled into the land of Ararat, & Asarhaddon his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: In those days Hezekiah became sick and was at the point of death. The prophet Isaiah son of Amoz came to him, and said to him, “Thus says the Lord: Set your house in order, for you shall die; you shall not recover.”
text.Rotherham: In those days, was Hezekiah sick, unto death,—and Isaiah the prophet son of Amoz came in unto him, and said unto him—Thus, saith Yahweh, Set in order thy house, for, about to die thou art and shalt not recover.
text.KJV: In those days was Hezekiah sick unto death. And Isaiah the prophet the son of Amoz came unto him, and said unto him, Thus saith the Lord, Set thine house in order: for thou shalt die, and not live.
text.NABRE: In those days, when Hezekiah was mortally ill, the prophet Isaiah, son of Amoz, came and said to him: “Thus says the Lord: Put your house in order, for you are about to die; you shall not recover.”
text.DRB: In those days Ezechias was sick even to death: and Isaie the son of Amos the Prophet, went in unto him, and said to him: Thus saith our Lord: Take order with thy house, for thou shalt die, and shalt not live.

text.NRSV: Then Hezekiah turned his face to the wall, and prayed to the Lord:
text.Rotherham: Then Hezekiah turned his face unto the wall,—and prayed unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: Then Hezekiah turned his face toward the wall, and prayed unto the Lord,
text.NABRE: Hezekiah turned his face to the wall and prayed to the Lord:
text.DRB: And Ezechias turned his face to the wall, & prayed to our Lord,

text.NRSV: “Remember now, O Lord, I implore you, how I have walked before you in faithfulness with a whole heart, and have done what is good in your sight.” And Hezekiah wept bitterly.
text.Rotherham: and said,—I beseech thee, O Yahweh, remember, I pray thee, how I have walked before thee in faithfulness and with an undivided heart, and, that which is good in thine eyes, have I done. And Hezekiah wept aloud.
text.KJV: And said, Remember now, O Lord, I beseech thee, how I have walked before thee in truth and with a perfect heart, and have done that which is good in thy sight. And Hezekiah wept sore.
text.NABRE: “Ah, Lord, remember how faithfully and wholeheartedly I conducted myself in your presence, doing what was good in your sight!” And Hezekiah wept bitterly.
text.DRB: and said: I beseech thee Lord, remember I pray thee how I have walked before thee in truth, and in a perfect heart, and have done that which is good in thine eyes. And Ezechias wept with great weeping.

text.NRSV: Then the word of the Lord came to Isaiah:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto, Isaiah, saying:
text.KJV: Then came the word of the Lord to Isaiah, saying,
text.NABRE: Then the word of the Lord came to Isaiah:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to Isaie, saying:

text.NRSV: “Go and say to Hezekiah, Thus says the Lord, the God of your ancestor David: I have heard your prayer, I have seen your tears; I will add fifteen years to your life.
text.Rotherham: Go, and say unto Hezekiah—Thus, saith Yahweh, the God of David thy father: I have heard thy prayer, I have seen thy tears,—Behold me! about to add unto thy days, fifteen years;
text.KJV: Go, and say to Hezekiah, Thus saith the Lord, the God of David thy father, I have heard thy prayer, I have seen thy tears: behold, I will add unto thy days fifteen years.
text.NABRE: Go, tell Hezekiah: Thus says the Lord, the God of your father David: I have heard your prayer; I have seen your tears. Now I will add fifteen years to your life.
text.DRB: Go, & tell Ezechias: Thus saith our Lord the God of David thy father: I have heard thy prayer, and seen thy tears: Lo I will add upon thy days fifteen years:

text.NRSV: I will deliver you and this city out of the hand of the king of Assyria, and defend this city.
text.Rotherham: And out of the hand of the king of Assyria, will I deliver thee, and this city; And I will throw a covering over this city.
text.KJV: And I will deliver thee and this city out of the hand of the king of Assyria: and I will defend this city.
text.NABRE: I will rescue you and this city from the hand of the king of Assyria; I will be a shield to this city.
text.DRB: and out of the hand of the king of the Assyrians will I deliver thee, and this city, & will protect it.

text.NRSV: “This is the sign to you from the Lord, that the Lord will do this thing that he has promised:
text.Rotherham: And, this, to thee, shall be the sign from Yahweh,—that Yahweh will do this thing which he hath spoken:—
text.KJV: And this shall be a sign unto thee from the Lord, that the Lord will do this thing that he hath spoken;
text.NABRE: This will be the sign for you from the Lord that the Lord will carry out the word he has spoken:
text.DRB: And this shall be a sign to thee from our Lord that our Lord will do this word, which he hath spoken:

text.NRSV: See, I will make the shadow cast by the declining sun on the dial of Ahaz turn back ten steps.” So the sun turned back on the dial the ten steps by which it had declined.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! causing the shadow on the steps, which hath come gone down on the steps of Ahaz with the sun, to return, backwards ten steps. So the sun returned ten steps, by the steps which it had come down.
text.KJV: Behold, I will bring again the shadow of the degrees, which is gone down in the sun dial of Ahaz, ten degrees backward. So the sun returned ten degrees, by which degrees it was gone down.
text.NABRE: See, I will make the shadow cast by the sun on the stairway to the terrace of Ahaz go back the ten steps it has advanced. So the sun came back the ten steps it had advanced.
text.DRB: Behold I will make the shadow of the lines return, by the which it is now gone down in the dial of Achaz in the sun, back-ward ten lines. And the sun returned ten lines by the degrees whereby it was gone down.

text.NRSV: A writing of King Hezekiah of Judah, after he had been sick and had recovered from his sickness:
text.Rotherham: the writing of Hezekiah king of Judah, when he had been sick and then recovered from his sickness:
text.KJV: The writing of Hezekiah king of Judah, when he had been sick, and was recovered of his sickness:
text.NABRE: Hezekiah’s Hymn of Thanksgiving. The song of Hezekiah, king of Judah, after he had been sick and had recovered from his illness:
text.DRB: The scripture of Ezechias the king of Juda, when he had been sick, & was recovered of his infirmity.

text.NRSV: I said: In the noontide of my days I must depart; I am consigned to the gates of Sheol for the rest of my years.
text.Rotherham: I, said—In the noontide of my days, I must enter the gates of hades,—I am deprived of the residue of my years!
text.KJV: I said in the cutting off of my days, I shall go to the gates of the grave: I am deprived of the residue of my years.
text.NABRE: In the noontime of life I said, I must depart! To the gates of Sheol I have been consigned for the rest of my years.
text.DRB: I have said: In the midst of my days shall I go to the gates of hell. I have sought the residue of my years.

text.NRSV: I said, I shall not see the Lord in the land of the living; I shall look upon mortals no more among the inhabitants of the world.
text.Rotherham: I said—I shall not see Yah, Yah, in the land of the living, I shall discern the son of earth no longer, with the dwellers in the quiet land.
text.KJV: I said, I shall not see the Lord, even the Lord, in the land of the living: I shall behold man no more with the inhabitants of the world.
text.NABRE: I said, I shall see the Lord no more in the land of the living. Nor look on any mortals among those who dwell in the world.
text.DRB: I have said: I shall not see our Lord God in the land of the living. I shall behold man no more, and the inhabiter of rest.

text.NRSV: My dwelling is plucked up and removed from me like a shepherd’s tent; like a weaver I have rolled up my life; he cuts me off from the loom; from day to night you bring me to an end;
text.Rotherham: My dwelling, hath been broken up. And is stripped from me, like a shepherd’s tent,—I have rolled up—as a weaver—my life From the loom, doth he cut me off, From day until night, [I said] —Thou wilt finish me.
text.KJV: Mine age is departed, and is removed from me as a shepherd’s tent: I have cut off like a weaver my life: he will cut me off with pining sickness: from day even to night wilt thou make an end of me.
text.NABRE: My dwelling, like a shepherd’s tent, is struck down and borne away from me; You have folded up my life, like a weaver who severs me from the last thread. From morning to night you make an end of me;
text.DRB: My generation is taken away; and is wrapped together from me, as the tent of shepherds: My life is cut off, as by a weaver; whilst I yet began he cut me off: from morning until night thou wilt make an end of me.

text.NRSV: I cry for help until morning; like a lion he breaks all my bones; from day to night you bring me to an end.
text.Rotherham: I cried out, until morning, like a lion, Thus, will he break all my bones! From day until night, Thou wilt finish me!
text.KJV: I reckoned till morning, that, as a lion, so will he break all my bones: from day even to night wilt thou make an end of me.
text.NABRE: I cry out even until the dawn. Like a lion he breaks all my bones; from morning to night you make an end of me.
text.DRB: I hoped until morning, as a lion so hath he broken all my bones: From morning until evening thou wilt make an end of me.

text.NRSV: Like a swallow or a crane I clamor, I moan like a dove. My eyes are weary with looking upward. O Lord, I am oppressed; be my security!
text.Rotherham: As a twittering swallow, so, do I chatter, I coo as a dove,—Mine eyes languish through looking on high, O My Lord! distress is upon me—my Surety!
text.KJV: Like a crane or a swallow, so did I chatter: I did mourn as a dove: mine eyes fail with looking upward: O Lord, I am oppressed; undertake for me.
text.NABRE: Like a swallow I chirp; I moan like a dove. My eyes grow weary looking heavenward: Lord, I am overwhelmed; go security for me!
text.DRB: As a young swallow so will I cry. I will meditate as a dove: Mine eyes are weakened, looking on high: Lord I suffer violence, answer for me.

text.NRSV: But what can I say? For he has spoken to me, and he himself has done it. All my sleep has fled because of the bitterness of my soul.
text.Rotherham: What can I say? Since he hath promised for me, Himself, will perform. I will go softly, all my years. Because of the bitterness of my soul,
text.KJV: What shall I say? he hath both spoken unto me, and himself hath done it: I shall go softly all my years in the bitterness of my soul.
text.NABRE: What am I to say or tell him? He is the one who has done it! All my sleep has fled, because of the bitterness of my soul.
text.DRB: What shall I say, or what shall he answer me, whereas himself hath done it?

text.NRSV: O Lord, by these things people live, and in all these is the life of my spirit. Oh, restore me to health and make me live!
text.Rotherham: O My Lord! on those things do men live,—And, altogether in them, is the life of my spirit, When thou hast strengthened me and made me live.
text.KJV: O Lord, by these things men live, and in all these things is the life of my spirit: so wilt thou recover me, and make me to live.
text.NABRE: Those live whom the Lord protects; yours is the life of my spirit. You have given me health and restored my life!
text.DRB: I will recount to thee all my years in the bitterness of my soul. Lord if man's life be such, and the life of my spirit in such things, thou shalt chasten me, and shalt quicken me.

text.NRSV: Surely it was for my welfare that I had great bitterness; but you have held back my life from the pit of destruction, for you have cast all my sins behind your back.
text.Rotherham: Lo! for well-being, I had bitterness—bitterness,—But, thou, cleaving unto my soul, hast raised me from the pit of corruption, For thou hast cast, behind thy back all my sins.
text.KJV: Behold, for peace I had great bitterness: but thou hast in love to my soul delivered it from the pit of corruption: for thou hast cast all my sins behind thy back.
text.NABRE: Peace in place of bitterness! You have preserved my life from the pit of destruction; Behind your back you cast all my sins.
text.DRB: Behold in peace is my bitterness most bitter: But thou hast delivered my soul that it should not perish, thou hast cast all my sins behind thy back.

text.NRSV: For Sheol cannot thank you, death cannot praise you; those who go down to the Pit cannot hope for your faithfulness.
text.Rotherham: For, hades, cannot praise thee Nor, death, celebrate thee,—They who go down to the pit cannot wait for thy faithfulness.
text.KJV: For the grave cannot praise thee, death can not celebrate thee: they that go down into the pit cannot hope for thy truth.
text.NABRE: For it is not Sheol that gives you thanks, nor death that praises you; Neither do those who go down into the pit await your kindness.
text.DRB: Because hell shall not confess to thee, neither shall death praise thee: they that go down into the lake, shall not expect thy truth.

text.NRSV: The living, the living, they thank you, as I do this day; fathers make known to children your faithfulness.
text.Rotherham: The living, the living, he, can praise thee, As I do this day,—A father, to his children, can make known thy faithfulness.
text.KJV: The living, the living, he shall praise thee, as I do this day: the father to the children shall make known thy truth.
text.NABRE: The living, the living give you thanks, as I do today. Parents declare to their children, O God, your faithfulness.
text.DRB: The living the living he shall confess to thee, as I also this day: the father shall make thy truth known to the children.

text.NRSV: The Lord will save me, and we will sing to stringed instruments all the days of our lives, at the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, [was willing] to save me,—Therefore, on my stringed instruments, will we play—All the days of our life By the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: The Lord was ready to save me: therefore we will sing my songs to the stringed instruments all the days of our life in the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: The Lord is there to save us. We shall play our music In the house of the Lord all the days of our life.
text.DRB: O Lord save me, and we shall sing our Psalms, all the days of our life in the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Now Isaiah had said, “Let them take a lump of figs, and apply it to the boil, so that he may recover.”
text.Rotherham: And Isaiah had said, Let them take a cake of figs, and let them press it over the boil, that he may recover.
text.KJV: For Isaiah had said, Let them take a lump of figs, and lay it for a plaister upon the boil, and he shall recover.
text.NABRE: Then Isaiah said, “Bring a poultice of figs and apply it to the boil for his recovery.”
text.DRB: And Isaie commanded that they should take a lump of figs, & plaster it upon the wound, and he should be healed.

text.NRSV: Hezekiah also had said, “What is the sign that I shall go up to the house of the Lord?”
text.Rotherham: And Hezekiah had said—What is the sign—that I shall go up unto the house of Yahweh?
text.KJV: Hezekiah also had said, What is the sign that I shall go up to the house of the Lord?
text.NABRE: Hezekiah asked, “What is the sign that I shall go up to the house of the Lord?”
text.DRB: And Ezechias said: What shall be the sign that I shall go up into the house of our Lord?

text.NRSV: At that time Merodach-baladan son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent envoys with letters and a present to Hezekiah, for he heard that he had been sick and had recovered.
text.Rotherham: At that time, Merodach-baladan son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a present unto Hezekiah,—for he had heard that Hezekiah had been sick, and had recovered.
text.KJV: At that time Merodach–baladan, the son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a present to Hezekiah: for he had heard that he had been sick, and was recovered.
text.NABRE: At that time Merodach-baladan, son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and gifts to Hezekiah, when he heard that he had been sick and had recovered.
text.DRB: At that time Merodach Baladan the son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters & gifts to Ezechias: for he had heard that he had been sick, and was recovered,

text.NRSV: Hezekiah welcomed them; he showed them his treasure house, the silver, the gold, the spices, the precious oil, his whole armory, all that was found in his storehouses. There was nothing in his house or in all his realm that Hezekiah did not show them.
text.Rotherham: And Hezekiah, rejoiced over them, and showed them his house of precious things—the silver and the gold and the spices and the precious ointment and all his armoury—and all that was found among his treasures,—there was nothing, which Hezekiah, did not show them—in his house or in all his dominion.
text.KJV: And Hezekiah was glad of them, and shewed them the house of his precious things, the silver, and the gold, and the spices, and the precious ointment, and all the house of his armour, and all that was found in his treasures: there was nothing in his house, nor in all his dominion, that Hezekiah shewed them not.
text.NABRE: Hezekiah was pleased at their coming, and then showed the messengers his treasury, the silver and gold, the spices and perfumed oil, his whole armory, and everything in his storerooms; there was nothing in his house or in all his realm that Hezekiah did not show them.
text.DRB: And Ezechias rejoiced upon them, and he shewed them the storehouse of aromatical spices, & of silver, and of gold, and of sweet-odours and of the best ointment, and all the store houses of his furniture, and all things that were found in his treasures. There was not any thing, which Ezechias shewed them not in his house, and in all his dominion.

text.NRSV: Then the prophet Isaiah came to King Hezekiah and said to him, “What did these men say? From where did they come to you?” Hezekiah answered, “They have come to me from a far country, from Babylon.”
text.Rotherham: Then came Isaiah the prophet, unto King Hezekiah,—and said unto him—What said these men? and whence came they unto thee? And Hezekiah said, From a land far away, came they unto me, from Babylon!
text.KJV: Then came Isaiah the prophet unto king Hezekiah, and said unto him, What said these men? and from whence came they unto thee? And Hezekiah said, They are come from a far country unto me, even from Babylon.
text.NABRE: Then Isaiah the prophet came to King Hezekiah and asked him, “What did these men say to you? Where did they come from?” Hezekiah replied, “They came to me from a distant land, from Babylon.”
text.DRB: But Isaie the Prophet went in to Ezechias the king, and said to him: What said these men, and from whence came they to thee? And Ezechias said: from a far country they came to me, from Babylon.

text.NRSV: He said, “What have they seen in your house?” Hezekiah answered, “They have seen all that is in my house; there is nothing in my storehouses that I did not show them.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, What have they seen in thy house? Then said Hezekiah,—All that is in my house, have they seen, there is nothing which I shewed them not among my treasures.
text.KJV: Then said he, What have they seen in thine house? And Hezekiah answered, All that is in mine house have they seen: there is nothing among my treasures that I have not shewed them.
text.NABRE: He asked, “What did they see in your house?” Hezekiah answered, “They saw everything in my house. There is nothing in my storerooms that I did not show them.”
text.DRB: And he said: What saw they in thy house? And Ezechias said: All things that are in my house have they seen, there was not any thing, which I have not shewed them in my treasures.

text.NRSV: Then Isaiah said to Hezekiah, “Hear the word of the Lord of hosts:
text.Rotherham: Then said Isaiah unto Hezekiah,—Hear thou the word of Yahweh of hosts:
text.KJV: Then said Isaiah to Hezekiah, Hear the word of the Lord of hosts:
text.NABRE: Then Isaiah said to Hezekiah, “Hear the word of the Lord of hosts:
text.DRB: And Isaie said to Ezechias: Hear the word of the Lord of hosts.

text.NRSV: Days are coming when all that is in your house, and that which your ancestors have stored up until this day, shall be carried to Babylon; nothing shall be left, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Lo! days are coming, when all that is in thy house, and that which thy fathers have treasured up until this day, shall be carried away unto Babylon,—nothing, shall be left, Saith Yahweh.
text.KJV: Behold, the days come, that all that is in thine house, and that which thy fathers have laid up in store until this day, shall be carried to Babylon: nothing shall be left, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: The time is coming when all that is in your house, everything that your ancestors have stored up until this day, shall be carried off to Babylon; nothing shall be left, says the Lord.
text.DRB: Behold the days shall come: and all things that are in thy house, and that thy fathers have laid up for treasure until this day, shall be taken away into Babylon: there shall not any thing be left, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Some of your own sons who are born to you shall be taken away; they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.”
text.Rotherham: And of thy sons who shall issue from thee whom thou shalt beget, shall they take away,—and they shall become eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.
text.KJV: And of thy sons that shall issue from thee, which thou shalt beget, shall they take away; and they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.
text.NABRE: Some of your own descendants, your progeny, shall be taken and made attendants in the palace of the king of Babylon.”
text.DRB: And of thy children, which shall come forth of thee, whom thou shalt beget, they shall take away, and they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.

text.NRSV: Then Hezekiah said to Isaiah, “The word of the Lord that you have spoken is good.” For he thought, “There will be peace and security in my days.”
text.Rotherham: And Hezekiah said unto Isaiah, Good is the word of Yahweh, which thou hast spoken. And he said, Surely, there shall be peace and stability in my days.
text.KJV: Then said Hezekiah to Isaiah, Good is the word of the Lord which thou hast spoken. He said moreover, For there shall be peace and truth in my days.
text.NABRE: Hezekiah replied to Isaiah, “The word of the Lord which you have spoken is good.” For he thought, “There will be peace and stability in my lifetime.”
text.DRB: And Ezechias said to Isaie: The word of our Lord which he hath spoken is good. And he said: Only be there peace and truth in my days.

text.NRSV: Comfort, O comfort my people, says your God.
text.Rotherham: Comfort ye—comfort ye my people,—Saith your God.
text.KJV: Comfort ye, comfort ye my people, saith your God.
text.NABRE: II. Isaiah 40—55 A. The Lord’s Glory in Israel’s Liberation Chapter 40 Promise of Salvation Comfort, give comfort to my people, says your God.
text.DRB: Be comforted, be comforted my people, saith your God.

text.NRSV: Speak tenderly to Jerusalem, and cry to her that she has served her term, that her penalty is paid, that she has received from the Lord’s hand double for all her sins.
text.Rotherham: Speak ye unto the heart of Jerusalem, And cry unto her,—That accomplished is her warfare, That accepted is her punishment,—That she hath received, at the hand of Yahweh, According to the full measure of all her sins.
text.KJV: Speak ye comfortably to Jerusalem, and cry unto her, that her warfare is accomplished, that her iniquity is pardoned: for she hath received of the Lord’s hand double for all her sins.
text.NABRE: Speak to the heart of Jerusalem, and proclaim to her that her service has ended, that her guilt is expiated, That she has received from the hand of the Lord double for all her sins.
text.DRB: Speak to the heart of Jerusalem, and call to her: because her malice is accomplished, her iniquity is forgiven: she hath received of the hand of our Lord double for all her sins.

text.NRSV: A voice cries out: “In the wilderness prepare the way of the Lord, make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
text.Rotherham: A voice of one crying!—In the desert, prepare ye the way of Yahweh,—Make smooth in the waste plain a highway for our God:
text.KJV: The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
text.NABRE: A voice proclaims: In the wilderness prepare the way of the Lord! Make straight in the wasteland a highway for our God!
text.DRB: The voice of one crying in the desert: Prepare the way of our Lord, make straight the paths of our God in the wilderness.

text.NRSV: Every valley shall be lifted up, and every mountain and hill be made low; the uneven ground shall become level, and the rough places a plain.
text.Rotherham: Let, every valley, be exalted, And every mountain and hill, be made low,—And, the steep ground become, level, And, the chain of hills—a plain:
text.KJV: Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and hill shall be made low: and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough places plain:
text.NABRE: Every valley shall be lifted up, every mountain and hill made low; The rugged land shall be a plain, the rough country, a broad valley.
text.DRB: Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and little hill shall be humbled, and crooked things shall become straight, and rough ways, plain.

text.NRSV: Then the glory of the Lord shall be revealed, and all people shall see it together, for the mouth of the Lord has spoken.”
text.Rotherham: Then shall be revealed the glory of Yahweh,—And all flesh shall see it together, For, the mouth of Yahweh, hath spoken!
text.KJV: And the glory of the Lord shall be revealed, and all flesh shall see it together: for the mouth of the Lord hath spoken it.
text.NABRE: Then the glory of the Lord shall be revealed, and all flesh shall see it together; for the mouth of the Lord has spoken.
text.DRB: And the glory of our Lord shall be revealed, and all flesh together shall see, the mouth of our Lord hath spoken.

text.NRSV: A voice says, “Cry out!” And I said, “What shall I cry?” All people are grass, their constancy is like the flower of the field.
text.Rotherham: A voice saying Cry! And one said—What should I cry? All flesh, is grass, And, all the grace thereof, like the flower of the field:
text.KJV: The voice said, Cry. And he said, What shall I cry? All flesh is grass, and all the goodliness thereof is as the flower of the field:
text.NABRE: A voice says, “Proclaim!” I answer, “What shall I proclaim?” “All flesh is grass, and all their loyalty like the flower of the field.
text.DRB: The voice of one saying: Cry. And I said: What shall I cry? All flesh is grass, and all the glory thereof as the flower of the field.

text.NRSV: The grass withers, the flower fades, when the breath of the Lord blows upon it; surely the people are grass.
text.Rotherham: The grass hath withered The flower hath faded, Because, the breath of Yahweh, hath blown upon it! Surely the people is grass!
text.KJV: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: because the spirit of the Lord bloweth upon it: surely the people is grass.
text.NABRE: The grass withers, the flower wilts, when the breath of the Lord blows upon it.” “Yes, the people is grass!
text.DRB: The grass is withered, and the flower is fallen, because the spirit of our Lord hath blown on it. Indeed the people is grass:

text.NRSV: The grass withers, the flower fades; but the word of our God will stand forever.
text.Rotherham: The grass, hath withered, The flower, hath faded,—But the word of our God, shall stand unto times age-abiding!
text.KJV: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: but the word of our God shall stand for ever.
text.NABRE: The grass withers, the flower wilts, but the word of our God stands forever.”
text.DRB: the grass is withered, and the flower is fallen: but the word of our Lord abideth for ever.

text.NRSV: Get you up to a high mountain, O Zion, herald of good tidings; lift up your voice with strength, O Jerusalem, herald of good tidings, lift it up, do not fear; say to the cities of Judah, “Here is your God!”
text.Rotherham: To a high mountain, get ye up, O herald—band of Zion, Lift high with strength your voice, O herald—band of Jerusalem,—Lift it high, do not fear, Say to the cities of Judah—Lo! your God!
text.KJV: O Zion, that bringest good tidings, get thee up into the high mountain; O Jerusalem, that bringest good tidings, lift up thy voice with strength; lift it up, be not afraid; say unto the cities of Judah, Behold your God!
text.NABRE: Go up onto a high mountain, Zion, herald of good news! Cry out at the top of your voice, Jerusalem, herald of good news! Cry out, do not fear! Say to the cities of Judah: Here is your God!
text.DRB: Upon an high mountain get thee up, thou that evangelizest to Sion: exalt the voice in strength, which evangelizest to Jerusalem: exalt it, fear not. Say to the cities of Juda: Behold your God:

text.NRSV: See, the Lord God comes with might, and his arm rules for him; his reward is with him, and his recompense before him.
text.Rotherham: Lo! My Lord, Yahweh, as a mighty one, doth come, And, his own arm, is about to rule for him,—Lo! his reward, is with him, And, his recompense, before him;
text.KJV: Behold, the Lord God will come with strong hand, and his arm shall rule for him: behold, his reward is with him, and his work before him.
text.NABRE: Here comes with power the Lord God, who rules by his strong arm; Here is his reward with him, his recompense before him.
text.DRB: behold our Lord God shall come in strength, & his arm shall have dominion: behold his reward is with him, and his work before him.

text.NRSV: He will feed his flock like a shepherd; he will gather the lambs in his arms, and carry them in his bosom, and gently lead the mother sheep.
text.Rotherham: Like a shepherd, his flock, will he tend, In his own arm, will he take up the lambs, And in his own bosom, will he carry [them],—Them which are with young, will he lead to a place of rest.
text.KJV: He shall feed his flock like a shepherd: he shall gather the lambs with his arm, and carry them in his bosom, and shall gently lead those that are with young.
text.NABRE: Like a shepherd he feeds his flock; in his arms he gathers the lambs, Carrying them in his bosom, leading the ewes with care.
text.DRB: As a shepherd shall he feed his flock: in his arm shall he gather together the lambs, and in his bosom shall he lift them up, and them with young himself shall carry.

text.NRSV: Who has measured the waters in the hollow of his hand and marked off the heavens with a span, enclosed the dust of the earth in a measure, and weighed the mountains in scales and the hills in a balance?
text.Rotherham: Who hath measured, with the hollow of his hand, the waters. Or the heavens with a span, hath meted out, Or hath comprehended, in a measure, the dust of the earth, Or weighed, in scales, the mountains, Or the hills, in a balance?
text.KJV: Who hath measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, and meted out heaven with the span, and comprehended the dust of the earth in a measure, and weighed the mountains in scales, and the hills in a balance?
text.NABRE: Power of God and the Vanity of Idols Who has measured with his palm the waters, marked off the heavens with a span, held in his fingers the dust of the earth, weighed the mountains in scales and the hills in a balance?
text.DRB: Who hath measured the waters with his fist, and pondered the heavens with a span? Who hath poised with three fingers the huge greatness of the earth, & weighed the mountains in weight, and the little hills in balance?

text.NRSV: Who has directed the spirit of the Lord, or as his counselor has instructed him?
text.Rotherham: Who hath proved the spirit of Yahweh? Or being his counsellor hath been giving him knowledge?
text.KJV: Who hath directed the Spirit of the Lord, or being his counsellor hath taught him?
text.NABRE: Who has directed the spirit of the Lord, or instructed him as his counselor?
text.DRB: Who hath holpen the spirit of our Lord? Or who hath been his counseller, & shewed to him?

text.NRSV: Whom did he consult for his enlightenment, and who taught him the path of justice? Who taught him knowledge, and showed him the way of understanding?
text.Rotherham: With whom hath he taken counsel—And he hath given him intelligence, And instructed him in the path of justice,—And taught him knowledge, And in the way of intelligence, hath been giving him understanding?
text.KJV: With whom took he counsel, and who instructed him, and taught him in the path of judgment, and taught him knowledge, and shewed to him the way of understanding?
text.NABRE: Whom did he consult to gain knowledge? Who taught him the path of judgment, or showed him the way of understanding?
text.DRB: With whom hath he taken counsel, and who hath instructed him, and taught him the path of justice, and taught him knowledge, and shewed him the way of prudence?

text.NRSV: Even the nations are like a drop from a bucket, and are accounted as dust on the scales; see, he takes up the islands like fine dust.
text.Rotherham: Lo! nations, Are us a drop on a bucket, And as fine dust on a balance, are accounted,—Lo! islands, like an atom, can he hoist;
text.KJV: Behold, the nations are as a drop of a bucket, and are counted as the small dust of the balance: behold, he taketh up the isles as a very little thing.
text.NABRE: See, the nations count as a drop in the bucket, as a wisp of cloud on the scales; the coastlands weigh no more than a speck.
text.DRB: Behold the Gentiles are as a drop of a bucket, and are reputed as the moment of a balance: behold the islands are as a little dust.

text.NRSV: Lebanon would not provide fuel enough, nor are its animals enough for a burnt offering.
text.Rotherham: And, Lebanon, is not sufficient to burn,—Nor, the beasts thereof sufficient for an ascending-sacrifice!
text.KJV: And Lebanon is not sufficient to burn, nor the beasts thereof sufficient for a burnt offering.
text.NABRE: Lebanon would not suffice for fuel, nor its animals be enough for burnt offerings.
text.DRB: And Libanus shall not suffice to kindle the fire, and the beasts thereof shall not be sufficient for holocaust.

text.NRSV: All the nations are as nothing before him; they are accounted by him as less than nothing and emptiness.
text.Rotherham: All nations, are as nothing before him,—A thing of nought or a waste, are they accounted unto him?
text.KJV: All nations before him are as nothing; and they are counted to him less than nothing, and vanity.
text.NABRE: Before him all the nations are as nought, as nothing and void he counts them.
text.DRB: All nations as if they were not, so are they before him, and they are reputed of him as nothing, and a vain thing.

text.NRSV: To whom then will you liken God, or what likeness compare with him?
text.Rotherham: Unto whom then can ye liken GOD? Or, what likeness, can ye compare unto him?
text.KJV: To whom then will ye liken God? or what likeness will ye compare unto him?
text.NABRE: To whom can you liken God? With what likeness can you confront him?
text.DRB: To whom then have you made God like? Or what image will you set to him?

text.NRSV: An idol? —A workman casts it, and a goldsmith overlays it with gold, and casts for it silver chains.
text.Rotherham: The image, hath been cast by an artificer, And a goldsmith, with gold, overlayeth it,—And, chains of silver, he worketh.
text.KJV: The workman melteth a graven image, and the goldsmith spreadeth it over with gold, and casteth silver chains.
text.NABRE: An idol? An artisan casts it, the smith plates it with gold, fits it with silver chains.
text.DRB: Hath the artificer cast a sculptile? or hath the goldsmith figured it with gold, or the silversmith with plates of silver?

text.NRSV: As a gift one chooses mulberry wood—wood that will not rot—then seeks out a skilled artisan to set up an image that will not totter.
text.Rotherham: The needy offerer, of a tree that will not rot, maketh choice,—A skilled artificer, seeketh he out for himself to construct an image that shall not totter,
text.KJV: He that is so impoverished that he hath no oblation chooseth a tree that will not rot; he seeketh unto him a cunning workman to prepare a graven image, that shall not be moved.
text.NABRE: Is mulberry wood the offering? A skilled artisan picks out a wood that will not rot, Seeks to set up for himself an idol that will not totter.
text.DRB: Strong wood, and that which will not putrefy hath he chosen: the wise artificer seeketh how he may set up a sculptile which may not be moved.

text.NRSV: Have you not known? Have you not heard? Has it not been told you from the beginning? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth?
text.Rotherham: Have ye never taken note? Have ye never heard? Hath it not from the beginning, been told you? Have ye not been led to discern, from the foundations of the earth?
text.KJV: Have ye not known? have ye not heard? hath it not been told you from the beginning? have ye not understood from the foundations of the earth?
text.NABRE: Do you not know? Have you not heard? Was it not told you from the beginning? Have you not understood from the founding of the earth?
text.DRB: Why, do you not know: why, have you not heard? Why, hath it not been told you from the beginning? Have you not understood the foundations of the earth?

text.NRSV: It is he who sits above the circle of the earth, and its inhabitants are like grasshoppers; who stretches out the heavens like a curtain, and spreads them like a tent to live in;
text.Rotherham: It is he who sitteth upon the circle of the earth, While the inhabitants thereof are, as grass-hoppers,—Who stretcheth forth, as a curtain, the heavens, And spreadeth them out as a tent to dwell in;
text.KJV: It is he that sitteth upon the circle of the earth, and the inhabitants thereof are as grasshoppers; that stretcheth out the heavens as a curtain, and spreadeth them out as a tent to dwell in:
text.NABRE: The one who is enthroned above the vault of the earth, its inhabitants like grasshoppers, Who stretches out the heavens like a veil and spreads them out like a tent to dwell in,
text.DRB: He that sitteth upon the compass of the earth, and the inhabitants thereof are as locusts: he that stretcheth out the heavens as nothing, and spreadeth them as a tent to dwell in.

text.NRSV: who brings princes to naught, and makes the rulers of the earth as nothing.
text.Rotherham: Who delivereth dignitaries to nothingness,—Judges of earth, like a desolation, hath he made:
text.KJV: That bringeth the princes to nothing; he maketh the judges of the earth as vanity.
text.NABRE: Who brings princes to nought and makes the rulers of the earth as nothing.
text.DRB: He that maketh the searchers of secrets as if they were not, that hath made the judges of the earth as a vain thing:

text.NRSV: Scarcely are they planted, scarcely sown, scarcely has their stem taken root in the earth, when he blows upon them, and they wither, and the tempest carries them off like stubble.
text.Rotherham: Scarcely have they been planted, Scarcely have they been sown, Scarcely hath their stock, begun to take root in the earth, When he hath just blown upon them and they have withered, And, a whirlwind, as though they had been chaff, carrieth them away.
text.KJV: Yea, they shall not be planted; yea, they shall not be sown: yea, their stock shall not take root in the earth: and he shall also blow upon them, and they shall wither, and the whirlwind shall take them away as stubble.
text.NABRE: Scarcely are they planted, scarcely sown, scarcely their stem rooted in the earth, When he breathes upon them and they wither, and the stormwind carries them away like straw.
text.DRB: and indeed their stock was neither planted, nor sown, nor rooted in the earth: suddenly he hath blown upon them, and they have withered, and a whirlwind shall take them away as stubble.

text.NRSV: To whom then will you compare me, or who is my equal? says the Holy One.
text.Rotherham: Unto whom then, can ye liken me, or can I be equal? Saith the Holy One.
text.KJV: To whom then will ye liken me, or shall I be equal? saith the Holy One.
text.NABRE: To whom can you liken me as an equal? says the Holy One.
text.DRB: And to whom have ye likened me, and made me equal? Saith the holy one.

text.NRSV: Lift up your eyes on high and see: Who created these? He who brings out their host and numbers them, calling them all by name; because he is great in strength, mighty in power, not one is missing.
text.Rotherham: Lift on high your eyes—and see who hath created these, That bringeth forth, by number, their host,—To all of them by name, doth call, Because of the abundance of vigour and alertness of strength, not one, is missing!
text.KJV: Lift up your eyes on high, and behold who hath created these things, that bringeth out their host by number: he calleth them all by names by the greatness of his might, for that he is strong in power; not one faileth.
text.NABRE: Lift up your eyes on high and see who created these: He leads out their army and numbers them, calling them all by name. By his great might and the strength of his power not one of them is missing!
text.DRB: Lift up your eyes on high, and see who hath created these things: he that bringeth out the host of them in number, and calleth them all by name: by the multitude of his force and strength, & power, not one of them was missing.

text.NRSV: Why do you say, O Jacob, and speak, O Israel, “My way is hidden from the Lord, and my right is disregarded by my God”?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, shouldest thou say, O Jacob, or speak, O Israel,—Hidden is my path from Yahweh, And from my God, my vindication, will pass?
text.KJV: Why sayest thou, O Jacob, and speakest, O Israel, My way is hid from the Lord, and my judgment is passed over from my God?
text.NABRE: Why, O Jacob, do you say, and declare, O Israel, “My way is hidden from the Lord, and my right is disregarded by my God”?
text.DRB: Why sayest thou Jacob, and speakest thou Israel: My way is hid from our Lord, and my judgement is passed over of my God?

text.NRSV: Have you not known? Have you not heard? The Lord is the everlasting God, the Creator of the ends of the earth. He does not faint or grow weary; his understanding is unsearchable.
text.Rotherham: Hast thou not known, Hast thou not heard, That The God of age-past time—Yahweh The Creator of the ends of the earth Fainteth not neither groweth weary—There is no searching of his understanding:
text.KJV: Hast thou not known? hast thou not heard, that the everlasting God, the Lord, the Creator of the ends of the earth, fainteth not, neither is weary? there is no searching of his understanding.
text.NABRE: Do you not know? Have you not heard? The Lord is God from of old, creator of the ends of the earth. He does not faint or grow weary, and his knowledge is beyond scrutiny.
text.DRB: Why knowest thou not, or hast thou not heard? Our Lord is God everlasting, which hath created the ends of the earth: he shall not fail, nor labour, neither is there searching out of his wisdom.

text.NRSV: He gives power to the faint, and strengthens the powerless.
text.Rotherham: Giving to him that fainteth, strength, And to him that hath no vigour, he causeth, power to abound?
text.KJV: He giveth power to the faint; and to them that have no might he increaseth strength.
text.NABRE: He gives power to the faint, abundant strength to the weak.
text.DRB: Which giveth strength to the weary: and to them that are not, multiplieth force and strength.

text.NRSV: Even youths will faint and be weary, and the young will fall exhausted;
text.Rotherham: Youths both faint and grow weary, And, young warriors—they fall, they fall;
text.KJV: Even the youths shall faint and be weary, and the young men shall utterly fall:
text.NABRE: Though young men faint and grow weary, and youths stagger and fall,
text.DRB: Children shall faint, and labour, and youngmen shall fall by infirmity.

text.NRSV: but those who wait for the Lord shall renew their strength, they shall mount up with wings like eagles, they shall run and not be weary, they shall walk and not faint.
text.Rotherham: But, they who wait for Yahweh, shall renew their strength, They shall mount on strong pinion like eagles,—They shall run and not grow weary, They shall walk and not faint.
text.KJV: But they that wait upon the Lord shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles; they shall run, and not be weary; and they shall walk, and not faint.
text.NABRE: They that hope in the Lord will renew their strength, they will soar on eagles’ wings; They will run and not grow weary, walk and not grow faint.
text.DRB: But they that hope in our Lord shall change their strength, they shall take wings as eagles, they shall run and not labour, they shall walk and not faint.

text.NRSV: Listen to me in silence, O coastlands; let the peoples renew their strength; let them approach, then let them speak; let us draw near together for judgment.
text.Rotherham: Be silent [and hearken] unto me, O ye Coastlands, And let the Races of Men renew their strength,—Let them approach, then, let them speak, Together, for controversy, let us draw near:—
text.KJV: Keep silence before me, O islands; and let the people renew their strength: let them come near; then let them speak: let us come near together to judgment.
text.NABRE: Chapter 41 The Liberator of Israel Keep silence before me, O coastlands; let the nations renew their strength. Let them draw near and speak; let us come together for judgment.
text.DRB: Let the islands hold their peace before me, and the Gentiles change their strength: let them come near, & then speak, let us approach to judgement together.

text.NRSV: Who has roused a victor from the east, summoned him to his service? He delivers up nations to him, and tramples kings under foot; he makes them like dust with his sword, like driven stubble with his bow.
text.Rotherham: Who roused up one from the East, In righteousness, called him to his feet,—Set before him nations And over kings, caused him to rule, His sword, made [them] like dust, His bow like driven chaff:
text.KJV: Who raised up the righteous man from the east, called him to his foot, gave the nations before him, and made him rule over kings? he gave them as the dust to his sword, and as driven stubble to his bow.
text.NABRE: Who has stirred up from the East the champion of justice, and summoned him to be his attendant? To him he delivers nations and subdues kings; With his sword he reduces them to dust, with his bow, to driven straw.
text.DRB: Who hath raised the just from the East, hath called him that he should follow him? He shall give the Gentiles in his sight, & he shall obtain kings: he shall give them as it were dust to his sword, as stubble taken violently with the wind, to his bow.

text.NRSV: He pursues them and passes on safely, scarcely touching the path with his feet.
text.Rotherham: He pursued them passed along safely,—Upon the path of his own feet, entered he not?
text.KJV: He pursued them, and passed safely; even by the way that he had not gone with his feet.
text.NABRE: He pursues them, passing on without loss, by a path his feet scarcely touch.
text.DRB: He shall pursue them, he shall pass in peace, there shall no path appear after his feet.

text.NRSV: Who has performed and done this, calling the generations from the beginning? I, the Lord, am first, and will be with the last.
text.Rotherham: Who hath wrought and performed, Calling the generations, in advance? I, Yahweh, [who am] First, And with them who are last, I, am the Same!
text.KJV: Who hath wrought and done it, calling the generations from the beginning? I the Lord, the first, and with the last; I am he.
text.NABRE: Who has performed these deeds? Who has called forth the generations from the beginning? I, the Lord, am the first, and at the last I am he.
text.DRB: Who hath wrought and done these things, calling the generations from the beginning? I the Lord, the first and the last I am.

text.NRSV: The coastlands have seen and are afraid, the ends of the earth tremble; they have drawn near and are come.
text.Rotherham: Coastlands have seen, so they fear, The ends of the earth, are in dread,—They have drawn near, and have come:
text.KJV: The isles saw it, and feared; the ends of the earth were afraid, drew near, and came.
text.NABRE: The coastlands see, and fear; the ends of the earth tremble: they approach, they come on.
text.DRB: The islands have seen, and have been afraid, the ends of the earth have been astonied, they have approached, and come near.

text.NRSV: Each one helps the other, saying to one’s neighbor, “Take courage!”
text.Rotherham: Every man, to his neighbour, giveth help,—And to his brother, saith Take courage!
text.KJV: They helped every one his neighbour; and every one said to his brother, Be of good courage.
text.NABRE: Each one helps his neighbor, one says to the other, “Courage!”
text.DRB: Every one shall help his neighbour, and shall say to his brother: Be strong.

text.NRSV: The artisan encourages the goldsmith, and the one who smooths with the hammer encourages the one who strikes the anvil, saying of the soldering, “It is good”; and they fasten it with nails so that it cannot be moved.
text.Rotherham: So, the carver, hath encouraged, the goldsmith, he that maketh smooth with the hammer, him that smiteth the anvil,—Saying of the welding, It is, good, Then hath he fastened it with nails—it must not totter!
text.KJV: So the carpenter encouraged the goldsmith, and he that smootheth with the hammer him that smote the anvil, saying, It is ready for the sodering: and he fastened it with nails, that it should not be moved.
text.NABRE: The woodworker encourages the goldsmith, the one who beats with the hammer, him who strikes on the anvil, Saying of the soldering, “It is good!” then fastening it with nails so it will not totter.
text.DRB: The coppersmith striking with the hammer encouraged him that forged at that time, saying: It is good for soldering: and he strengthened it with nails, that it should not be moved.

text.NRSV: But you, Israel, my servant, Jacob, whom I have chosen, the offspring of Abraham, my friend;
text.Rotherham: But, thou, Israel, my Servant, Jacob whom I have chosen,—The seed of Abraham my loving one;
text.KJV: But thou, Israel, art my servant, Jacob whom I have chosen, the seed of Abraham my friend.
text.NABRE: But you, Israel, my servant, Jacob, whom I have chosen, offspring of Abraham my friend—
text.DRB: And thou Israel my servant, Jacob whom I have chosen, the seed of Abraham my friend:

text.NRSV: you whom I took from the ends of the earth, and called from its farthest corners, saying to you, “You are my servant, I have chosen you and not cast you off”;
text.Rotherham: Thou whom I have taken hold of from the ends of the earth, And from the extremities thereof, have called thee,—And said to thee, My Servant, thou! I have chosen thee and not cast thee off,
text.KJV: Thou whom I have taken from the ends of the earth, and called thee from the chief men thereof, and said unto thee, Thou art my servant; I have chosen thee, and not cast thee away.
text.NABRE: You whom I have taken from the ends of the earth and summoned from its far-off places, To whom I have said, You are my servant; I chose you, I have not rejected you—
text.DRB: in whom I have taken thee from the ends of the earth, and from the far parts thereof have called thee, and said to thee: Thou art my servant, I have chosen thee, & have not cast thee away.

text.NRSV: do not fear, for I am with you, do not be afraid, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my victorious right hand.
text.Rotherham: Do not fear, for with thee, I am! Look not around, for, I, am thy God,—I have emboldened thee, Yea I have helped thee, Yea I have upheld thee with my righteous right-hand.
text.KJV: Fear thou not; for I am with thee: be not dismayed; for I am thy God: I will strengthen thee; yea, I will help thee; yea, I will uphold thee with the right hand of my righteousness.
text.NABRE: Do not fear: I am with you; do not be anxious: I am your God. I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my victorious right hand.
text.DRB: Fear not, because I am with thee: decline not, because I am thy God: I have strengthened thee, and have holpen thee, and the right hand of my just one hath sustained thee.

text.NRSV: Yes, all who are incensed against you shall be ashamed and disgraced; those who strive against you shall be as nothing and shall perish.
text.Rotherham: Lo! they shall turn pale and be ashamed—All they who have been incensed against thee,—They shall become as nothing and perish—The men who have been thine accusers:
text.KJV: Behold, all they that were incensed against thee shall be ashamed and confounded: they shall be as nothing; and they that strive with thee shall perish.
text.NABRE: Yes, all shall be put to shame and disgrace who vent their anger against you; Those shall be as nothing and perish who offer resistance.
text.DRB: Behold all that fight against thee shall be confounded and ashamed, they shall be as if they were not, and the men shall perish that gainsay thee.

text.NRSV: You shall seek those who contend with you, but you shall not find them; those who war against you shall be as nothing at all.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt seek them, but shalt not find them—The men who have contended with thee, They shall become as nothing, and as a thing of nought—The men who have warred against thee;
text.KJV: Thou shalt seek them, and shalt not find them, even them that contended with thee: they that war against thee shall be as nothing, and as a thing of nought.
text.NABRE: You shall seek but not find those who strive against you; They shall be as nothing at all who do battle with you.
text.DRB: Thou shalt seek them, and shalt not find, the men thy rebels: they shall be as if they were not: and as consumption the men that war against thee.

text.NRSV: For I, the Lord your God, hold your right hand; it is I who say to you, “Do not fear, I will help you.”
text.Rotherham: For I, Yahweh thy God am firmly grasping thy right-hand,—Who am saying unto thee Do not fear! I, have become thy helper!
text.KJV: For I the Lord thy God will hold thy right hand, saying unto thee, Fear not; I will help thee.
text.NABRE: For I am the Lord, your God, who grasp your right hand; It is I who say to you, Do not fear, I will help you.
text.DRB: Because I am the Lord thy God, taking thy hand, and saying to thee: Fear not, I have holpen thee.

text.NRSV: Do not fear, you worm Jacob, you insect Israel! I will help you, says the Lord; your Redeemer is the Holy One of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Do not fear, Thou worm Jacob, Ye men of Israel,—I, have become thy helper, Declareth Yahweh, And thy redeemer The Holy One of Israel.
text.KJV: Fear not, thou worm Jacob, and ye men of Israel; I will help thee, saith the Lord, and thy redeemer, the Holy One of Israel.
text.NABRE: Do not fear, you worm Jacob, you maggot Israel; I will help you—oracle of the Lord; the Holy One of Israel is your redeemer.
text.DRB: Fear not thou worm of Jacob, ye that are dead of Israel: I have holpen thee, saith our Lord: and thy redeemer the holy one of Israel.

text.NRSV: Now, I will make of you a threshing sledge, sharp, new, and having teeth; you shall thresh the mountains and crush them, and you shall make the hills like chaff.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I have made of thee a new pointed threshing sledge, owning teeth,—Thou shalt thresh mountains and crush them, And, hills—like chaff, shalt thou make:
text.KJV: Behold, I will make thee a new sharp threshing instrument having teeth: thou shalt thresh the mountains, and beat them small, and shalt make the hills as chaff.
text.NABRE: I will make of you a threshing sledge, sharp, new, full of teeth, To thresh the mountains and crush them, to make the hills like chaff.
text.DRB: I have made thee as a new threshing wain, having teeth like a saw: thou shalt thresh the mountains, and break them in pieces: and shalt make the little hills as dust.

text.NRSV: You shall winnow them and the wind shall carry them away, and the tempest shall scatter them. Then you shall rejoice in the Lord; in the Holy One of Israel you shall glory.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt fan them and, a wind, shall carry them away, And, a whirlwind, scatter, them,—But, thou, shalt exult in Yahweh, And, in the Holy One of Israel, shalt thou boast thyself.
text.KJV: Thou shalt fan them, and the wind shall carry them away, and the whirlwind shall scatter them: and thou shalt rejoice in the Lord, and shalt glory in the Holy One of Israel.
text.NABRE: When you winnow them, the wind shall carry them off, the storm shall scatter them. But you shall rejoice in the Lord; in the Holy One of Israel you shall glory.
text.DRB: Thou shalt fan them, and the wind shall take them away, & the whirlwind shall disperse them: and thou shalt rejoice in the Lord, in the holy one of Israel thou shalt be joyful.

text.NRSV: When the poor and needy seek water, and there is none, and their tongue is parched with thirst, I the Lord will answer them, I the God of Israel will not forsake them.
text.Rotherham: As for the oppressed and the needy. Seeking water when there is none, Their tongue with thirst being parched I—Yahweh, will answer them, The God of Israel, I will not forsake them:
text.KJV: When the poor and needy seek water, and there is none, and their tongue faileth for thirst, I the Lord will hear them, I the God of Israel will not forsake them.
text.NABRE: The afflicted and the needy seek water in vain, their tongues are parched with thirst. I, the Lord, will answer them; I, the God of Israel, will not forsake them.
text.DRB: The needy and the poor seek for waters, and there are none: their tongue hath been dry with thirst. I the Lord will hear them, I the God of Israel will not forsake them.

text.NRSV: I will make rivers gush forth on bare heights, and springs in the midst of valleys; I will make the wilderness a pool of water, and the dry land springs of water.
text.Rotherham: I will open, On bare hills rivers, and In the midst of plains, fountains,—I will make The desert, a lake of water, and Parched land, springs of water;
text.KJV: I will open rivers in high places, and fountains in the midst of the valleys: I will make the wilderness a pool of water, and the dry land springs of water.
text.NABRE: I will open up rivers on the bare heights, and fountains in the broad valleys; I will turn the wilderness into a marshland, and the dry ground into springs of water.
text.DRB: I will open rivers in the high hills, and fountains in the midst of plain fields: I will make the desert into pools of waters, and the land not passable into rivers of waters.

text.NRSV: I will plant in the wilderness the cedar, the acacia, the myrtle, and the olive; I will set in the desert the cypress, the plane and the pine together,
text.Rotherham: I will set in the desert! Cedar, acacia, and myrtle, and oil-tree,—I will place, in the waste plain, Cypress, holm-oak, and sherbin-cedar, together
text.KJV: I will plant in the wilderness the cedar, the shittah tree, and the myrtle, and the oil tree; I will set in the desert the fir tree, and the pine, and the box tree together:
text.NABRE: In the wilderness I will plant the cedar, acacia, myrtle, and olive; In the wasteland I will set the cypress, together with the plane tree and the pine,
text.DRB: I will give into the wilderness the cedar, and the thorn, and the myrtle and the olive tree: I will set in the desert the fir tree, the elm, and the box tree together.

text.NRSV: so that all may see and know, all may consider and understand, that the hand of the Lord has done this, the Holy One of Israel has created it.
text.Rotherham: That men may see and observe and consider and understand at once, That, the hand of Yahweh, hath done this, That the Holy One of Israel hath created it.
text.KJV: That they may see, and know, and consider, and understand together, that the hand of the Lord hath done this, and the Holy One of Israel hath created it.
text.NABRE: That all may see and know, observe and understand, That the hand of the Lord has done this, the Holy One of Israel has created it.
text.DRB: That they may see, and know, and recount, and understand together, that the hand of the Lord hath done this, and the holy one of Israel hath created it.

text.NRSV: Set forth your case, says the Lord; bring your proofs, says the King of Jacob.
text.Rotherham: Bring near your contention, Saith Yahweh,—Advance your defences, Saith the King of Jacob:
text.KJV: Produce your cause, saith the Lord; bring forth your strong reasons, saith the King of Jacob.
text.NABRE: Present your case, says the Lord; bring forward your arguments, says the King of Jacob.
text.DRB: Make your judgement approach, saith the Lord: bring hither, if perhaps you have any thing, said the King of Jacob.

text.NRSV: Let them bring them, and tell us what is to happen. Tell us the former things, what they are, so that we may consider them and know their outcome; or declare to us the things to come.
text.Rotherham: Let them advance them, and tell us, What shall happen,—Things known in advance—what they were, tell ye, That we may lay them to our heart and mark the after-story of them, Or, things yet to come, let us hear:
text.KJV: Let them bring them forth, and shew us what shall happen: let them shew the former things, what they be, that we may consider them, and know the latter end of them; or declare us things for to come.
text.NABRE: Let them draw near and foretell to us what it is that shall happen! What are the things of long ago? Tell us, that we may reflect on them and know their outcome; Or declare to us the things to come,
text.DRB: Let them come, and tell us what things soever are to come: tell the former things what they have been: and we will set our heart, and shall know the later ends of them, and tell us the things that are to come.

text.NRSV: Tell us what is to come hereafter, that we may know that you are gods; do good, or do harm, that we may be afraid and terrified.
text.Rotherham: Tell ye the events which shall be here-after, That we may perceive that gods, ye are,—Surely ye must do something—good or bad, That we may be amazed and behold it, at once.
text.KJV: Shew the things that are to come hereafter, that we may know that ye are gods: yea, do good, or do evil, that we may be dismayed, and behold it together.
text.NABRE: tell what is to be in the future, that we may know that you are gods! Do something, good or evil, that will put us in awe and in fear.
text.DRB: Shew what things are to come hereafter, and we shall know that ye are gods. Do ye also good or evil, if you can: & let us speak & see together.

text.NRSV: You, indeed, are nothing and your work is nothing at all; whoever chooses you is an abomination.
text.Rotherham: Lo! ye, are, of nought, And your work is a puff of breath,—An abomination, he that chooseth you!
text.KJV: Behold, ye are of nothing, and your work of nought: an abomination is he that chooseth you.
text.NABRE: Why, you are nothing and your work is nought; to choose you is an abomination!
text.DRB: Behold, you are of nothing, and your work of that which is not: he is abomination that hath chosen you.

text.NRSV: I stirred up one from the north, and he has come, from the rising of the sun he was summoned by name. He shall trample on rulers as on mortar, as the potter treads clay.
text.Rotherham: I have roused up one from the North, and he hath come, From the rising of the sun, calleth he on my Name,—And he hath come, on deputies, as though they were mortar, And as a potter treadeth clay.
text.KJV: I have raised up one from the north, and he shall come: from the rising of the sun shall he call upon my name: and he shall come upon princes as upon morter, and as the potter treadeth clay.
text.NABRE: I have stirred up one from the north, and he comes; from the east I summon him by name; He shall trample the rulers down like mud, like a potter treading clay.
text.DRB: I have raised up from the North, and he shall come from the rising of the sun: he shall call upon my name, and shall bring the magistrates as mire, and as the plasterer treading clay.

text.NRSV: Who declared it from the beginning, so that we might know, and beforehand, so that we might say, “He is right”? There was no one who declared it, none who proclaimed, none who heard your words.
text.Rotherham: Who hath told in advance, that we might know, And beforetime, that we might say Right! Nay, there is none who can tell. Nay, there is none who can let us hear, Nay, there is none who can understand what ye utter.
text.KJV: Who hath declared from the beginning, that we may know? and beforetime, that we may say, He is righteous? yea, there is none that sheweth, yea, there is none that declareth, yea, there is none that heareth your words.
text.NABRE: Who announced this from the beginning, that we might know; beforehand, that we might say, “True”? Not one of you foretold it, not one spoke; not one heard you say,
text.DRB: Who hath shewed from the beginning, that we may know: and from the beginning that we may say: Art thou just? There is neither that sheweth, nor telleth before, nor heareth your words.

text.NRSV: I first have declared it to Zion, and I give to Jerusalem a herald of good tidings.
text.Rotherham: He who is First, [can say], To Zion, Lo! there they are! And to Jerusalem, A herald of good-tidings, do I give.
text.KJV: The first shall say to Zion, Behold, behold them: and I will give to Jerusalem one that bringeth good tidings.
text.NABRE: “The first news for Zion: here they come,” or, “I will give Jerusalem a herald of good news.”
text.DRB: The first shall say to Sion: Lo I am present, and to Jerusalem: I will give an evangelist.

text.NRSV: But when I look there is no one; among these there is no counselor who, when I ask, gives an answer.
text.Rotherham: So I look, And there is not, a Man! Even among these And there is none to advise,—That, when I ask them, can answer a word.
text.KJV: For I beheld, and there was no man; even among them, and there was no counsellor, that, when I asked of them, could answer a word.
text.NABRE: When I look, there is not one, not one of them to give counsel, to make an answer when I question them.
text.DRB: And I saw, and neither of these was there any that would consult, and being asked would answer a word.

text.NRSV: See, they are all a delusion; their works are nothing; their images are empty wind.
text.Rotherham: Lo! as to all of them, Vanity—nothingness, are their works, Wind and emptiness, their molten images!
text.KJV: Behold, they are all vanity; their works are nothing: their molten images are wind and confusion.
text.NABRE: Ah, all of them are nothing, their works are nought, their idols, empty wind!
text.DRB: Behold all are unjust, and their works vain: their idols are wind and vanity.

text.NRSV: Here is my servant, whom I uphold, my chosen, in whom my soul delights; I have put my spirit upon him; he will bring forth justice to the nations.
text.Rotherham: Lo! my Servant, I will uphold him, My chosen, well-pleased is my soul,—I have put my spirit upon him, Justice—to the nations, will he bring forth:
text.KJV: Behold my servant, whom I uphold; mine elect, in whom my soul delighteth; I have put my spirit upon him: he shall bring forth judgment to the Gentiles.
text.NABRE: Chapter 42 The Servant of the Lord Here is my servant whom I uphold, my chosen one with whom I am pleased. Upon him I have put my spirit; he shall bring forth justice to the nations.
text.DRB: Behold my servant, I will receive him: mine elect, my soul hath pleased it-self in him: I have given my spirit upon him, he shall bring forth judgement to the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: He will not cry or lift up his voice, or make it heard in the street;
text.Rotherham: He will not cry out nor will he speak loud,—Nor cause to be heard, in the street, his voice:
text.KJV: He shall not cry, nor lift up, nor cause his voice to be heard in the street.
text.NABRE: He will not cry out, nor shout, nor make his voice heard in the street.
text.DRB: He shall not cry, nor accept person, neither shall his voice be heard abroad.

text.NRSV: a bruised reed he will not break, and a dimly burning wick he will not quench; he will faithfully bring forth justice.
text.Rotherham: Cane that is crushed, will he not break, And wick that is fading, will he not quench,—Faithfully, will he bring forth justice:
text.KJV: A bruised reed shall he not break, and the smoking flax shall he not quench: he shall bring forth judgment unto truth.
text.NABRE: A bruised reed he will not break, and a dimly burning wick he will not quench. He will faithfully bring forth justice.
text.DRB: The bruised reed he shall not break, and smoking flax he shall not quench: he shall bring forth judgement in truth.

text.NRSV: He will not grow faint or be crushed until he has established justice in the earth; and the coastlands wait for his teaching.
text.Rotherham: He will not fade, nor will he be crushed, Until he establish, in the earth, justice, And for his instruction, Coastlands, wait.
text.KJV: He shall not fail nor be discouraged, till he have set judgment in the earth: and the isles shall wait for his law.
text.NABRE: He will not grow dim or be bruised until he establishes justice on the earth; the coastlands will wait for his teaching.
text.DRB: He shall not be sad, nor turbulent, till he set judgement in the earth: and the islands shall expect his law.

text.NRSV: Thus says God, the Lord, who created the heavens and stretched them out, who spread out the earth and what comes from it, who gives breath to the people upon it and spirit to those who walk in it:
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith GOD himself—Yahweh,—Creator of the heavens that stretched them forth, Out-spreader of earth, and the products thereof,—Giver of breath to the people thereon, And of spirit to them who walk therein,
text.KJV: Thus saith God the Lord, he that created the heavens, and stretched them out; he that spread forth the earth, and that which cometh out of it; he that giveth breath unto the people upon it, and spirit to them that walk therein:
text.NABRE: Thus says God, the Lord, who created the heavens and stretched them out, who spread out the earth and its produce, Who gives breath to its people and spirit to those who walk on it:
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord God that created the heavens, and stretched them out: that established the earth, and the things that spring thereof: that giveth breath to the people, that is upon it, and spirit to them that tread thereupon.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord, I have called you in righteousness, I have taken you by the hand and kept you; I have given you as a covenant to the people, a light to the nations,
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, have called thee in righteousness, And will firmly grasp thy hand,—And will keep thee And give thee—As the covenant of a people, As the light of nations:
text.KJV: I the Lord have called thee in righteousness, and will hold thine hand, and will keep thee, and give thee for a covenant of the people, for a light of the Gentiles;
text.NABRE: I, the Lord, have called you for justice, I have grasped you by the hand; I formed you, and set you as a covenant for the people, a light for the nations,
text.DRB: I the Lord have called thee in justice, and taken thy hand, & preserved thee. And I have given thee for a covenant of the people, for a light of the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: to open the eyes that are blind, to bring out the prisoners from the dungeon, from the prison those who sit in darkness.
text.Rotherham: To open eyes that are blind,—To bring forth Out of the dungeon the captive Out of the prison, the dwellers in darkness.
text.KJV: To open the blind eyes, to bring out the prisoners from the prison, and them that sit in darkness out of the prison house.
text.NABRE: To open the eyes of the blind, to bring out prisoners from confinement, and from the dungeon, those who live in darkness.
text.DRB: That thou mightest open the eyes of the blind, and bring forth the prisoner out of prison, and them that sit in darkness out of the prison house.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord, that is my name; my glory I give to no other, nor my praise to idols.
text.Rotherham: I, am Yahweh, that, is my Name, And, my glory, to another, will I not give, Nor my praise to images.
text.KJV: I am the Lord: that is my name: and my glory will I not give to another, neither my praise to graven images.
text.NABRE: I am the Lord, Lord is my name; my glory I give to no other, nor my praise to idols.
text.DRB: I the Lord, this is my name: I will not give my glory to another, and my praise to graven things.

text.NRSV: See, the former things have come to pass, and new things I now declare; before they spring forth, I tell you of them.
text.Rotherham: Things told in advance, lo! they have come to pass,—And new things, am I telling, Ere yet they spring forth, I let, you, hear them.
text.KJV: Behold, the former things are come to pass, and new things do I declare: before they spring forth I tell you of them.
text.NABRE: See, the earlier things have come to pass, new ones I now declare; Before they spring forth I announce them to you.
text.DRB: The things that were first, lo they are come: new things also I do shew: before they come forth, I will make you hear them.

text.NRSV: Sing to the Lord a new song, his praise from the end of the earth! Let the sea roar and all that fills it, the coastlands and their inhabitants.
text.Rotherham: Sing to Yahweh, a song that is new, His praise, from the end of the earth,—Ye that go down to the sea, and the fulness thereof, The Coastlands and ye who dwell therein.
text.KJV: Sing unto the Lord a new song, and his praise from the end of the earth, ye that go down to the sea, and all that is therein; the isles, and the inhabitants thereof.
text.NABRE: The Lord’s Purpose for Israel Sing to the Lord a new song, his praise from the ends of the earth: Let the sea and what fills it resound, the coastlands, and those who dwell in them.
text.DRB: Sing ye to the Lord a new song, his praise is from the ends of the earth: ye that go down to the sea, and you the fullness thereof: ye islands, and inhabitants of the same.

text.NRSV: Let the desert and its towns lift up their voice, the villages that Kedar inhabits; let the inhabitants of Sela sing for joy, let them shout from the tops of the mountains.
text.Rotherham: Let the wilderness shout, and the cities thereof, The villages wherein dwelleth Kedar,—Let the inhabitants of the crag, raise shouts of triumph, From the top of the mountains, let them cry aloud:
text.KJV: Let the wilderness and the cities thereof lift up their voice, the villages that Kedar doth inhabit: let the inhabitants of the rock sing, let them shout from the top of the mountains.
