text.Rotherham: They carry him about on the shoulder They bear the burden of him—and set him in his place that he may stand,—Out of his place, will he not move,—Though one even make outcry unto him, he will not answer, Out of one’s trouble, he will not save him.
text.KJV: They bear him upon the shoulder, they carry him, and set him in his place, and he standeth; from his place shall he not remove: yea, one shall cry unto him, yet can he not answer, nor save him out of his trouble.
text.NABRE: They lift it to their shoulders to carry; when they set it down, it stays, and does not move from the place. They cry out to it, but it cannot answer; it delivers no one from distress.
text.DRB: They bear him on their shoulders carrying, and setting him in his place, and he shall stand, and shall not move out of his place. Yea when they shall cry also unto him, he shall not hear: from tribulation he shall not save them.

text.NRSV: Remember this and consider, recall it to mind, you transgressors,
text.Rotherham: Remember ye this and shew yourselves men,—Bring it back, ye transgressors, to your minds;
text.KJV: Remember this, and shew yourselves men: bring it again to mind, O ye transgressors.
text.NABRE: Remember this and be firm, take it to heart, you rebels;
text.DRB: Remember this, and be confounded: return ye transgressors to the heart.

text.NRSV: remember the former things of old; for I am God, and there is no other; I am God, and there is no one like me,
text.Rotherham: Remember ye the things named in advance from age-past times,—For, I, am, The Mighty One, and there is none else, The Adorable and there is none like me!
text.KJV: Remember the former things of old: for I am God, and there is none else; I am God, and there is none like me,
text.NABRE: remember the former things, those long ago: I am God, there is no other; I am God, there is none like me.
text.DRB: Remember the former world, because I am God, and there is no God beside, neither is there the like to me.

text.NRSV: declaring the end from the beginning and from ancient times things not yet done, saying, “My purpose shall stand, and I will fulfill my intention,”
text.Rotherham: Declaring from the beginning, the latter end, And from olden time, that which had never been done,—Saying My purpose shall stand, and All my pleasure, will I perform;
text.KJV: Declaring the end from the beginning, and from ancient times the things that are not yet done, saying, My counsel shall stand, and I will do all my pleasure:
text.NABRE: At the beginning I declare the outcome; from of old, things not yet done. I say that my plan shall stand, I accomplish my every desire.
text.DRB: Which shew the last thing from the beginning, and from the beginning the things that as yet were not done, saying: My counsel shall stand, & all my will shall be done:

text.NRSV: calling a bird of prey from the east, the man for my purpose from a far country. I have spoken, and I will bring it to pass; I have planned, and I will do it.
text.Rotherham: Calling, From the East, a Bird of Prey, From a far country, the Man I intended,—Yea I have spoken I will also bring it to pass, I have planned, I will also do it.
text.KJV: Calling a ravenous bird from the east, the man that executeth my counsel from a far country: yea, I have spoken it, I will also bring it to pass; I have purposed it, I will also do it.
text.NABRE: I summon from the east a bird of prey, from a distant land, one to carry out my plan. Yes, I have spoken, I will accomplish it; I have planned it, and I will do it.
text.DRB: Which call a bird from the east, and from a far country, the man of mine own will, and I have spoken, and will bring it: I have created, and will do it.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, you stubborn of heart, you who are far from deliverance:
text.Rotherham: Hearken unto me, Ye valiant of heart,—Who are far away from righteousness:
text.KJV: Hearken unto me, ye stouthearted, that are far from righteousness:
text.NABRE: Listen to me, you fainthearted, far from the victory of justice:
text.DRB: Hear me ye hard hearted, which are far from justice.

text.NRSV: I bring near my deliverance, it is not far off, and my salvation will not tarry; I will put salvation in Zion, for Israel my glory.
text.Rotherham: I have brought near my righteousness. It shall not be far away, And my deliverance, shall not linger,—But I will give In Zion, deliverance, To Israel, my glory.
text.KJV: I bring near my righteousness; it shall not be far off, and my salvation shall not tarry: and I will place salvation in Zion for Israel my glory.
text.NABRE: I am bringing on that victory, it is not far off, my salvation shall not tarry; I will put salvation within Zion, give to Israel my glory.
text.DRB: I have made my justice near, it shall not be far off, and my salvation shall not tarry. I will give salvation in Sion, and my glory to Israel.

text.NRSV: Come down and sit in the dust, virgin daughter Babylon! Sit on the ground without a throne, daughter Chaldea! For you shall no more be called tender and delicate.
text.Rotherham: Down—and sit in the dust, O virgin Daughter of Babylon, Sit on the ground—throneless, Daughter of the Chaldeans; For thou shalt no more be called Tender and Dainty.
text.KJV: Come down, and sit in the dust, O virgin daughter of Babylon, sit on the ground: there is no throne, O daughter of the Chaldeans: for thou shalt no more be called tender and delicate.
text.NABRE: Chapter 47 The Fall of Babylon Come down, sit in the dust, virgin daughter Babylon; Sit on the ground, dethroned, daughter of the Chaldeans. No longer shall you be called dainty and delicate.
text.DRB: Come down, sit in the dust o Virgin daughter of Babylon, sit on the ground: there is no throne for the daughter of the Chaldees, because thou shalt no more be called nice and tender.

text.NRSV: Take the millstones and grind meal, remove your veil, strip off your robe, uncover your legs, pass through the rivers.
text.Rotherham: Take millstones, and grind meal,—Put back thy veil—tuck up thy train Bare the leg, wade through streams:
text.KJV: Take the millstones, and grind meal: uncover thy locks, make bare the leg, uncover the thigh, pass over the rivers.
text.NABRE: Take the millstone and grind flour, remove your veil; Strip off your skirt, bare your legs, cross through the streams.
text.DRB: Take a mill, and grind meal: make bare thy turpitude, discover the shoulder, uncover the thighs, pass the rivers.

text.NRSV: Your nakedness shall be uncovered, and your shame shall be seen. I will take vengeance, and I will spare no one.
text.Rotherham: Bared shall be thy shame, Yea seen thy reproach,—An avenging, will I take, And will accept no son of earth.
text.KJV: Thy nakedness shall be uncovered, yea, thy shame shall be seen: I will take vengeance, and I will not meet thee as a man.
text.NABRE: Your nakedness shall be uncovered, and your shame be seen; I will take vengeance, I will yield to no entreaty, says
text.DRB: Thine ignominy shall be discovered, and thy reproach shall be seen: I will take vengeance, and no man shall resist me.

text.NRSV: Our Redeemer—the Lord of hosts is his name—is the Holy One of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Our Redeemer, Yahweh of hosts, is his name! The Holy One of Israel.
text.KJV: As for our redeemer, the Lord of hosts is his name, the Holy One of Israel.
text.NABRE: our redeemer, Whose name is the Lord of hosts, the Holy One of Israel.
text.DRB: Our redeemer, the Lord of hosts is his name, the holy one of Israel.

text.NRSV: Sit in silence, and go into darkness, daughter Chaldea! For you shall no more be called the mistress of kingdoms.
text.Rotherham: Sit silent, and get into darkness, Daughter of the Chaldeans! For thou shalt no more be called Mistress of Kingdoms.
text.KJV: Sit thou silent, and get thee into darkness, O daughter of the Chaldeans: for thou shalt no more be called, The lady of kingdoms.
text.NABRE: Go into darkness and sit in silence, daughter of the Chaldeans, No longer shall you be called sovereign mistress of kingdoms.
text.DRB: Sit holding thy peace, and enter into darkness, o daughter of the Chaldees: because thou shalt no more be called the lady of kingdoms.

text.NRSV: I was angry with my people, I profaned my heritage; I gave them into your hand, you showed them no mercy; on the aged you made your yoke exceedingly heavy.
text.Rotherham: I had been provoked with my people, Had profaned mine inheritance, And given them into thy hand, …Thou shewedst them no compassion, Upon the elder, madest thou very heavy thy yoke.
text.KJV: I was wroth with my people, I have polluted mine inheritance, and given them into thine hand: thou didst shew them no mercy; upon the ancient hast thou very heavily laid thy yoke.
text.NABRE: Angry at my people, I profaned my heritage And gave them into your power; but you showed them no mercy; Upon the aged you laid a very heavy yoke.
text.DRB: I was angry against my people, I have contaminated mine inheritance, and have given them into thy hand: thou hast not shewed mercies to them: upon the ancient thou hast made thy yoke exceeding heavy.

text.NRSV: You said, “I shall be mistress forever,” so that you did not lay these things to heart or remember their end.
text.Rotherham: And thou saidst, Unto times age-abiding, shall I be Mistress,—Insomuch that thou laidst not these things to thy heart, Didst not keep in mind the issue thereof,
text.KJV: And thou saidst, I shall be a lady for ever: so that thou didst not lay these things to thy heart, neither didst remember the latter end of it.
text.NABRE: You said, “I shall remain always, a sovereign mistress forever!” You did not take these things to heart, but disregarded their outcome.
text.DRB: And thou hast said: I will be a lady for ever: thou hast not put these things upon thy heart, neither hast thou remembered thy later end.

text.NRSV: Now therefore hear this, you lover of pleasures, who sit securely, who say in your heart, “I am, and there is no one besides me; I shall not sit as a widow or know the loss of children” —
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore hear this, Thou Lady of pleasure Who dwelleth securely, Who saith in her heart,—I, [am], and there is no one besides, I shall not sit a widow, Nor know loss of children.
text.KJV: Therefore hear now this, thou that art given to pleasures, that dwellest carelessly, that sayest in thine heart, I am, and none else beside me; I shall not sit as a widow, neither shall I know the loss of children:
text.NABRE: Now hear this, voluptuous one, enthroned securely, Saying in your heart, “I, and no one else! I shall never be a widow, bereft of my children”—
text.DRB: And now hear these things thou that art delicate, and dwellest confidently, that sayest in thy heart: I am, and there is none else beside me: I shall not sit a widow, and I shall not know barrenness.

text.NRSV: both these things shall come upon you in a moment, in one day: the loss of children and widowhood shall come upon you in full measure, in spite of your many sorceries and the great power of your enchantments.
text.Rotherham: Yet shall there come to thee—both these, in a moment, in one day, Loss of children and widowhood,—To their full, have they come on thee, Spite of the mass of thine incantations, Spite of the great throng of thy spells.
text.KJV: But these two things shall come to thee in a moment in one day, the loss of children, and widowhood: they shall come upon thee in their perfection for the multitude of thy sorceries, and for the great abundance of thine enchantments.
text.NABRE: Both these things shall come to you suddenly, in a single day: Complete bereavement and widowhood shall come upon you Despite your many sorceries and the full power of your spells;
text.DRB: These two things shall come to thee suddenly in one day, barrenness and widowhood. All things are come upon thee, because of the multitude of thy sorceries, and for the vehement hardness of thine enchanters.

text.NRSV: You felt secure in your wickedness; you said, “No one sees me.” Your wisdom and your knowledge led you astray, and you said in your heart, “I am, and there is no one besides me.”
text.Rotherham: And so thou didst trust in thy wickedness, Thou saidst, no one, seeth me, Thy wisdom and knowledge, the same, seduced thee,—Therefore saidst thou in thy heart, I [am], and there is no one besides.
text.KJV: For thou hast trusted in thy wickedness: thou hast said, None seeth me. Thy wisdom and thy knowledge, it hath perverted thee; and thou hast said in thine heart, I am, and none else beside me.
text.NABRE: Secure in your wickedness, you said, “No one sees me.” Your wisdom and your knowledge led you astray, And you said in your heart, “I, and no one else!”
text.DRB: And thou hast confidence in thy malice, and hast said: There is none that seeth me. Thy wisdom, and thy knowledge, this hath deceived thee. And thou hast said in thy heart: I am, and beside me there is none other.

text.NRSV: But evil shall come upon you, which you cannot charm away; disaster shall fall upon you, which you will not be able to ward off; and ruin shall come on you suddenly, of which you know nothing.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall come on thee—Mischief, Thou shalt not know how to charm it away Yea there shall fall on thee, Ruin, Thou shalt not be able to appease it,—And there shall come on thee suddenly. Desolation. Thou shalt not know.
text.KJV: Therefore shall evil come upon thee; thou shalt not know from whence it riseth: and mischief shall fall upon thee; thou shalt not be able to put it off: and desolation shall come upon thee suddenly, which thou shalt not know.
text.NABRE: But upon you shall come an evil you will not be able to charm away; Upon you shall fall a disaster you cannot ward off. Upon you shall suddenly come a ruin you cannot imagine.
text.DRB: Evil shall come upon thee, and thou shalt not know the rising thereof: and calamity shall fall violently upon thee, which thou canst not expiate: misery shall come upon thee suddenly, which thou shalt not know.

text.NRSV: Stand fast in your enchantments and your many sorceries, with which you have labored from your youth; perhaps you may be able to succeed, perhaps you may inspire terror.
text.Rotherham: Take thy stand, I pray thee, With thy spells. And with the throng of thine incantations wherein thou hast wearied thyself from thy youth,—Peradventure thou mayest be able to profit Peradventure thou mayest strike me with terror.
text.KJV: Stand now with thine enchantments, and with the multitude of thy sorceries, wherein thou hast laboured from thy youth; if so be thou shalt be able to profit, if so be thou mayest prevail.
text.NABRE: Keep on with your spells and your many sorceries, at which you toiled from your youth. Perhaps you can prevail, perhaps you can strike terror!
text.DRB: Stand with thine enchanters, and with the multitude of thy sorceries, in which thou hast travailed from thy youth, if perhaps it may profit thee any thing, or if thou mayst become stronger.

text.NRSV: You are wearied with your many counsels; let those who study the heavens stand up and save you, those who gaze at the stars, and at each new moon predict what shall befall you.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast worn thyself out with the mass of thy consultations,—Let them take their stand I pray thee that they may save thee—The dividers of the heavens—The gazers at the stars, They who make known by new moons, Somewhat of the things which shall come upon thee.
text.KJV: Thou art wearied in the multitude of thy counsels. Let now the astrologers, the stargazers, the monthly prognosticators, stand up, and save thee from these things that shall come upon thee.
text.NABRE: You wore yourself out with so many consultations! Let the astrologers stand forth to save you, The stargazers who forecast at each new moon what would happen to you.
text.DRB: Thou hast failed in the multitude of thy counsels: let the astrologers of the heaven stand and save thee, which did contemplate the stars, and count the months, that by them they might tell things that shall come to thee.

text.NRSV: See, they are like stubble, the fire consumes them; they cannot deliver themselves from the power of the flame. No coal for warming oneself is this, no fire to sit before!
text.Rotherham: Lo! they have become as straw—a fire, hath burned them up, They shall not deliver their own soul from the grasp of the flame,—There is, no live coal to warm them, nor blaze to sit before.
text.KJV: Behold, they shall be as stubble; the fire shall burn them; they shall not deliver themselves from the power of the flame: there shall not be a coal to warm at, nor fire to sit before it.
text.NABRE: See, they are like stubble, fire consumes them; They cannot deliver themselves from the spreading flames. This is no warming ember, no fire to sit before!
text.DRB: Behold they are become as stubble, fire hath burnt them, they shall not deliver their soul from the hand of the flame: there are no coals, wherewith they may be warmed, nor fire, that they may sit thereat.

text.NRSV: Such to you are those with whom you have labored, who have trafficked with you from your youth; they all wander about in their own paths; there is no one to save you.
text.Rotherham: Such, have they become to thee, with whom thou hast wearied thyself,—Thy merchants—from thy youth, will every man stagger straight onwards—There is none to save thee.
text.KJV: Thus shall they be unto thee with whom thou hast laboured, even thy merchants, from thy youth: they shall wander every one to his quarter; none shall save thee.
text.NABRE: Thus do your wizards serve you with whom you have toiled from your youth; They wander their separate ways, with none to save you.
text.DRB: So are the things become unto thee, in whatsoever thou hast travailed: thy merchants from thy youth, every one hath erred in his own way, there is none that can save thee.

text.NRSV: Hear this, O house of Jacob, who are called by the name of Israel, and who came forth from the loins of Judah; who swear by the name of the Lord, and invoke the God of Israel, but not in truth or right.
text.Rotherham: Hear ye this—O house of Jacob, Ye who call yourselves by the name of Israel, Yea from the waters of Judah, came they forth,—Who swear by the name of Yahweh, And, by the God of Israel, call to remembrance—Not in truth, nor in righteousness;
text.KJV: Hear ye this, O house of Jacob, which are called by the name of Israel, and are come forth out of the waters of Judah, which swear by the name of the Lord, and make mention of the God of Israel, but not in truth, nor in righteousness.
text.NABRE: Chapter 48 Exhortations to the Exiles Hear this, house of Jacob called by the name Israel, sprung from the stock of Judah, You who swear by the name of the Lord and invoke the God of Israel without sincerity, without justice,
text.DRB: Hear ye these things o house of Jacob, which are called by the name of Israel, and are come out of the waters of Juda, which swear in the name of our Lord, and are mindful of the God of Israel not in truth, nor in justice.

text.NRSV: For they call themselves after the holy city, and lean on the God of Israel; the Lord of hosts is his name.
text.Rotherham: For of the holy city, do they call themselves, And on the God of Israel, do they stay themselves,—Yahweh of hosts, is his name.
text.KJV: For they call themselves of the holy city, and stay themselves upon the God of Israel; The Lord of hosts is his name.
text.NABRE: Though you are named after the holy city and rely on the God of Israel, whose name is the Lord of hosts.
text.DRB: For they are called of the holy city, and are established upon the God of Israel: the Lord of hosts is his name.

text.NRSV: The former things I declared long ago, they went out from my mouth and I made them known; then suddenly I did them and they came to pass.
text.Rotherham: Things in advance, of old, I declared, Yea out of mine own mouth, came they forth, that I might let them be known,—Suddenly, I wrought and they came to pass.
text.KJV: I have declared the former things from the beginning; and they went forth out of my mouth, and I shewed them; I did them suddenly, and they came to pass.
text.NABRE: Things of the past I declared long ago, they went forth from my mouth, I announced them; then suddenly I took action and they came to be.
text.DRB: The former things of old I have declared, & they proceeded out of my mouth, and I have made them to be heard: Suddenly I have wrought, and they came.

text.NRSV: Because I know that you are obstinate, and your neck is an iron sinew and your forehead brass,
text.Rotherham: Because I knew that—Obstinate, thou wast,—And, a sinew of iron, was thy neck, And thy brow, brazen,
text.KJV: Because I knew that thou art obstinate, and thy neck is an iron sinew, and thy brow brass;
text.NABRE: Because I know that you are stubborn and that your neck is an iron sinew and your forehead bronze,
text.DRB: For I knew that thou art stubborn, and thy neck is an iron sinew, and thy forehead of brass.

text.NRSV: I declared them to you from of old, before they came to pass I announced them to you, so that you would not say, “My idol did them, my carved image and my cast image commanded them.”
text.Rotherham: therefore told I thee—in time past, Ere yet it came, I let thee hear,—Lest thou shouldest say Mine idol wrought them, Yea, my carved image and my molten image, commanded them!
text.KJV: I have even from the beginning declared it to thee; before it came to pass I shewed it thee: lest thou shouldest say, Mine idol hath done them, and my graven image, and my molten image, hath commanded them.
text.NABRE: I declared them to you of old; before they took place I informed you, That you might not say, “My idol did them, my statue, my molten image commanded them.”
text.DRB: Iforetold thee of old: before they came I told thee, lest perhaps thou shouldst say: My idols have done these things, & my sculptiles & moltens have commanded these things.

text.NRSV: You have heard; now see all this; and will you not declare it? From this time forward I make you hear new things, hidden things that you have not known.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast heard—see it whole, And will, ye, not tell? I have let thee hear new things—from the present time, Even secrets which ye knew not.
text.KJV: Thou hast heard, see all this; and will not ye declare it? I have shewed thee new things from this time, even hidden things, and thou didst not know them.
text.NABRE: Now that you have heard, look at all this; must you not admit it? From now on I announce new things to you, hidden events you never knew.
text.DRB: See all the things which thou hast heard: but have you declared them? I have made thee know new things of old, and the things are kept which thou knowest not:

text.NRSV: They are created now, not long ago; before today you have never heard of them, so that you could not say, “I already knew them.”
text.Rotherham: Now, are they created, and not in time past, And, before to-day, thou hadst not heard of them,—Lest thou shouldest say, Lo! I knew them!
text.KJV: They are created now, and not from the beginning; even before the day when thou heardest them not; lest thou shouldest say, Behold, I knew them.
text.NABRE: Now, not from of old, they are created, before today you did not hear of them, so that you cannot claim, “I have known them.”
text.DRB: now they are created, and not of old: and before the day, and thou heardest them not, lest perhaps thou mightest say: Behold I knew them.

text.NRSV: You have never heard, you have never known, from of old your ear has not been opened. For I knew that you would deal very treacherously, and that from birth you were called a rebel.
text.Rotherham: Nay! thou hadst not heard, Nay! thou hadst not known, Nay! in time past, thine ear, was not opened,—For I knew that thou, wouldst be treacherous, Yea a transgressor from birth, hast thou been called.
text.KJV: Yea, thou heardest not; yea, thou knewest not; yea, from that time that thine ear was not opened: for I knew that thou wouldest deal very treacherously, and wast called a transgressor from the womb.
text.NABRE: You never heard, you never knew, they never reached your ears beforehand. Yes, I know you are utterly treacherous, a rebel you were named from the womb.
text.DRB: Thou hast neither heard, nor known, neither was thine ear opened of old. For I know that transgressing thou wilt transgress, and I have called thee a transgressor from the womb.

text.NRSV: For my name’s sake I defer my anger, for the sake of my praise I restrain it for you, so that I may not cut you off.
text.Rotherham: For the sake of mine own Name, will I defer mine anger, And for my praise, will I restrain myself towards thee,—So as not to cut thee off.
text.KJV: For my name’s sake will I defer mine anger, and for my praise will I refrain for thee, that I cut thee not off.
text.NABRE: For the sake of my name I restrain my anger, for the sake of my renown I hold it back from you, lest I destroy you.
text.DRB: For my name's sake I will make my fury far off: and for my praise I will bridle thee, that thou perish not.

text.NRSV: See, I have refined you, but not like silver; I have tested you in the furnace of adversity.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I have refined thee, but not as silver,—I have tested thee, in a smelting-pot of affliction.
text.KJV: Behold, I have refined thee, but not with silver; I have chosen thee in the furnace of affliction.
text.NABRE: See, I refined you, but not like silver; I tested you in the furnace of affliction.
text.DRB: Behold I have fined thee, but not as silver, I have chosen thee in the furnace of poverty.

text.NRSV: For my own sake, for my own sake, I do it, for why should my name be profaned? My glory I will not give to another.
text.Rotherham: For mine own sake—for mine own sake, will I effectually work For how should it be profaned? And, my glory—to another, will I not give.
text.KJV: For mine own sake, even for mine own sake, will I do it: for how should my name be polluted? and I will not give my glory unto another.
text.NABRE: For my sake, for my own sake, I do this; why should my name be profaned? My glory I will not give to another.
text.DRB: For myself, for myself will I do it, that I be not blasphemed: and I will not give my glory to another.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, O Jacob, and Israel, whom I called: I am he; I am the first, and I am the last.
text.Rotherham: Hearken unto me O Jacob, And Israel, my called one,—I, am, the Same I, the first, yea, I, the last:
text.KJV: Hearken unto me, O Jacob and Israel, my called; I am he; I am the first, I also am the last.
text.NABRE: Listen to me, Jacob, Israel, whom I called! I, it is I who am the first, and am I the last.
text.DRB: Hear me, o Jacob, and thou Israel whom I call: I the same, I the first, & I the last.

text.NRSV: My hand laid the foundation of the earth, and my right hand spread out the heavens; when I summon them, they stand at attention.
text.Rotherham: Surely, mine own hand, founded the earth, And, my right hand, stretched out the heavens,—While I was calling unto them, they stood forth, at once
text.KJV: Mine hand also hath laid the foundation of the earth, and my right hand hath spanned the heavens: when I call unto them, they stand up together.
text.NABRE: Yes, my hand laid the foundations of the earth; my right hand spread out the heavens. When I summon them, they stand forth at once.
text.DRB: My hand also hath founded the earth, and my right hand hath measured the heavens: I shall call them, & they shall stand together.

text.NRSV: Assemble, all of you, and hear! Who among them has declared these things? The Lord loves him; he shall perform his purpose on Babylon, and his arm shall be against the Chaldeans.
text.Rotherham: Assemble yourselves—all of you and hear, Who among them, hath told these things? he whom Yahweh loveth, will execute—His pleasure, on Babylon, And his stroke on the Chaldeans.
text.KJV: All ye, assemble yourselves, and hear; which among them hath declared these things? The Lord hath loved him: he will do his pleasure on Babylon, and his arm shall be on the Chaldeans.
text.NABRE: All of you assemble and listen: Who among you declared these things? The one the Lord loves shall do his will against Babylon and the offspring of Chaldea.
text.DRB: Assemble ye together all you, & hear: which of them hath shewed these things? The Lord hath loved him, he will do his will in Babylon, and his arm in the Chaldees.

text.NRSV: I, even I, have spoken and called him, I have brought him, and he will prosper in his way.
text.Rotherham: I—I, have spoken, Yea I have called him,—I have brought him in, and he shall make prosperous his way.
text.KJV: I, even I, have spoken; yea, I have called him: I have brought him, and he shall make his way prosperous.
text.NABRE: I myself have spoken, I have summoned him, I have brought him, and his way succeeds!
text.DRB: I, even I have spoken, and called him: I have brought him, and his way is directed.

text.NRSV: Draw near to me, hear this! From the beginning I have not spoken in secret, from the time it came to be I have been there. And now the Lord God has sent me and his spirit.
text.Rotherham: Draw ye near unto me—hear ye this, Not in advance in secret, have I spoken, From the very time it cometh into being, there, am I,—And, now, My Lord Yahweh hath sent me and his spirit.
text.KJV: Come ye near unto me, hear ye this; I have not spoken in secret from the beginning; from the time that it was, there am I: and now the Lord God, and his Spirit, hath sent me.
text.NABRE: Come near to me and hear this! From the beginning I did not speak in secret; At the time it happens, I am there: “Now the Lord God has sent me, and his spirit.”
text.DRB: Come ye to me, and hear this: I have not spoken in secret from the beginning, from the time before it was done I was there, and now the Lord God hath sent me, and his spirit.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: I am the Lord your God, who teaches you for your own good, who leads you in the way you should go.
text.Rotherham: Thus saith Yahweh—Thy Redeemer, The Holy One of Israel,—I—Yahweh, am thy God, Teaching thee to profit, Guiding thee in the way thou shouldest go.
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord, thy Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel; I am the Lord thy God which teacheth thee to profit, which leadeth thee by the way that thou shouldest go.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord, your redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: I am the Lord, your God, teaching you how to prevail, leading you on the way you should go.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord thy Redeemer, the holy one of Israel: I the Lord thy God that teach thee profitable things, that govern thee in the way that thou walkest.

text.NRSV: O that you had paid attention to my commandments! Then your prosperity would have been like a river, and your success like the waves of the sea;
text.Rotherham: Oh! that thou hadst hearkened to my commandments,—Then had been, like a river, thy prosperity, And, thy righteousness, like the waves of the sea:
text.KJV: O that thou hadst hearkened to my commandments! then had thy peace been as a river, and thy righteousness as the waves of the sea:
text.NABRE: If only you would attend to my commandments, your peace would be like a river, your vindication like the waves of the sea,
text.DRB: I would thou hadst attended to my commandments: thy peace had been as a flood, and thy justice as the waves of the sea.

text.NRSV: your offspring would have been like the sand, and your descendants like its grains; their name would never be cut off or destroyed from before me.
text.Rotherham: Then had been, like the sand, thy seed, And, the offspring of thy body, like the grains thereof,—Neither cut off nor destroyed had been his name from before me.
text.KJV: Thy seed also had been as the sand, and the offspring of thy bowels like the gravel thereof; his name should not have been cut off nor destroyed from before me.
text.NABRE: Your descendants like the sand, the offspring of your loins like its grains, Their name never cut off or blotted out from my presence.
text.DRB: And thy seed had been as the sand, and the stock of thy womb as the gravel stones thereof: his name had not perished, neither had it been destroyed from before my face.

text.NRSV: Go out from Babylon, flee from Chaldea, declare this with a shout of joy, proclaim it, send it forth to the end of the earth; say, “The Lord has redeemed his servant Jacob!”
text.Rotherham: Come ye forth out of Babylon, Flee from among the Chaldeans, With triumphant voice, tell ye—let this be heard, Let it go forth as far as the end of the earth,—Say ye—Yahweh hath redeemed his servant Jacob!
text.KJV: Go ye forth of Babylon, flee ye from the Chaldeans, with a voice of singing declare ye, tell this, utter it even to the end of the earth; say ye, The Lord hath redeemed his servant Jacob.
text.NABRE: Go forth from Babylon, flee from Chaldea! With shouts of joy declare this, announce it; Make it known to the ends of the earth, Say: “The Lord has redeemed his servant Jacob.
text.DRB: Come forth out of Babylon, fly from the Chaldees, shew it forth in the voice of exultation: make this to be heard, and speak it out even to the ends of the earth. Say: Our Lord hath redeemed his servant Jacob.

text.NRSV: They did not thirst when he led them through the deserts; he made water flow for them from the rock; he split open the rock and the water gushed out.
text.Rotherham: And they thirsted not, when, through dry places, he led them, Waters out of the rock, caused he to flow out to them,—Yea be cleft a rock and, there gushed out waters:
text.KJV: And they thirsted not when he led them through the deserts: he caused the waters to flow out of the rock for them: he clave the rock also, and the waters gushed out.
text.NABRE: They did not thirst when he led them through dry lands; Water from the rock he set flowing for them; he cleft the rock, and waters welled forth.”
text.DRB: They thirsted not in the desert, when he brought them forth: water out of the rock he brought forth to them, and he clove the rocks, and there flowed waters.

text.NRSV: “There is no peace,” says the Lord, “for the wicked.”
text.Rotherham: No well-being, saith Yahweh to the lawless,
text.KJV: There is no peace, saith the Lord, unto the wicked.
text.NABRE: There is no peace for the wicked, says the Lord.
text.DRB: There is no peace to the impious, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, O coastlands, pay attention, you peoples from far away! The Lord called me before I was born, while I was in my mother’s womb he named me.
text.Rotherham: Hearken, ye Coastlands, unto me, And give ear ye peoples afar off,—Yahweh, called me, from my birth, From my nativity, made he mention of my name;
text.KJV: Listen, O isles, unto me; and hearken, ye people, from far; The Lord hath called me from the womb; from the bowels of my mother hath he made mention of my name.
text.NABRE: B. Expiation of Sin, Spiritual Liberation of Israel Chapter 49 The Servant of the Lord Hear me, coastlands, listen, distant peoples. Before birth the Lord called me, from my mother’s womb he gave me my name.
text.DRB: Hear ye islands, and attend ye peoples from a far. The Lord hath called me from the womb, from my mother's belly he hath been mindful of my name.

text.NRSV: He made my mouth like a sharp sword, in the shadow of his hand he hid me; he made me a polished arrow, in his quiver he hid me away.
text.Rotherham: And he made my mouth, like a sharp sword, In the shadow of his hand, he concealed me,—And made of me a polished arrow, In his quiver, he hid me;
text.KJV: And he hath made my mouth like a sharp sword; in the shadow of his hand hath he hid me, and made me a polished shaft; in his quiver hath he hid me;
text.NABRE: He made my mouth like a sharp-edged sword, concealed me, shielded by his hand. He made me a sharpened arrow, in his quiver he hid me.
text.DRB: And he hath made my mouth as a sharp sword: in the shadow of his hand he hath protected me, & hath made me as a chosen arrow in his quiver he hath hidden me.

text.NRSV: And he said to me, “You are my servant, Israel, in whom I will be glorified.”
text.Rotherham: And said to me My Servant, thou art,—Israel, in whom I will get myself glory.
text.KJV: And said unto me, Thou art my servant, O Israel, in whom I will be glorified.
text.NABRE: He said to me, You are my servant, in you, Israel, I show my glory.
text.DRB: And he said to me: Thou art my servant Israel, because in thee will I glory.

text.NRSV: But I said, “I have labored in vain, I have spent my strength for nothing and vanity; yet surely my cause is with the Lord, and my reward with my God.”
text.Rotherham: But I, said, To no purpose, have I toiled, For waste and mist—my vigour, have I spent,—Surely, my vindication, is, with, Yahweh, And, my recompence, with my God.
text.KJV: Then I said, I have laboured in vain, I have spent my strength for nought, and in vain: yet surely my judgment is with the Lord, and my work with my God.
text.NABRE: Though I thought I had toiled in vain, for nothing and for naught spent my strength, Yet my right is with the Lord, my recompense is with my God.
text.DRB: And I said: I have laboured in vain, without cause, and in vain have I spent my strength: therefore my judgement is with the Lord, and my work with my God.

text.NRSV: And now the Lord says, who formed me in the womb to be his servant, to bring Jacob back to him, and that Israel might be gathered to him, for I am honored in the sight of the Lord, and my God has become my strength —
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, said Yahweh—Fashioning me from birth To be Servant to him, To restore Jacob unto him, And that, Israel, unto him, might be gathered And I be honourable in the eyes of Yahweh, And, my God, be proved to have been my strength,
text.KJV: And now, saith the Lord that formed me from the womb to be his servant, to bring Jacob again to him, Though Israel be not gathered, yet shall I be glorious in the eyes of the Lord, and my God shall be my strength.
text.NABRE: For now the Lord has spoken who formed me as his servant from the womb, That Jacob may be brought back to him and Israel gathered to him; I am honored in the sight of the Lord, and my God is now my strength!
text.DRB: And now saith the Lord, that formed me from the womb to be his servant, that I may reduce Jacob unto him, and Israel will not be gathered together: and I am glorified in the eyes of the Lord, and my God is made my strength.

text.NRSV: he says, “It is too light a thing that you should be my servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob and to restore the survivors of Israel; I will give you as a light to the nations, that my salvation may reach to the end of the earth.”
text.Rotherham: Yea he said—It is too small a thing, for being my Servant, That thou shouldest raise up the tribes of Jacob, And the preserved of Israel, shouldst restore,—So I will give thee to become a light of nations, That, my salvation, may reach as far as the end of the earth.
text.KJV: And he said, It is a light thing that thou shouldest be my servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob, and to restore the preserved of Israel: I will also give thee for a light to the Gentiles, that thou mayest be my salvation unto the end of the earth.
text.NABRE: It is too little, he says, for you to be my servant, to raise up the tribes of Jacob, and restore the survivors of Israel; I will make you a light to the nations, that my salvation may reach to the ends of the earth.
text.DRB: And he said: It is a small thing that thou shouldst be my servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob, and to convert the dregs of Israel. Behold, I have given thee to be the light of the Gentiles, that thou mayst be salvation even to the farthest part of the earth.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, the Redeemer of Israel and his Holy One, to one deeply despised, abhorred by the nations, the slave of rulers: “Kings shall see and stand up; princes, and they shall bow down, because of the Lord, who is faithful, the Holy One of Israel, who has chosen you.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh—the Redeemer of Israel, his Holy One To him that is despised of the soul, To the abhorred of the nation To the servant of rulers, Kings, shall see and arise, Princes, lo! they have bowed themselves down,—Because of Yahweh, who is faithful, The Holy One of Israel, lo! he hath chosen thee.
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord, the Redeemer of Israel, and his Holy One, to him whom man despiseth, to him whom the nation abhorreth, to a servant of rulers, Kings shall see and arise, princes also shall worship, because of the Lord that is faithful, and the Holy One of Israel, and he shall choose thee.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord, the redeemer, the Holy One of Israel, To the one despised, abhorred by the nations, the slave of rulers: When kings see you, they shall stand up, and princes shall bow down Because of the Lord who is faithful, the Holy One of Israel who has chosen you.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord the redeemer of Israel, the holy one thereof, to the contemptible soul, to the nation that is abhorred, to the servant of lords: kings shall see, & princes shall rise, & adore for our Lord's sake, because he is faithful, & for the holy one of Israel who hath chosen thee.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord: In a time of favor I have answered you, on a day of salvation I have helped you; I have kept you and given you as a covenant to the people, to establish the land, to apportion the desolate heritages;
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh—In a time of acceptance, have I answered thee, In a day of salvation, have I helped thee,—That I may preserve thee And give thee as the covenant of a people, To establish the land, To bring into possession the desolate heritages;
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord, In an acceptable time have I heard thee, and in a day of salvation have I helped thee: and I will preserve thee, and give thee for a covenant of the people, to establish the earth, to cause to inherit the desolate heritages;
text.NABRE: The Liberation and Restoration of Zion Thus says the Lord: In a time of favor I answer you, on the day of salvation I help you; I form you and set you as a covenant for the people, To restore the land and allot the devastated heritages,
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord: In time acceptable I have heard thee, and in the day of salvation I have holpen thee: and I have kept thee, and given thee to be a covenant of the people, that thou mightest raise up the land, and possess the inheritances dissipated.

text.NRSV: saying to the prisoners, “Come out,” to those who are in darkness, “Show yourselves.” They shall feed along the ways, on all the bare heights shall be their pasture;
text.Rotherham: Saying To them who are bound Go forth, To them who are in darkness, Shew yourselves,—By the roads, shall they graze, And on all bare places, shall be their pasture:
text.KJV: That thou mayest say to the prisoners, Go forth; to them that are in darkness, Shew yourselves. They shall feed in the ways, and their pastures shall be in all high places.
text.NABRE: To say to the prisoners: Come out! To those in darkness: Show yourselves! Along the roadways they shall find pasture, on every barren height shall their pastures be.
text.DRB: That thou mightest say to them, that are bound: Come forth, & to them that are in darkness: Be ye discovered. Upon the ways shall they feed, & their pastures shall be in all plains.

text.NRSV: they shall not hunger or thirst, neither scorching wind nor sun shall strike them down, for he who has pity on them will lead them, and by springs of water will guide them.
text.Rotherham: They shall neither hunger, nor thirst, Nor shall smite them the glowing sand, or the glaring sun,—For, he that hath compassion upon them, will lead them, And unto springs of water, will he conduct them.
text.KJV: They shall not hunger nor thirst; neither shall the heat nor sun smite them: for he that hath mercy on them shall lead them, even by the springs of water shall he guide them.
text.NABRE: They shall not hunger or thirst; nor shall scorching wind or sun strike them; For he who pities them leads them and guides them beside springs of water.
text.DRB: They shall not hunger nor thirst, & heat and sun shall not strike them: because he that is merciful to them shall govern them, and all the fountains of waters shall give them drink.

text.NRSV: And I will turn all my mountains into a road, and my highways shall be raised up.
text.Rotherham: Then will I make of all my mountains, a road, And my highways, shall be upraised.
text.KJV: And I will make all my mountains a way, and my highways shall be exalted.
text.NABRE: I will turn all my mountains into roadway, and make my highways level.
text.DRB: And I will make all my mountains to be a way, & my paths shall be exalted.

text.NRSV: Lo, these shall come from far away, and lo, these from the north and from the west, and these from the land of Syene.
text.Rotherham: Lo! these, from afar, shall come in,—And lo! these, from the North and from the West, And these, from the land of Sinim.
text.KJV: Behold, these shall come from far: and, lo, these from the north and from the west; and these from the land of Sinim.
text.NABRE: See, these shall come from afar: some from the north and the west, others from the land of Syene.
text.DRB: Behold these shall come from far, & behold they from the North and the sea, and these from the South country.

text.NRSV: Sing for joy, O heavens, and exult, O earth; break forth, O mountains, into singing! For the Lord has comforted his people, and will have compassion on his suffering ones.
text.Rotherham: Shout in triumph—O heavens! And exult—O earth! And break forth, ye mountains, into shouts of triumph,—For Yahweh hath comforted his people, And on his humbled ones, taketh he compassion.
text.KJV: Sing, O heavens; and be joyful, O earth; and break forth into singing, O mountains: for the Lord hath comforted his people, and will have mercy upon his afflicted.
text.NABRE: Sing out, heavens, and rejoice, earth, break forth into song, you mountains, For the Lord comforts his people and shows mercy to his afflicted.
text.DRB: Ye heavens praise, and earth rejoice, ye mountains give praise with jubilation, because our Lord hath comforted his people, and will have mercy on his poor ones.

text.NRSV: But Zion said, “The Lord has forsaken me, my Lord has forgotten me.”
text.Rotherham: But Zion had said. Yahweh hath forsaken me,—Even, My Lord, hath forgotten me!
text.KJV: But Zion said, The Lord hath forsaken me, and my Lord hath forgotten me.
text.NABRE: But Zion said, “The Lord has forsaken me; my Lord has forgotten me.”
text.DRB: And Sion said: Our Lord hath forsaken me, & our Lord hath forgotten me.

text.NRSV: Can a woman forget her nursing child, or show no compassion for the child of her womb? Even these may forget, yet I will not forget you.
text.Rotherham: Can, a woman, forget, her sucking child, Past taking compassion on the son of her womb? Even these, may forget, Yet will, I, not forget, thee:
text.KJV: Can a woman forget her sucking child, that she should not have compassion on the son of her womb? yea, they may forget, yet will I not forget thee.
text.NABRE: Can a mother forget her infant, be without tenderness for the child of her womb? Even should she forget, I will never forget you.
text.DRB: Why, can a woman forget her infant, that she will not have pity on the son of her womb? And if she should forget, yet will not I forget thee.

text.NRSV: See, I have inscribed you on the palms of my hands; your walls are continually before me.
text.Rotherham: Lo! on the palms of my hands, have I portrayed thee,—Thy walls are before me continually.
text.KJV: Behold, I have graven thee upon the palms of my hands; thy walls are continually before me.
text.NABRE: See, upon the palms of my hands I have engraved you; your walls are ever before me.
text.DRB: Behold, I have written thee in my hands, thy walls are before mine eyes always.

text.NRSV: Your builders outdo your destroyers, and those who laid you waste go away from you.
text.Rotherham: In haste are thy sons,—They who have been tearing thee down and laying thee waste, out of thee, let them go forth!
text.KJV: Thy children shall make haste; thy destroyers and they that made thee waste shall go forth of thee.
text.NABRE: Your children hasten— your levelers, your destroyers go forth from you;
text.DRB: Thy builders are come: they that destroy thee and dissipate thee shall go out of thee.

text.NRSV: Lift up your eyes all around and see; they all gather, they come to you. As I live, says the Lord, you shall put all of them on like an ornament, and like a bride you shall bind them on.
text.Rotherham: Lift up, round about, thine eyes and see, All those, have gathered themselves together—have come to thee! As I live, Declareth Yahweh,—Surely all those as an ornament, shalt thou put on, And bind them about thee for a girdle as a bride.
text.KJV: Lift up thine eyes round about, and behold: all these gather themselves together, and come to thee. As I live, saith the Lord, thou shalt surely clothe thee with them all, as with an ornament, and bind them on thee, as a bride doeth.
text.NABRE: Look about and see, they are all gathering and coming to you. As I live—oracle of the Lord — you shall don them as jewels, bedeck yourself like a bride.
text.DRB: Lift up thine eyes round about, and see, all these are gathered together, they are come to thee: I live, saith our Lord, for thou shalt be clothed with all these as with an ornament, and as a bride thou shalt put them about thee.

text.NRSV: Surely your waste and your desolate places and your devastated land—surely now you will be too crowded for your inhabitants, and those who swallowed you up will be far away.
text.Rotherham: Surely, as for thy wastes, and thy desolations, and thy land of ruins, Surely, now, shalt thou be too strait for thine inhabitants, And, far off, shall be they who have been swallowing thee up.
text.KJV: For thy waste and thy desolate places, and the land of thy destruction, shall even now be too narrow by reason of the inhabitants, and they that swallowed thee up shall be far away.
text.NABRE: Though you were waste and desolate, a land of ruins, Now you shall be too narrow for your inhabitants, while those who swallowed you up will be far away.
text.DRB: Because thy deserts, and thy solitary places, & the land of thy ruin, shall now be strait by reason of the inhabitants, & they shall be chased far away that swallowed thee up.

text.NRSV: The children born in the time of your bereavement will yet say in your hearing: “The place is too crowded for me; make room for me to settle.”
text.Rotherham: The children of whom thou wast bereaved shall yet say in thine ears,—Too strait for me, is the place Make room for me that I may settle down.
text.KJV: The children which thou shalt have, after thou hast lost the other, shall say again in thine ears, The place is too strait for me: give place to me that I may dwell.
text.NABRE: The children of whom you were bereft shall yet say in your hearing, “This place is too narrow for me, make room for me to live in.”
text.DRB: As yet shall the children of thy barrenness say in thine ears: The place is strait for me, make me space to dwell.

text.NRSV: Then you will say in your heart, “Who has borne me these? I was bereaved and barren, exiled and put away—so who has reared these? I was left all alone—where then have these come from?”
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou say in thy heart,—Who hath borne me, these, Seeing, I, have been bereaved and unfruitful, a captive and banished? These, therefore, who hath brought them up? Lo! I, was left, alone, These, where were they?
text.KJV: Then shalt thou say in thine heart, Who hath begotten me these, seeing I have lost my children, and am desolate, a captive, and removing to and fro? and who hath brought up these? Behold, I was left alone; these, where had they been?
text.NABRE: You shall ask yourself: “Who has borne me these, when I was bereft and barren? Exiled and repudiated, who has reared them? I was left all alone; where then do these come from?”
text.DRB: And thou shalt say in thy heart: Who hath begot me these? I am barren & not bearing, led into transmigration, & captive: & these who hath brought up? I destitute & alone: & these where were they?

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: See, I will lift up my hand to the nations, and raise my signal to the peoples; and they shall bring your sons in their bosom, and your daughters shall be carried on their shoulders.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh—Lo! I will lift up, unto nations my hand, Yea unto peoples, will I raise high my banner,—And they shall bring in thy sons in their bosom, And, thy daughters, on the shoulder shall be borne;
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord God, Behold, I will lift up mine hand to the Gentiles, and set up my standard to the people: and they shall bring thy sons in their arms, and thy daughters shall be carried upon their shoulders.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord God: See, I will lift up my hand to the nations, and to the peoples raise my signal; They shall bring your sons in their arms, your daughters shall be carried on their shoulders.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord God: behold I will lift up my hand to the Gentiles, & to the peoples I will exalt my sign. And they shall carry thy sons in their arms, and thy daughters upon their shoulders.

text.NRSV: Kings shall be your foster fathers, and their queens your nursing mothers. With their faces to the ground they shall bow down to you, and lick the dust of your feet. Then you will know that I am the Lord; those who wait for me shall not be put to shame.
text.Rotherham: And, kings, shall be, thy foster-fathers. And their queens, thy nursing mothers, With face to the ground, shall they bow down to thee, The dust of thy feet, shall they lick,—So shalt thou know that, I, am Yahweh, In that, they shall not be put to shame, Who were waiting for me.
text.KJV: And kings shall be thy nursing fathers, and their queens thy nursing mothers: they shall bow down to thee with their face toward the earth, and lick up the dust of thy feet; and thou shalt know that I am the Lord: for they shall not be ashamed that wait for me.
text.NABRE: Kings shall be your guardians, their princesses your nursemaids; Face to the ground, they shall bow down before you and lick the dust at your feet. Then you shall know that I am the Lord, none who hope in me shall be ashamed.
text.DRB: And kings shall be thy nursing fathers, & queens thy nurses: with countenance cast down toward the ground they shall adore thee, & they shall lick up the dust of thy feet. And thou shalt know that I am the Lord, upon whom they shall not be confounded that expect him.

text.NRSV: Can the prey be taken from the mighty, or the captives of a tyrant be rescued?
text.Rotherham: Shall prey, be taken from a mighty one? Or the captive of one in the right, be delivered?
text.KJV: Shall the prey be taken from the mighty, or the lawful captive delivered?
text.NABRE: Can plunder be taken from a warrior, or captives rescued from a tyrant?
text.DRB: Shall a prey be taken from the strong? Or can that which was caught of the mighty be saved?

text.NRSV: But thus says the Lord: Even the captives of the mighty shall be taken, and the prey of the tyrant be rescued; for I will contend with those who contend with you, and I will save your children.
text.Rotherham: Surely, thus, saith Yahweh—Even the captive of the mighty one, shall be taken away, And the prey of the tyrant, be delivered; And thine opposers, I, will oppose, And thy children, I, will save;
text.KJV: But thus saith the Lord, Even the captives of the mighty shall be taken away, and the prey of the terrible shall be delivered: for I will contend with him that contendeth with thee, and I will save thy children.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord: Yes, captives can be taken from a warrior, and plunder rescued from a tyrant; Those who oppose you I will oppose, and your sons I will save.
text.DRB: Because thus saith our Lord: Yea verily, even the captivity shall be taken away from the strong: and that which was taken by the mighty, shall be saved. But those that have judged thee, will I judge, and thy children I will save.

text.NRSV: I will make your oppressors eat their own flesh, and they shall be drunk with their own blood as with wine. Then all flesh shall know that I am the Lord your Savior, and your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.
text.Rotherham: Then will I feed thy tormentors with their own flesh, And as with new wine—with their own blood, shall they be drunk,—So shall all flesh, know, that, I—Yahweh, am thy Saviour, And, thy Redeemer, is the Mighty One of Jacob.
text.KJV: And I will feed them that oppress thee with their own flesh; and they shall be drunken with their own blood, as with sweet wine: and all flesh shall know that I the Lord am thy Saviour and thy Redeemer, the mighty One of Jacob.
text.NABRE: I will make your oppressors eat their own flesh, and they shall be drunk with their own blood as though with new wine. All flesh shall know that I, the Lord, am your savior, your redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.
text.DRB: And I will feed thine enemies with their own flesh: & as with new wine, so shall they be imbrued with their own blood: and all flesh shall know, that I am the Lord that save thee, & thy redeemer the mighty one of Jacob.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord: Where is your mother’s bill of divorce with which I put her away? Or which of my creditors is it to whom I have sold you? No, because of your sins you were sold, and for your transgressions your mother was put away.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh—Where then is the scroll of your mother’s divorce, whom I have put away? Or, which of my creditors, is it to whom I have sold you? Lo! for your iniquities, have ye been sold, And for your transgressions, hath your mother been put away.
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord, Where is the bill of your mother’s divorcement, whom I have put away? or which of my creditors is it to whom I have sold you? Behold, for your iniquities have ye sold yourselves, and for your transgressions is your mother put away.
text.NABRE: Chapter 50 Salvation Through the Lord’s Servant Thus says the Lord: Where is the bill of divorce with which I dismissed your mother? Or to which of my creditors have I sold you? It was for your sins you were sold, for your rebellions your mother was dismissed.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord: What is this bill of the divorce of our mother, wherewith I have dismissed her? Or who is my creditor, to whom I sold you? Lo you are sold for your wicked deeds, I have dismist your mother.

text.NRSV: Why was no one there when I came? Why did no one answer when I called? Is my hand shortened, that it cannot redeem? Or have I no power to deliver? By my rebuke I dry up the sea, I make the rivers a desert; their fish stink for lack of water, and die of thirst.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, When I came in, was there no one? When I called, was there none to answer? Is mine own hand, really shortened, that it cannot redeem? Or is there not, in me, strength to deliver? Lo! by my rebuke, I dry up the sea I make rivers a desert, Their fish Stinketh, for want of water, Yea dieth, for thirst:
text.KJV: Wherefore, when I came, was there no man? when I called, was there none to answer? Is my hand shortened at all, that it cannot redeem? or have I no power to deliver? behold, at my rebuke I dry up the sea, I make the rivers a wilderness: their fish stinketh, because there is no water, and dieth for thirst.
text.NABRE: Why was no one there when I came? Why did no one answer when I called? Is my hand too short to ransom? Have I not the strength to deliver? See, with my rebuke I dry up the sea, I turn rivers into wilderness; Their fish rot for lack of water, and die of thirst.
text.DRB: Because I came, and there was not a man: I called, and there was none that would hear. Why, is mine hand abridged and made a little one, that I cannot redeem? Or is there no strength in me to deliver? Behold, in my rebuke I will make the sea desert, I will turn the floods into dry land: the fishes shall rot without water, and shall die for thirst.

text.NRSV: I clothe the heavens with blackness, and make sackcloth their covering.
text.Rotherham: I clothe the heavens with gloom, And, of sackcloth, make I their covering.
text.KJV: I clothe the heavens with blackness, and I make sackcloth their covering.
text.NABRE: I clothe the heavens in black, and make sackcloth their covering.
text.DRB: I will clothe the heavens with darkness, and will make sackcloth their covering.

text.NRSV: The Lord God has given me the tongue of a teacher, that I may know how to sustain the weary with a word. Morning by morning he wakens—wakens my ear to listen as those who are taught.
text.Rotherham: My Lord Yahweh, hath given unto me the tongue of the instructed, That I should know how to succour the fainting, with discourse,—He kept wakening—morning by morning. He kept wakening mine ear, to hearken as do the instructed;
text.KJV: The Lord God hath given me the tongue of the learned, that I should know how to speak a word in season to him that is weary: he wakeneth morning by morning, he wakeneth mine ear to hear as the learned.
text.NABRE: The Lord God has given me a well-trained tongue, That I might know how to answer the weary a word that will waken them. Morning after morning he wakens my ear to hear as disciples do;
text.DRB: The Lord hath given me a learned tongue, that I may know to stay him up that is weary, with a word: he stirreth up in the morning, in the morning he stirreth up mine ear, that I may hear him as a master.

text.NRSV: The Lord God has opened my ear, and I was not rebellious, I did not turn backward.
text.Rotherham: My Lord, Yahweh, opened mine ear, And, I, was not rebellious,—Away, turned I not:
text.KJV: The Lord God hath opened mine ear, and I was not rebellious, neither turned away back.
text.NABRE: The Lord God opened my ear; I did not refuse, did not turn away.
text.DRB: The Lord God hath opened mine ear, & I do not gainsay? I am not gone backward.

text.NRSV: I gave my back to those who struck me, and my cheeks to those who pulled out the beard; I did not hide my face from insult and spitting.
text.Rotherham: My back, gave I to smiters, and My cheeks, to them who pulled out the beard,—My face, hid I not from insult and spitting.
text.KJV: I gave my back to the smiters, and my cheeks to them that plucked off the hair: I hid not my face from shame and spitting.
text.NABRE: I gave my back to those who beat me, my cheeks to those who tore out my beard; My face I did not hide from insults and spitting.
text.DRB: I have given my body to the strikers, & my cheeks to the pluckers: I have not turned away my face from the rebukers & spitters.

text.NRSV: The Lord God helps me; therefore I have not been disgraced; therefore I have set my face like flint, and I know that I shall not be put to shame;
text.Rotherham: Because, My Lord, Yahweh, would help me, Therefore was I not deterred by insult,—Therefore did I set my face like flint, And I knew that I should not be put to shame.
text.KJV: For the Lord God will help me; therefore shall I not be confounded: therefore have I set my face like a flint, and I know that I shall not be ashamed.
text.NABRE: The Lord God is my help, therefore I am not disgraced; Therefore I have set my face like flint, knowing that I shall not be put to shame.
text.DRB: The Lord God is mine helper, therefore am I not confounded: therefore have I set my face, as a most hard rock, and I know that I shall not be confounded.

text.NRSV: he who vindicates me is near. Who will contend with me? Let us stand up together. Who are my adversaries? Let them confront me.
text.Rotherham: At hand, is one who can justify me. Who will contend with me? let, us stand forth together,—Who can accuse me? let him draw near to me!
text.KJV: He is near that justifieth me; who will contend with me? let us stand together: who is mine adversary? let him come near to me.
text.NABRE: He who declares my innocence is near. Who will oppose me? Let us appear together. Who will dispute my right? Let them confront me.
text.DRB: He is near that justifieth me, who shall gainsay me? Let us stand together. Who is mine adversary? Let him come to me.

text.NRSV: It is the Lord God who helps me; who will declare me guilty? All of them will wear out like a garment; the moth will eat them up.
text.Rotherham: Lo! My Lord, Yahweh, will help me, Who is he that shall prove me lawless? Lo! they all, as a garment, shall fall to pieces, the moth shall consume them.
text.KJV: Behold, the Lord God will help me; who is he that shall condemn me? lo, they all shall wax old as a garment; the moth shall eat them up.
text.NABRE: See, the Lord God is my help; who will declare me guilty? See, they will all wear out like a garment, consumed by moths.
text.DRB: Behold the Lord God my helper: who is he that shall condemn me? Lo they shall all be destroyed as a garment, the moth shall eat them.

text.NRSV: Who among you fears the Lord and obeys the voice of his servant, who walks in darkness and has no light, yet trusts in the name of the Lord and relies upon his God?
text.Rotherham: Who among you, revereth Yahweh, Hearkening unto the voice of his Servant,—That hath walked in dark places And hath had no gleam of light? Let him trust in the name of Yahweh, And lean upon his God.
text.KJV: Who is among you that feareth the Lord, that obeyeth the voice of his servant, that walketh in darkness, and hath no light? let him trust in the name of the Lord, and stay upon his God.
text.NABRE: Who among you fears the Lord, heeds his servant’s voice? Whoever walk in darkness, without any light, Yet trust in the name of the Lord and rely upon their God!
text.DRB: Which of you feareth our Lord, heareth the voice of his servant, who hath walked in darkness, and hath no light? Let him hope in the name of our Lord, & lean upon his God.

text.NRSV: But all of you are kindlers of fire, lighters of firebrands. Walk in the flame of your fire, and among the brands that you have kindled! This is what you shall have from my hand: you shall lie down in torment.
text.Rotherham: Lo! all ye that kindle a fire, That gird yourselves with fiery darts,—Walk ye in the blaze of your own fire. And in the fiery darts ye have kindled, At my hand, hath this befallen you, In sorrow, shall ye lie down.
text.KJV: Behold, all ye that kindle a fire, that compass yourselves about with sparks: walk in the light of your fire, and in the sparks that ye have kindled. This shall ye have of mine hand; ye shall lie down in sorrow.
text.NABRE: All you who kindle flames and set flares alight, Walk by the light of your own fire and by the flares you have burnt! This is your fate from my hand: you shall lie down in a place of torment.
text.DRB: Lo all you do kindle a fire, are compassed with flames, walk in the light of your fire, and in the flames which you have kindled: of my hand is this done to you, you shall sleep in sorrows.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, you that pursue righteousness, you that seek the Lord. Look to the rock from which you were hewn, and to the quarry from which you were dug.
text.Rotherham: Hearken unto me, Ye that pursue righteousness, Ye that seek Yahweh,—Look well unto the rock whence ye were hewn, And unto the quarry whence ye were digged:
text.KJV: Hearken to me, ye that follow after righteousness, ye that seek the Lord: look unto the rock whence ye are hewn, and to the hole of the pit whence ye are digged.
text.NABRE: Chapter 51 Exhortation to Trust in the Lord Listen to me, you who pursue justice, who seek the Lord; Look to the rock from which you were hewn, to the quarry from which you were taken;
text.DRB: Hear me ye that follow that which is just, and that seek our Lord: attend to the rock whence you are hewn out, & to the cave of the lake from the which you are cut out.

text.NRSV: Look to Abraham your father and to Sarah who bore you; for he was but one when I called him, but I blessed him and made him many.
text.Rotherham: Look well unto Abraham your father, And unto Sarah who gave you birth,—For he was, alone, when called I him, And, I blessed him that I might make him, many.
text.KJV: Look unto Abraham your father, and unto Sarah that bare you: for I called him alone, and blessed him, and increased him.
text.NABRE: Look to Abraham, your father, and to Sarah, who gave you birth; Though he was but one when I called him, I blessed him and made him many.
text.DRB: Attend to Abraham your father, and to Sara that bare you: because I called him alone, and blessed him, & multiplied him.

text.NRSV: For the Lord will comfort Zion; he will comfort all her waste places, and will make her wilderness like Eden, her desert like the garden of the Lord; joy and gladness will be found in her, thanksgiving and the voice of song.
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh hath comforted Zion, He hath comforted all her waste places, And hath made her wilderness like Eden, And her waste plain like the garden of Yahweh,—Joy and gladness, shall be found in her, Thanksgiving, and the voice of melody.
text.KJV: For the Lord shall comfort Zion: he will comfort all her waste places; and he will make her wilderness like Eden, and her desert like the garden of the Lord; joy and gladness shall be found therein, thanksgiving, and the voice of melody.
text.NABRE: Yes, the Lord shall comfort Zion, shall comfort all her ruins; Her wilderness he shall make like Eden, her wasteland like the garden of the Lord; Joy and gladness shall be found in her, thanksgiving and the sound of song.
text.DRB: Our Lord therefore will comfort Sion, and will comfort all the ruins thereof: and he will make her desert as delicacies, and her wilderness as the garden of our Lord. Joy and gladness shall be found in it, giving of thanks, and voice of praise.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, my people, and give heed to me, my nation; for a teaching will go out from me, and my justice for a light to the peoples.
text.Rotherham: Attend unto me, O my people, And, O, my nation unto me give ear,—For, instruction, from me, shall go forth, And, my justice—for a light of peoples, will I establish:
text.KJV: Hearken unto me, my people; and give ear unto me, O my nation: for a law shall proceed from me, and I will make my judgment to rest for a light of the people.
text.NABRE: Be attentive to me, my people; my nation, give ear to me. For teaching shall go forth from me, and my judgment, as light to the peoples.
text.DRB: Attend unto me, o my people, and my tribe hear ye me: because a law shall proceed from me, and my judgement shall rest to be a light of the peoples.

text.NRSV: I will bring near my deliverance swiftly, my salvation has gone forth and my arms will rule the peoples; the coastlands wait for me, and for my arm they hope.
text.Rotherham: At hand is my righteousness. Gone forth hath my salvation, And, mine own arms, unto the peoples, shall give justice,—Upon me, Coastlands, shall wait, And for mine arm, shall they hope.
text.KJV: My righteousness is near; my salvation is gone forth, and mine arms shall judge the people; the isles shall wait upon me, and on mine arm shall they trust.
text.NABRE: I will make my victory come swiftly; my salvation shall go forth and my arm shall judge the nations; In me the coastlands shall hope, and my arm they shall await.
text.DRB: My just one is nigh at hand, my saviour is gone forth, and mine arms shall judge peoples: the islands shall expect me, & shall patiently wait for mine arm.

text.NRSV: Lift up your eyes to the heavens, and look at the earth beneath; for the heavens will vanish like smoke, the earth will wear out like a garment, and those who live on it will die like gnats; but my salvation will be forever, and my deliverance will never be ended.
text.Rotherham: Lift up—to the heavens—your eyes, And look around to the earth beneath Though, the heavens, like smoke, should have vanished And, the earth, like a garment, should fall to pieces, And, her inhabitants, in like manner, should die, Yet, my salvation, unto times age-abiding, shall continue, And, my righteousness, shall not be broken down.
text.KJV: Lift up your eyes to the heavens, and look upon the earth beneath: for the heavens shall vanish away like smoke, and the earth shall wax old like a garment, and they that dwell therein shall die in like manner: but my salvation shall be for ever, and my righteousness shall not be abolished.
text.NABRE: Raise your eyes to the heavens, look at the earth below; Though the heavens vanish like smoke, the earth wear out like a garment and its inhabitants die like flies, My salvation shall remain forever and my victory shall always be firm.
text.DRB: Lift up your eyes into heaven, and look down to the earth beneath: because the heavens shall melt as smoke, and the earth shall be worn away as a garment, and like to these things shall the inhabitants thereof perish: but my salvation shall be for ever, and my justice shall not fail.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, you who know righteousness, you people who have my teaching in your hearts; do not fear the reproach of others, and do not be dismayed when they revile you.
text.Rotherham: Hearken unto me ye who discern righteousness, The people in whose heart is my law,—Do not fear the reproach of frail men, Nor at their revilings, be dismayed;
text.KJV: Hearken unto me, ye that know righteousness, the people in whose heart is my law; fear ye not the reproach of men, neither be ye afraid of their revilings.
text.NABRE: Hear me, you who know justice, you people who have my teaching at heart: Do not fear the reproach of others; remain firm at their revilings.
text.DRB: Hear me ye that know that which is just, my people which have my law in their heart: fear ye not the reproach of men, and be not afraid of their blasphemies.

text.NRSV: For the moth will eat them up like a garment, and the worm will eat them like wool; but my deliverance will be forever, and my salvation to all generations.
text.Rotherham: For like a garment, shall they be eaten of the moth, And like wool, shall they be eaten of the larva; But, my righteousness, unto times age-abiding shall continue, And, my salvation, unto the remotest generation.
text.KJV: For the moth shall eat them up like a garment, and the worm shall eat them like wool: but my righteousness shall be for ever, and my salvation from generation to generation.
text.NABRE: They shall be like a garment eaten by moths, like wool consumed by grubs; But my victory shall remain forever, my salvation, for all generations.
text.DRB: For as a garment so shall the worm eat them: and as wool, so shall the moth devour them, but my salvation shall be for ever, and my justice unto generations of generations.

text.NRSV: Awake, awake, put on strength, O arm of the Lord! Awake, as in days of old, the generations of long ago! Was it not you who cut Rahab in pieces, who pierced the dragon?
text.Rotherham: Awake, awake put on strength, O arm of Yahweh, Awake, As in days of old, The generations of bygone ages: Art not thou that which—Hewed down Rahab, Piercing the Crocodile?
text.KJV: Awake, awake, put on strength, O arm of the Lord; awake, as in the ancient days, in the generations of old. Art thou not it that hath cut Rahab, and wounded the dragon?
text.NABRE: Awake, awake, put on strength, arm of the Lord! Awake as in the days of old, in ages long ago! Was it not you who crushed Rahab, you who pierced the dragon?
text.DRB: Arise, arise, put on strength o arm of our Lord: arise as in the old days, in the generations of worlds. Hast not thou strucken the proud, & wounded the dragon?

text.NRSV: Was it not you who dried up the sea, the waters of the great deep; who made the depths of the sea a way for the redeemed to cross over?
text.Rotherham: Art not thou that which dried up—The Sea, The waters of the mighty Deep,—That which made of the abysses of the sea a road for the passing over of the redeemed?
text.KJV: Art thou not it which hath dried the sea, the waters of the great deep; that hath made the depths of the sea a way for the ransomed to pass over?
text.NABRE: Was it not you who dried up the sea, the waters of the great deep, You who made the depths of the sea into a way for the redeemed to pass through?
text.DRB: Hast not thou dried the sea, the water of the vehement, which madest the depth of the sea a way, that the delivered might pass.

text.NRSV: So the ransomed of the Lord shall return, and come to Zion with singing; everlasting joy shall be upon their heads; they shall obtain joy and gladness, and sorrow and sighing shall flee away.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, the ransomed of Yahweh, shall again come unto Zion, with shouts of triumph, With gladness age-abiding on their head,—Joy, and gladness, shall overtake [them]. Sorrow and sighing have fled away.
text.KJV: Therefore the redeemed of the Lord shall return, and come with singing unto Zion; and everlasting joy shall be upon their head: they shall obtain gladness and joy; and sorrow and mourning shall flee away.
text.NABRE: Those whom the Lord has ransomed will return and enter Zion singing, crowned with everlasting joy; They will meet with joy and gladness, sorrow and mourning will flee.
text.DRB: And now they that are redeemed of our Lord shall return, and shall come into Sion praying, and joy everlasting upon their heads, they shall possess joy and gladness, sorrow and mourning shall fly away.

text.NRSV: I, I am he who comforts you; why then are you afraid of a mere mortal who must die, a human being who fades like grass?
text.Rotherham: I—I, am he that comforteth you,—Who art, thou, that thou hast feared Frail man that dieth, and A son of the earthborn, who, as grass, shall be delivered up?
text.KJV: I, even I, am he that comforteth you: who art thou, that thou shouldest be afraid of a man that shall die, and of the son of man which shall be made as grass;
text.NABRE: I, it is I who comfort you. Can you then fear mortals who die, human beings who are just grass,
text.DRB: I, even I myself will comfort you: who art thou that thou shouldest be afraid of a mortal man, and of the son of man, which as grass so shall wither?

text.NRSV: You have forgotten the Lord, your Maker, who stretched out the heavens and laid the foundations of the earth. You fear continually all day long because of the fury of the oppressor, who is bent on destruction. But where is the fury of the oppressor?
text.Rotherham: That thou hast forgotten Yahweh thy maker, Who stretched out the heavens And founded the earth? That thou hast dreaded continually, all the day, by reason of the fury of the oppressor, in that he was ready to destroy? Where then is the fury of the oppressor?
text.KJV: And forgettest the Lord thy maker, that hath stretched forth the heavens, and laid the foundations of the earth; and hast feared continually every day because of the fury of the oppressor, as if he were ready to destroy? and where is the fury of the oppressor?
text.NABRE: And forget the Lord, your maker, who stretched out the heavens and laid the foundations of earth? All the day you are in constant dread of the fury of the oppressor When he prepares himself to destroy; but where is the oppressor’s fury?
